Arjuna said :
Kṛṣņa, you extol Sāṅkhyayoga (the Yoga of Knowledge) and then the Yoga of Action. Pray, tell me which of the two is decidedly conducive to my good.

column3:
śrībhagavānuvāca
sannyāsaḥ(kh) karmayogaśca, niḥśreyasakarāvubhau।
tayostu    karmasannyāsāt,  karmayogo    viśiṣyate॥5.2॥
column4: Śrī Bhagavān said :The Yoga of Knowledge and the Yoga of Action both lead to supreme Bliss. Of the two, however, the Yoga of Action, being easier of practice, is superior to the Yoga of Knowledge.
column5:
Śrī Bhagavān said : 
The Yoga of Knowledge and the Yoga of Action both lead to supreme bliss. Of the two, however, the Yoga of Action, being easier of practice, is superior to the Yoga of Knowledge.

column3:
jñeyaḥ(s) sa nityasannyāsī, yo na dveṣṭi na kāṅkṣati। 
nirdvandvo hi mahābāho, sukhaṃ(m) bandhātpramucyate॥5.3॥
column4: The Karmayogī who neither hates nor desires should ever be considered as an ever renunciant. For, Arjuna, he who is free from the pairs of opposites is easily liberated from bondage.
column5: The Karmayogī who neither hates nor desires should ever be considered as an ever renunciant. For, Arjuna, he who is free from the pairs of opposites is easily liberated from bondage.

column3:
sāṅkhyayogau pṛthagbālāḥ(ph), pravadanti na paṇḍitāḥ। 
ekamapyāsthitaḥ(s) samyag, ubhayorvindate phalam॥5.4॥
column4: It is the ignorant, not the wise, who say that Sāṅkhyayoga and Karmayoga lead to divergent results. For, one who is firmly established in either, gets the fruit of both which is the same, viz., God-realization.
column5: It is the ignorant, not the wise, who say that Sāṅkhyayoga and Karmayoga lead to divergent results. For, one who is firmly established in either, gets the fruit of both which is the same, viz., God-realization.

column3:
yatsāṅkhyaiḥ(ph) prāpyate sthānaṃ(n), tadyogairapi gamyate।
ekaṃ(m) sāṅkhyaṃ(ñ) ca yogaṃ(ñ) ca, yaḥ(ph) paśyati sa paśyati॥5.5॥
column4: The (supreme) state which is reached by the Sāṅkhyayogī is attained also by the Karmayogī. Therefore, he alone who sees Sāṅkhyayoga and Karmayoga as identical so far as their result goes, sees truly.
column5: The (supreme) state which is reached by the Sāṅkhyayogī is attained also by the Karmayogī. Therefore, he alone who sees Sāṅkhyayoga and Karmayoga as identical so far as their result goes, sees truly.

column3:
sannyāsastu   mahābāho,   duḥkhamāptumayogataḥ।
yogayukto       munirbrahma,      nacireṇādhigacchati॥5.6॥
column4: Without Karmayoga, however, Sāṅkhyayoga i.e., renunciation of doership in relation to all activities of the mind, senses and body is difficult to accomplish; whereas the Karmayogī, who keeps his mind fixed on God, reaches Brahma in no time, Arjuna.
column5: Without Karmayoga, however, Sāṅkhyayoga i.e., renunciation of doership in relation to all activities of the mind, senses and body is difficult to accomplish; whereas the Karmayogī, who keeps his mind fixed on God, reaches Brahma in no time, Arjuna.

column3:
yogayukto    viśuddhātmā,    vijitātmā   jitendriyaḥ।
sarvabhūtātmabhūtātmā,    kurvannapi   na   lipyate॥5.7॥
column4: The Karmayogī, who has fully conquered his mind and mastered his senses, whose heart is pure, and who has identified himself with the Self of all beings (viz., God), remains untainted, even though performing action.
column5: The Karmayogī, who has fully conquered his mind and mastered his senses, whose heart is pure, and who has identified himself with the Self of all beings (viz., God), remains untainted, even though performing action.

column3:
naiva    kiñcitkaromīti,  yukto   manyeta    tattvavit।
paśyañśṛṇvanspṛśañjighran, naśnangacchansvapañśvasan॥5.8॥
column4: However, the Sāṅkhyayogī, who knows the reality of things, must believe that he does nothing, even though seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating or drinking, walking, sleeping, breathing,
column5: However, the Sāṅkhyayogī, who knows the reality of things, must believe that he does nothing, even though seeing, hearing, touching, smelling, eating or drinking, walking, sleeping, breathing,

column3:
pralapanvisṛjangṛhṇan,      nunmiṣannimiṣannapi।
indriyāṇīndriyārtheṣu,   vartanta   iti  dhārayan॥5.9॥
column4: speaking, answering the calls of nature, grasping, and opening or closing the eyes, holding that it is the senses alone that are moving among their objects.
column5: speaking, answering the calls of nature, grasping, and opening or closing the eyes, holding that it is the senses alone that are moving among their objects.

column3:
brahmaṇyādhāya karmāṇi, saṅgaṃ(n) tyaktvā karoti yaḥ।
lipyate   na   sa   pāpena,   padmapatramivāmbhasā॥5.10॥
column4: He who acts offering all actions to God, and shaking off attachment, remains untouched by sin, as the lotus leaf by water.
column5: He who acts offering all actions to God, and shaking off attachment, remains untouched by sin, as the lotus leaf by water.

column3:
kāyena   manasā   buddhyā,  kevalairindriyairapi।
yoginaḥ(kh) karma kurvanti, saṅgaṃ(n) tyaktvātmaśuddhaye॥5.11॥
column4: The Karmayogīs perform action only with their senses, mind, intellect and body as well, without the feeling of mine in respect of them and shaking off attachment, simply for the sake of self-purification.
column5: The Karmayogīs perform action only with their senses, mind, intellect and body as well, without the feeling of mine in respect of them and shaking off attachment, simply for the sake of self-purification.

column3:
yuktaḥ(kh) karmaphalaṃ(n) tyaktvā, śāntimāpnoti naiṣṭhikīm।
ayuktaḥ(kh)  kāmakāreṇa,  phale  sakto  nibadhyate॥5.12॥
column4: Offering the fruit of actions to God, the Karmayogī attains everlasting peace in the form of God-realization; whereas, he who works with a selfish motive, being attached to the fruit of actions through desire, gets tied down.
column5: Offering the fruit of actions to God, the Karmayogī attains everlasting peace in the form of God-realization; whereas, he who works with a selfish motive, being attached to the fruit of actions through desire, gets tied down.

column3:
sarvakarmāṇi manasā, sannyasyāste sukhaṃ(v̐ )vaśī।
navadvāre   pure  dehī,  naiva  kurvanna  kārayan॥5.13॥
column4: The self-controlled Sāṅkhyayogī, doing nothing himself and getting nothing done by others, rests happily in God-the embodiment of Truth, Knowledge and Bliss, mentally relegating all actions to the mansion of nine gates (the body with nine openings).
column5: The self-controlled Sāṅkhyayogī, doing nothing himself and getting nothing done by others, rests happily in God-the embodiment of Truth, Knowledge and Bliss, mentally relegating all actions to the mansion of nine gates (the body with nine openings).

column3:
na  kartṛtvaṃ(n)  na  karmāṇi,   lokasya  sṛjati  prabhuḥ।
na  karmaphalasaṃyogaṃ(m),  svabhāvastu  pravartate॥5.14॥
column4: God determines neither the doership nor the doings of men, nor even their contact with the fruit of actions; but it is Nature alone that does all this.
column5: God determines neither the doership nor the doings of men, nor even their contact with the fruit of actions; but it is Nature alone that does all this.

column3:
nādatte kasyacitpāpaṃ(n), na caiva sukṛtaṃ (v̐) vibhuḥ।
ajñānenāvṛtaṃ(ñ) jñānaṃ(n), tena muhyanti jantavaḥ॥5.15॥
column4: The omnipresent God does not partake the virtue or sin of anyone. Knowledge is enveloped by ignorance; hence it is that beings are constantly falling a prey to delusion.
column5: The omnipresent God does not partake the virtue or sin of anyone. Knowledge is enveloped by ignorance; hence it is that beings are constantly falling a prey to delusion.

column3:
jñānena  tu  tadajñānaṃ(y̐),  yeṣāṃ(n)  nāśitamātmanaḥ।
teṣāmādityavajjñānaṃ(m),   prakāśayati   tatparam॥5.16॥
column4: In the case, however, of those whose said ignorance has been destroyed by true knowledge of God, that wisdom shining like the sun reveals the Supreme.
column5: In the case, however, of those whose said ignorance has been destroyed by true knowledge of God, that wisdom shining like the sun reveals the Supreme.

column3:
tadbuddhayastadātmānaḥ(s),   tanniṣṭhāstatparāyaṇāḥ।
gacchantyapunarāvṛttiṃ(ñ),    jñānanirdhūtakalmaṣāḥ॥5.17॥
column4: Those whose mind and intellect are wholly merged in Him, who remain constantly established in identity with Him, and have finally become one with Him, their sins being wiped out by wisdom, reach the supreme goal whence there is no return.
column5: Those whose mind and intellect are wholly merged in Him, who remain constantly established in identity with Him, and have finally become one with Him, their sins being wiped out by wisdom, reach the supreme goal whence there is no return.

column3:
vidyāvinayasaṃpanne,    brāhmaṇe   gavi  hastini।
śuni caiva(ś) śvapāke ca, paṇḍitāḥ(s) samadarśinaḥ॥5.18॥
column4: The wise look with equanimity on all whether it be a Brāhmaņa endowed with learning and humility, a cow, an elephant, a dog and a pariah, too.
column5: The wise look with equanimity on all whether it be a Brāhmaņa endowed with learning and humility, a cow, an elephant, a dog and a pariah, too.

column3:
ihaiva tairjitaḥ(s) sargo, yeṣāṃ(m) sāmye sthitaṃ(m) manaḥ।
nirdoṣaṃ(m) hi samaṃ(m) brahma, tasmād brahmaṇi te sthitāḥ॥5.19॥
column4: Even here is the mortal plane conquered by those whose mind is established in equanimity; since the Absolute is untouched by evil and is the same to all, hence they are established in Paramātmā.
column5: Even here is the mortal plane conquered by those whose mind is established in equanimity; since the Absolute is untouched by evil and is the same to all, hence they are established in Paramātmā.

column3:
na prahṛṣyetpriyaṃ(m) prāpya, nodvijetprāpya cāpriyam।
sthirabuddhirasaṃmūḍho, brahmavid brahmaṇi sthitaḥ॥5.20॥
column4: He who, with firm intellect and free from doubt, rejoices not on obtaining what is pleasant and does not feel perturbed on meeting with the unpleasant, that knower of Brahma lives eternally in identity with Brahma.
column5: He who, with firm intellect and free from doubt, rejoices not on obtaining what is pleasant and does not feel perturbed on meeting with the unpleasant, that knower of Brahma lives eternally in identity with Brahma.

column3:
bāhyasparśeṣvasaktātmā,  vindatyātmani  yatsukham।  
sa  brahmayogayuktātmā,   sukhamakṣayamaśnute॥5.21॥
column4: He whose mind remains unattached to senseobjects, derives through meditation, the Sāttvika joy which dwells in the mind; then that Yogī, having completely identified himself through meditation with Brahma, enjoys eternal Bliss.
column5: He whose mind remains unattached to senseobjects, derives through meditation, the Sāttvika joy which dwells in the mind; then that Yogī, having completely identified himself through meditation with Brahma, enjoys eternal Bliss.

column3:
ye  hi  saṃsparśajā   bhogā,   duḥkhayonaya  eva  te।
ādyantavantaḥ(kh) kaunteya, na teṣu ramate budhaḥ॥5.22॥
column4: The pleasures which are born of sense-contacts, are verily a source of suffering only (though appearing as enjoyable to worldly-minded people). They have a beginning and an end (they come and go); Arjuna, it is for this reason that a wise man does not indulge in them.
column5: The pleasures which are born of sense-contacts, are verily a source of suffering only (though appearing as enjoyable to worldly-minded people). They have a beginning and an end (they come and go); Arjuna, it is for this reason that a wise man does not indulge in them.

column3:
śaknotīhaiva yaḥ(s) soḍhuṃ(m), prākṣarīravimokṣaṇāt।
kāmakrodhodbhvaṃ(v̐) vegaṃ(m), sa yuktaḥ(s) sa sukhī naraḥ॥5.23॥
column4: He alone, who is able to withstand, in this very life before casting off this body, the urges of lust and anger, is a Yogī, and he alone is a happy man.
column5: He alone, who is able to withstand, in this very life before casting off this body, the urges of lust and anger, is a Yogī, and he alone is a happy man.

column3:
yo'ntaḥsukho'ntarārāmaḥ(s),  tathāntarjyotireva  yaḥ।
sa  yogī  brahmanirvāṇaṃ(m),  brahmabhūto'dhigacchati॥5.24॥
column4: He who is happy within himself, enjoys within himself the delight of the soul, and, even so, is illumined by the inner light (light of the soul), such a Yogī (Sāṅkhyayogī) identified with Brahma attains Brahma, who is all peace.
column5: He who is happy within himself, enjoys within himself the delight of the soul, and, even so, is illumined by the inner light (light of the soul), such a Yogī (Sāṅkhyayogī) identified with Brahma attains Brahma, who is all peace.

column3:
labhante  brahmanirvāṇam,   ṛṣayaḥ  kṣīṇakalmaṣāḥ।
chinnadvaidhā yatātmānaḥ(s), sarvabhūtahite ratāḥ॥5.25॥
column4: The seers whose sins have been purged, whose doubts have been dispelled by knowledge, whose disciplined mind is firmly established in God and who are devoted to the welfare of all beings, attain Brahma, who is all peace.
column5: The seers whose sins have been purged, whose doubts have been dispelled by knowledge, whose disciplined mind is firmly established in God and who are devoted to the welfare of all beings, attain Brahma, who is all peace.

column3:
kāmakrodhaviyuktānāṃ(y̐),   yatīnāṃ(y̐)   yatacetasām।
abhito  brahmanirvāṇaṃ(v̐),   vartate   viditātmanām॥5.26॥
column4: To those wise men who are free from lust and anger, who have subdued their mind and have realized God, Brahma, the abode of eternal peace, is present all-round.
column5: To those wise men who are free from lust and anger, who have subdued their mind and have realized God, Brahma, the abode of eternal peace, is present all-round.

column3:
sparśānkṛtvā bahirbāhyāṃś, cakṣuścaivāntare bhruvoḥ।
prāṇāpānau    samau   kṛtvā,   nāsābhyantaracāriṇau॥5.27॥
column4: Shutting out all thoughts of external enjoyments, with the gaze fixed on the space between the eye-brows, having regulated the Prāņa (outgoing) and the Apāna (incoming) breaths flowing within the nostrils,
column5: Shutting out all thoughts of external enjoyments, with the gaze fixed on the space between the eye-brows, having regulated the Prāņa (outgoing) and the Apāna (incoming) breaths flowing within the nostrils,

column3:
yatendriyamanobuddhiḥ(r),    munirmokṣaparāyaṇaḥ।
vigatecchābhayakrodho,  yaḥ(s)  sadā  mukta   eva   saḥ॥5.28॥
column4: he who has brought his senses, mind and intellect under control-such a contemplative soul intent on liberation and free from desire, fear and anger, is ever liberated.
column5: he who has brought his senses, mind and intellect under control-such a contemplative soul intent on liberation and free from desire, fear and anger, is ever liberated.

column3:
bhoktāraṃ(y̐) yajñatapasāṃ(m), sarvalokamaheśvaram।
suhṛdaṃ(m) sarvabhūtānāṃ(ñ), jñātvā māṃ(m) śāntimṛcchati॥5.29॥
column4: Having known Me in reality as the enjoyer of all sacrifices and austerities, the supreme Lord of all the worlds, and the selfless friend of all beings, My devotee attains peace.
column5: Having known Me in reality as the enjoyer of all sacrifices and austerities, the supreme Lord of all the worlds, and the selfless friend of all beings, My devotee attains peace.

column3:
Om tatsaditi śrīmadbhagavadgītāsu upaniṣatsu brahmavidyāyāṃ(y̐)
yogaśāstre śrīkṛṣṇārjunasaṃvāde  karmasannyāsayogo nāma
pañcamo'dhyāyaḥ ||

om śrīparamātmane namaḥ
Śrīmadbhagavadgītā
atha ṣaṣṭho'dhyāyaḥ

śrībhagavānuvāca
anāśritaḥ(kh) karmaphalaṃ(ṅ), kāryaṃ(ṅ) karma karoti yaḥ।
sa  sannyāsī  ca  yogī  ca,   na  niragnirna cākriyaḥ॥6.1॥
column4: Śrī Bhagavān said :He who does his duty without expecting the fruit of action is Sannyāsī (Sāṅkhyayogī)  and Yogī (Karmayogī) both. He is no Sannyāsī (renouncer) who has simply renounced the sacred fire; even so, he is no Yogī who has given up all activity.
column5:
Śrī Bhagavān said : 
He who performs his duty without expecting the fruits of action is a Sannyāsī and a Yogī. He is not a Sannyāsī merely by renouncing the sacred fire, nor is he a Yogī by giving up all action.

column3:
yaṃ(m) sannyāsamiti prāhuḥ(r), yogaṃ(n) taṃ(v̐) viddhi pāṇḍava।
na  hyasannyastasaṅkalpo, yogī  bhavati  kaścana॥6.2॥
column4: Arjuna, you must know that what they call as Sannyāsa is no other than Yoga; for none becomes a Yogī who has not abandoned his 'Saṅkalpas' (thoughts of the world).
column5: Arjuna, you must know that what they call Sannyāsa is no other than Yoga; for none becomes a Yogī who has not abandoned his 'Saṅkalpas' (thoughts of the world).

column3:
ārurukṣormuneryogaṃ(ṅ),    karma   kāraṇamucyate।
yogārūḍhasya   tasyaiva,  śamaḥ(kh)  kāraṇamucyate॥6.3॥
column4: To the contemplative soul who desires to attain Karmayoga, selfless action is said to be the means; for the same man when he is established in Yoga, absence of all 'Saṅkalpas' (thoughts of the world) is said to be the way to blessedness.
column5: To the contemplative soul who desires to attain Karmayoga, selfless action is said to be the means; for the same man when he is established in Yoga, absence of all 'Saṅkalpas' (thoughts of the world) is said to be the way to blessedness.

column3:
yadā  hi  nendriyārtheṣu,  na  karmasvanuṣajjate।
sarvasaṅkalpasannyāsī,      yogārūḍhastadocyate॥6.4॥
column4: When a man ceases to have any attachment for the objects of senses and for actions, and has renounced all ' 'Saṅkalpas' (thoughts of the world), he is said to have attained Yoga.
column5: When a man ceases to have any attachment for the objects of senses and for actions, and has renounced all 'Saṅkalpas' (thoughts of the world), he is said to have attained Yoga.

column3:
uddharedātmanātmānaṃ(n),     nātmānamavasādayet।
ātmaiva  hyātmano  bandhuḥ(r),  ātmaiva  ripurātmanaḥ॥6.5॥
column4: One should lift oneself by one’s own efforts and should not degrade oneself; for one’s own self is one’s friend, and one’s own self is one’s enemy.
column5: One should lift oneself by one’s own efforts and should not degrade oneself; for one’s own self is one’s friend, and one’s own self is one’s enemy.

column3:
bandhurātmātmanastasya,    yenātmaivātmanā   jitaḥ।
anātmanastu    śatrutve,    vartetātmaiva  śatruvat॥6.6॥
column4: One’s own self is the friend of the soul by whom the lower self (consisting of the mind, senses, and body) has been conquered; even so, the very Self of him, who has not conquered his lower self, behaves antagonistically like an enemy.
column5: One’s own self is the friend of the soul by whom the lower self (consisting of the mind, senses, and body) has been conquered; even so, the very Self of him, who has not conquered his lower self, behaves antagonistically like an enemy.

column3:
jitātmanaḥ(ph)  praśāntasya, paramātmā  samāhitaḥ।
śītoṣṇasukhaduḥkheṣu,   tathā  mānāpamānayoḥ॥6.7॥
column4: The Supreme Spirit is rooted in the knowledge of the self-controlled man whose mind is perfectly serene in the midst of pairs of opposites, such as cold and heat, joy and sorrow, and honor and ignominy.
column5: The Supreme Spirit is rooted in the knowledge of the self-controlled man whose mind is perfectly serene in the midst of pairs of opposites, such as cold and heat, joy and sorrow, and honor and ignominy.

column3:
jñānavijñānatṛptātmā,     kūṭastho    vijitendriyaḥ।
yukta  ityucyate  yogī,    samaloṣṭāśmakāñcanaḥ॥6.8॥
column4: The Yogī whose mind is sated with Jñāna (Knowledge of Nirguņa Brahma) and Vijñāna (Knowledge of manifest Divinity), who is unmoved under all circumstances, whose senses are completely under control, and to whom mud, stone and gold are all alike, is spoken of as a God-realized soul.
column5: The Yogī whose mind is sated with Jñāna (Knowledge of Nirguņa Brahma) and Vijñāna (Knowledge of manifest Divinity), who is unmoved under all circumstances, whose senses are completely under control, and to whom mud, stone and gold are all alike, is spoken of as a God-realized soul.

column3:
suhṛnmitrāryudāsīna,   madhyasthadveṣyabandhuṣu।
sādhuṣvapi  ca  pāpeṣu,   samabuddhirviśiṣyate॥6.9॥
column4: He who looks upon well-wishers and neutrals as well as mediators, friends and foes, relatives and inimicals, the virtuous and the sinful, with equanimity, stands supreme.
column5: He who looks upon well-wishers and neutrals as well as mediators, friends and foes, relatives and inimicals, the virtuous and the sinful, with equanimity, stands supreme.

column3:
yogī  yuñjīta  satatam,  ātmānaṃ(m) rahasi sthitaḥ।
ekākī      yatacittātmā,          nirāśīraparigrahaḥ॥6.10॥
column4: Living in seclusion all by himself, the Yogī who has controlled his mind and body, and is free from desires and void of possessions, should constantly engage his mind in meditation.
column5: Living in seclusion all by himself, the Yogī who has controlled his mind and body, and is free from desires and void of possessions, should constantly engage his mind in meditation.

column3:
śucau  deśe  pratiṣṭhāpya,   sthiramāsanamātmanaḥ।
nātyucchritaṃ(n)  nātinīcaṃ(ñ),   cailājinakuśottaram॥6.11॥
column4: Having firmly set his seat in a spot which is free from dirt and other impurities with the sacred Kuśa grass, deerskin and cloth spread thereon one upon the other (Kuśa below, deerskin in the middle and cloth uppermost), neither very high nor very low;
column5: Having firmly set his seat in a spot which is free from dirt and other impurities with the sacred Kuśa grass, deer skin and cloth spread thereon one upon the other (Kuśa below, deer skin in the middle and cloth uppermost), neither very high nor very low;

column3:
tatraikāgraṃ(m) manaḥ(kh) kṛtvā, yatacittendriyakriyaḥ।
upaviśyāsane    yuñjyād,   yogamātmaviśuddhaye॥6.12॥
column4: And occupying that seat, concentrating the mind and controlling the functions of the mind and senses, he should practice Yoga for self purification.
column5: And occupying that seat, concentrating the mind and controlling the functions of the mind and senses, he should practice Yoga for self purification.

column3:
samaṃ(ṅ) kāyaśirogrīvaṃ(n), dhārayannacalaṃ(m) sthiraḥ।
samprekṣya  nāsikāgraṃ(m) svaṃ(n),  diśaścānavalokayan॥6.13॥
column4: Holding the trunk, head and neck straight and steady, remaining firm and fixing the gaze on the tip of his nose, without looking in other directions.
column5: Holding the trunk, head and neck straight and steady, remaining firm and fixing the gaze on the tip of his nose, without looking in other directions.

column3:
praśāntātmā  vigatabhīḥ(r),  brahmacārivrate  sthitaḥ।
manaḥ(s) saṃyamya  maccitto,  yukta āsīta matparaḥ॥6.14॥
column4: Firm in the vow of complete chastity and fearlessness, keeping himself perfectly calm and with the mind held in restraint and fixed on Me, the vigilant Yogī should sit absorbed in Me.
column5: Firm in the vow of complete chastity and fearlessness, keeping himself perfectly calm and with the mind held in restraint and fixed on Me, the vigilant Yogī should sit absorbed in Me.

column3:
yuñjannevaṃ(m) sadātmānaṃ(y̐), yogī niyatamānasaḥ।
śāntiṃ(n) nirvāṇaparamāṃ(m), matsaṃsthāmadhigacchati॥6.15॥
column4: Thus constantly applying his mind to Me, the Yogī of disciplined mind attains the everlasting peace, consisting of Supreme Bliss, which abides in Me.
column5: Thus constantly applying his mind to Me, the Yogī of disciplined mind attains the everlasting peace, consisting of Supreme Bliss, which abides in Me.

column3:
nātyaśnatastu   yogo'sti,   na   caikāntamanaśnataḥ।
na     cātisvapnaśīlasya,   jāgrato  naiva   cārjuna॥6.16॥
column4: Arjuna, this yoga is neither for him who overeats, nor for him who observes a complete fast; it is neither for him who is given to too much sleep, nor even for him who is ceaselessly awake.
column5: Arjuna, this yoga is neither for him who overeats, nor for him who observes a complete fast; it is neither for him who is given to too much sleep, nor even for him who is ceaselessly awake.

column3:
yuktāhāravihārasya,     yuktaceṣṭasya     karmasu।
yuktasvapnāvabodhasya,    yogo   bhavati  duḥkhahā॥6.17॥
column4: Yoga which rids one of woe, is accomplished only by him who is regulated in diet and recreation, regulated in performing actions, and is regulated in sleep and wakefulness.
column5: Yoga which rids one of woe, is accomplished only by him who is regulated in diet and recreation, regulated in performing actions, and is regulated in sleep and wakefulness.

column3:
yadā    viniyataṃ(ñ)   cittam,   ātmanyevāvatiṣṭhate।
niḥspṛhaḥ(s)   sarvakāmebhyo,   yukta  ityucyate  tadā॥6.18॥
column4: When the mind which is thoroughly disciplined, gets riveted on God alone, then the person who is free from yearning for all enjoyments is said to be established in Yoga.
column5: When the mind which is thoroughly disciplined, gets riveted on God alone, then the person who is free from yearning for all enjoyments is said to be established in Yoga.

column3:
yathā  dīpo  nivātastho,   neṅgate   sopamā  smṛtā।
yogino    yatacittasya,  yuñjato    yogamātmanaḥ॥6.19॥
column4: As a flame does not flicker in a windless place, such is stated to be the picture of the disciplined mind of Yogī practicing meditation on God.
column5: As a flame does not flicker in a windless place, such is stated to be the picture of the disciplined mind of Yogī practicing meditation on God.

column3:
yatroparamate   cittaṃ(n),  niruddhaṃ(y̐)   yogasevayā।
yatra caivātmanātmānaṃ(m),  paśyannātmani tuṣyati॥6.20॥
column4: The state in which the Chitta (mind), subdued through the practice of Yoga, becomes completely tranquil, and in which, realizing God through subtle reasoning purified by meditation on God, the soul rejoices only in God.
column5: The state in which the Chitta (mind), subdued through the practice of Yoga, becomes completely tranquil, and in which, realizing God through subtle reasoning purified by meditation on God, the soul rejoices only in God.

column3:
sukhamātyantikaṃ(y̐) yattad, buddhigrāhyamatīndriyam।
vetti  yatra  na  caivāyaṃ(m),  sthitaścalati  tattvataḥ॥6.21॥
column4: Nay, in which the soul experiences the eternal and super-sensuous joy which can be intuited only through the subtle and purified intellect, and wherein established the said Yogī moves not from Truth on any account.
column5: Nay, in which the soul experiences the eternal and super-sensuous joy which can be intuited only through the subtle and purified intellect, and wherein established the said Yogī moves not from Truth on any account.

column3:
yaṃ(l̐) labdhvā cāparaṃ(l̐) lābhaṃ(m), manyate nādhikaṃ(n) tataḥ।
yasminsthito     na    duḥkhena,  guruṇāpi   vicālyate॥6.22॥
column4: And having obtained which he does not reckon any other gain as greater than that, and established in which he is not shaken even by the heaviest of sorrows;
column5: And having obtained which he does not reckon any other gain as greater than that, and established in which he is not shaken even by the heaviest of sorrows;

column3:
taṃ(v̐) vidyād duḥkhasaṃyoga, viyogaṃ(y̐) yogasañjñitam।
sa    niścayena    yoktavyo,    yogo'nirviṇṇacetasā॥6.23॥
column4: That state called Yoga, which is free from the contact of sorrow (in the form of transmigration), should be known. Nay, this Yoga should be resolutely practiced with an unwearied mind.
column5: That state called Yoga, which is free from the contact of sorrow (in the form of transmigration), should be known. Nay, this Yoga should be resolutely practiced with an unwearied mind.

column3:
saṅkalpaprabhavānkāmāṃs, tyaktvā   sarvānaśeṣataḥ।
manasaivendriyagrāmaṃ(v̐),   viniyamya   samantataḥ॥6.24॥
column4: Completely renouncing all desires arising from the Saṅkalpas (thoughts of the world), and fully restraining all the senses from all sides by the mind.
column5: Completely renouncing all desires arising from the Saṅkalpas (thoughts of the world), and fully restraining all the senses from all sides by the mind.

column3:
śanaiḥ(s)  śanairuparamed,   buddhyā   dhṛtigṛhītayā।
ātmasaṃsthaṃ(m) manaḥ(kh) kṛtvā, na kiñcidapi cintayet॥6.25॥
column4: He should through gradual practice, attain tranquillity; and fixing the mind on God through reason controlled by steadfastness, he should not think of any thing else.
column5: He should through gradual practice, attain tranquillity; and fixing the mind on God through reason controlled by steadfastness, he should not think of any thing else.

column3:
yato   yato  niścarati,   manaścañcalamasthiram।
tatastato  niyamyaitad,  ātmanyeva  vaśaṃ(n)  nayet॥6.26॥
column4: Drawing back the restless and fidgety mind from all those objects after which it runs, he should repeatedly fix it on God
column5: Drawing back the restless and fidgety mind from all those objects after which it runs, he should repeatedly fix it on God

column3:
praśāntamanasaṃ(m) hyenaṃ(y̐), yoginaṃ(m) sukhamuttamam।
upaiti   śāntarajasaṃ(m),   brahmabhūtamakalmaṣam॥6.27॥
column4: For, to the Yogī whose mind is perfectly serene, who is sinless, whose passion is subdued, and who is identified with Brahma, the embodiment of Truth, Knowledge and Bliss, supreme happiness comes as a matter of course
column5: For, to the Yogī whose mind is perfectly serene, who is sinless, whose passion is subdued, and who is identified with Brahma, the embodiment of Truth, Knowledge and Bliss, supreme happiness comes as a matter of course.

column3:
yuñjannevaṃ(m) sadātmānaṃ(y̐), yogī vigatakalmaṣaḥ।
sukhena brahmasaṃsparśam, atyantaṃ(m) sukhamaśnute॥6.28॥
column4: The sinless Yogī, thus uniting his Self constantly with God, easily enjoys the eternal Bliss of oneness with Brahma.
column5: The sinless Yogī, thus uniting his Self constantly with God, easily enjoys the eternal Bliss of oneness with Brahma.

column3:
sarvabhūtasthamātmānaṃ(m), sarvabhūtāni cātmani।
īkṣate   yogayuktātmā,  sarvatra   samadarśanaḥ॥6.29॥
column4: The Yogī who is united in identity with the all-pervading, infinite consciousness, whose vision everwhere is even, beholds the Self existing in all beings and all beings as assumed in the Self.
column5: The Yogī who is united in identity with the all-pervading, infinite consciousness, whose vision everywhere is even, beholds the Self existing in all beings and all beings as assumed in the Self.

column3:
yo māṃ(m) paśyati sarvatra, sarvaṃ(ñ) ca mayi paśyati।
tasyāhaṃ(n) na praṇaśyāmi,  sa ca me na praṇaśyati॥6.30॥
column4: He who sees Me (the Universal Self) present in all beings, and all being existing within Me, he is never out of My sight, nor am I ever out of his sight.
column5: He who sees Me (the Universal Self) present in all beings, and all beings existing within Me, he is never out of My sight, nor am I ever out of his sight.

column3:
sarvabhūtasthitaṃ(y̐) yo māṃ(m), bhajatyekatvamāsthitaḥ।
sarvathā    vartamāno'pi,  sa   yogī   mayi  vartate॥6.31॥
column4: The Yogī who is established in union with Me, and worships Me as residing in all beings as their very Self, whatever activity he performs, he performs than in Me.
column5: The Yogī who is established in union with Me, and worships Me as residing in all beings as their very Self, whatever activity he performs, he performs them in Me.

column3:
ātmaupamyena sarvatra, samaṃ(m) paśyati yo'rjuna।
sukhaṃ(v̐) vā yadi vā duḥkhaṃ(m), sa yogī paramo mataḥ॥6.32॥
column4: Arjuna, he, who looks on all as one, on the analogy of his own Self, and looks upon the joy and sorrow of all equally - such a Yogī is deemed to be the highest of all.
column5: Arjuna, he, who looks on all as one, on the analogy of his own Self, and looks upon the joy and sorrow of all equally - such a Yogī is deemed to be the highest of all.

column3:
arjuna uvāca
yo’yaṃ(y̐) yogastvayā proktaḥ(s), sāmyena madhusūdana,
etasyāhaṃ(n) na paśyāmi, cañcalatvātsthitiṃ(m) sthirām॥6.33॥
column4: Arjuna said:Kṛṣṇa, owing to restlessness of mind, I do not perceive the stability of this Yoga in the form of equanimity, which You have just spoken of.
column5:
Arjuna said: 
Kṛṣṇa, owing to restlessness of mind, I do not perceive the stability of this Yoga in the form of equanimity, which You have just spoken of.

column3:
cañcalaṃ(m) hi manaḥ(kh) kṛṣṇa, pramāthi balavaddṛḍham।
tasyāhaṃ(n) nigrahaṃ(m) manye, vāyoriva suduṣkaram॥6.34॥
column4: For, Kṛṣṇa, the mind is very unsteady, turbulent, tenatious and powerful; therefore, I consider it as difficult to control as the wind.
column5: For, Kṛṣṇa, the mind is very unsteady, turbulent, tenacious and powerful; therefore, I consider it as difficult to control as the wind.

column3:
śrībhagavānuvāca
asaṃśayaṃ(m) mahābāho, mano durnigrahaṃ(ñ) calam।
abhyāsena  tu  kaunteya,  vairāgyeṇa  ca gṛhyate॥6.35॥
column4: Śrī Bhagavān said:The mind is restless no doubt, and difficult to curb, Arjuna; but it can be brought under control by repeated practice (of meditation) and by the exercise of dispassion, O son of Kuntī.
column5:
Śrī Bhagavān said: 
The mind is restless no doubt, and difficult to curb, Arjuna; but it can be brought under control by repeated practice (of meditation) and by the exercise of dispassion, O son of Kuntī.

column3:
asaṃyatātmanā  yogo,  duṣprāpa  iti  me  matiḥ।
vaśyātmanā  tu  yatatā,    śakyo'vāptumupāyataḥ॥6.36॥
column4: Yoga is difficult of achievement by one whose mind is not subdued by him; however, who has the mind under control, and is ceaselessly striving, it can be easily attained through practice. Such is My conviction.
column5: Yoga is difficult of achievement by one whose mind is not subdued by him; however, who has the mind under control, and is ceaselessly striving, it can be easily attained through practice. Such is My conviction.

column3:
arjuna uvāca
ayatiḥ(ś)     śraddhayopeto,    yogāccalitamānasaḥ।
aprāpya yogasaṃsiddhiṃ(ṅ), kāṃ(ṅ) gatiṃ(ṅ) kṛṣṇa gacchati॥6.37॥
column4: Arjuna said:Kṛṣṇa, what becomes of the aspirant who, though endowed with faith, has not been able to subdue his passion, and whose mind is, therefore, diverted from Yoga at the time of death, and who thus fails to reach perfection in Yoga (God-Realization)?
column5:
Arjuna said: 
Kṛṣṇa, what becomes of the aspirant who, though endowed with faith, has not been able to subdue his passion, and whose mind is, therefore, diverted from Yoga at the time of death, and who thus fails to reach perfection in Yoga (God-Realization)?

column3:
kaccinnobhayavibhraṣṭaḥ(ś), chinnābhramiva naśyati।
apratiṣṭho mahābāho, vimūḍho brahmaṇaḥ(ph) pathi॥6.38॥
column4: Kṛṣṇa, swerved from the path leading to God-Realization and without any thing to stand upon, is he not lost like the scattered cloud, deprived of both God-Realization and heavenly enjoyment?
column5: Kṛṣṇa, swerved from the path leading to God-Realization and without any thing to stand upon, is he not lost like the scattered cloud, deprived of both God-Realization and heavenly enjoyment?

column3:
etanme saṃśayaṃ(ṅ)  kṛṣṇa, chettumarhasyaśeṣataḥ।
tvadanyaḥ(s) saṃśayasyāsya, chettā na hyupapadyate॥6.39॥
column4: Kṛṣṇa, only You are capable to remove this doubt of mine completely; for none other than You can dispel this doubt.
column5: Kṛṣṇa, only You are capable of removing this doubt of mine completely; for none other than You can dispel this doubt.

column3:
śrībhagavānuvāca
pārtha  naiveha  nāmutra,  vināśastasya  vidyate।
na hi  kalyāṇakṛtkaścid,  durgatiṃ(n)  tāta  gacchati॥6.40॥
column4: Śrī Bhagavān said:Arjuna, there is no fall for him either here or hereafter. For, O My beloved,  none who strives for self-redemption (i.e., God-Realization) ever meets with evil destiny.
column5:
Śrī Bhagavān said: 
Arjuna, there is no fall for him either here or hereafter. For, O My beloved,  none who strives for self-redemption (i.e., God-Realization) ever meets with evil destiny.

column3:
prāpya puṇyakṛtāṃ(l̐) lokān, uṣitvā śāśvatīḥ(s) samāḥ।
śucīnāṃ(m) śrīmatāṃ(ṅ) gehe, yogabhraṣṭo'bhijāyate॥6.41॥
column4: Such a person who has strayed from Yoga, obtains the higher worlds, (heaven etc.) to which men of meritorious deeds alone are entitled, and having resided there for innumerable years, takes birth  of pious and prosperous parents.
column5: Such a person who has strayed from Yoga, obtains the higher worlds, (heaven etc.) to which men of meritorious deeds alone are entitled, and having resided there for innumerable years, is born to pious and prosperous parents.

column3:
athavā   yogināmeva,  kule  bhavati  dhīmatām।
etaddhi  durlabhataraṃ(l̐),  loke  janma  yadīdṛśam॥6.42॥
column4: Or, if he is possessed of dispassion, then not attaining to those reasons he is born in the family of enlightened Yogīs; but such a birth in this world is very difficult to obtain.
column5: Or, if he is possessed of dispassion, then not attaining to those reasons he is born in the family of enlightened Yogīs; but such a birth in this world is very difficult to obtain.

column3:
tatra taṃ(m) buddhisaṃyogaṃ(l̐), labhate paurvadehikam।
yatate ca tato bhūyaḥ(s),  saṃsiddhau kurunandana॥6.43॥
column4: Arjuna, he automatically regains in that birth the latencies of even-mindedness of his previous birth; and through that he strives, harder than ever for perfection in the form of God-Realization.
column5: Arjuna, he automatically regains in that birth the latencies of even-mindedness of his previous birth; and through that he strives, harder than ever for perfection in the form of God-Realization.

column3:
pūrvābhyāsena  tenaiva,  hriyate   hyavaśo'pi  saḥ।
jijñāsurapi     yogasya,   śabdabrahmātivartate॥6.44॥
column4: The other one who takes birth in a rich family, though under the sway of his senses, feels drawn towards God by force of the habit acquired in his previous birth; nay, even the seeker of Yoga (in the form of even-mindedness) transcends the fruit of actions performed with some interested motive as laid down in the Vedas.
column5: The other one who takes birth in a rich family, though under the sway of his senses, feels drawn towards God by force of the habit acquired in his previous birth; nay, even the seeker of Yoga (in the form of even-mindedness) transcends the fruit of actions performed with some interested motive as laid down in the Vedas.

column3:
prayatnādyatamānastu,   yogī  saṃśuddhakilbiṣaḥ।
anekajanmasaṃsiddhaḥ(s),  tato yāti parāṃ(ṅ) gatim॥6.45॥
column4: The Yogī, however, who diligently takes up the practice, attains perfection in this very life with the help of latencies of many births, and being thoroughly purged of sin, forthwith reaches the Supreme state.
column5: The Yogī, however, who diligently takes up the practice, attains perfection in this very life with the help of latencies of many births, and being thoroughly purged of sin, forthwith reaches the Supreme state.

column3:
tapasvibhyo'dhiko  yogī,  jñānibhyo'pi  mato'dhikaḥ।
karmibhyaścādhiko  yogī,  tasmādyogī  bhavārjuna॥6.46॥
column4: The Yogī is superior to the ascetics; he is regarded superior even to those versed in sacred lore. The Yogī is also superior to those who perform action with some interested motive. Therefore, Arjuna, do become a Yogī.
column5: The Yogī is superior to the ascetics; he is regarded superior even to those versed in sacred lore. The Yogī is also superior to those who perform action with some interested motive. Therefore, Arjuna, do become a Yogī.

column3:
yogināmapi   sarveṣāṃ(m),  madgatenāntarātmanā।
śraddhāvānbhajate yo māṃ(m), sa me yuktatamo mataḥ॥6.47॥
column4: Of all the Yogīs, again, he who devoutly worships Me with his mind focussed on Me is considered by Me to be the best Yogī.
column5: Of all the Yogīs, again, he who devoutly worships Me with his mind focused on Me is considered by Me to be the best Yogī.

column3:
Om tatsaditi śrīmadbhagavadgītāsu upaniṣatsu brahmavidyāyāṃ(y̐) yogaśāstre śrīkṛṣṇārjunasaṃvāde ātmasaṃyamayogo nāma 
ṣaṣṭho'dhyāyaḥ ॥
॥OM ŚRĪKṚṢṆĀRPAṆAMASTU॥

om śrīparamātmane namaḥ
Śrīmadbhagavadgītā
atha saptamo'dhyāyaḥ

śrībhagavānuvāca
mayyāsaktamanāḥ(ph) pārtha, yogaṃ(y̐) yuñjanmadāśrayaḥ।
asaṃśayaṃ(m) samagraṃ(m) māṃ(y̐), yathā jñāsyasi tacchṛṇu॥7.1॥
column4: Śrī Bhagavān said :Arjuna, now listen how with the mind attached to Me (through exclusive love) and practising Yoga with absolute dependence on Me, you will know Me, the repository of all power, strength and glory and other attributes, the Universal soul, in entirety and without any shadow of doubt.
column5:
Śrī Bhagavān said : 
Arjuna, now listen how, with your mind attached to Me (through exclusive love) and practicing Yoga with absolute dependence on Me, you will know Me, you will know Me, the repository of all power, strength and glory and other attributes, the Universal soul, in entirety and without any shadow of doubt.

column3:
jñānaṃ(n) te'haṃ(m) savijñānam, idaṃ(v̐) vakṣyāmyaśeṣataḥ।
yajjñātvā   neha   bhūyo'nyaj,  jñātavyamavaśiṣyate॥7.2॥
column4: I shall unfold to you in its entirety this wisdom (Knowledge of God in His absolute formless aspect) along with the Knowledge of the qualified aspect of God (both with form and without form), having known which nothing else remains yet to be known in this world.
column5: I shall unfold to you in its entirety this wisdom (Knowledge of God in His absolute formless aspect) along with the Knowledge of the qualified aspect of God (both with form and without form), having known which nothing else remains yet to be known in this world.

column3:
manuṣyāṇāṃ(m) sahasreṣu, kaścidyatati siddhaye।
yatatāmapi siddhānāṃ(ṅ), kaścinmāṃ(v̐) vetti tattvataḥ॥7.3॥
column4: Hardly one among thousands of men strives to realize Me; of those striving Yogīs, again, some rare one, devoting himself exclusively to Me, knows Me in reality.
column5: Hardly one among thousands of men strives to realize Me; and among those striving Yogis, again some rare one devotes himself exclusively to Me.

column3:
bhūmirāpo'nalo vāyuḥ(kh), khaṃ(m) mano buddhireva ca।
ahaṅkāra  itīyaṃ(m)  me,  bhinnā  prakṛtiraṣṭadhā॥7.4॥
column4: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, reason and also ego-these constitute My nature divided into eight parts. This indeed is My lower (material) nature;
column5: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, reason and also ego-these constitute My nature divided into eight parts. This indeed is My lower (material) nature;

column3:
apareyamitastvanyāṃ(m), prakṛtiṃ(v̐) viddhi me parām।
jīvabhūtāṃ(m) mahābāho, yayedaṃ(n) dhāryate jagat॥7.5॥
column4: This is My inferior Nature; but distinct from this, O Valiant One, know thou that my Superior Nature is the very Life which sustains the universe.
column5: This is My lower (material) nature. But beyond this, O valiant Arjuna, know My higher nature is the very Life by which this entire universe is sustained.

column3:
etadyonīni       bhūtāni,    sarvāṇītyupadhāraya।
ahaṃ(ṅ) kṛtsnasya jagataḥ(ph), prabhavaḥ(ph) pralayastathā॥7.6॥
column4: Arjuna, know that all beings have evolved from this twofold Prakṛti, and that I am the source of the entire creation, and into Me again it dissolves.
column5: Arjuna, know that all beings have evolved from this twofold Prakṛti, and that I am the source of the entire creation, and into Me again it dissolves.

column3:
mattaḥ(ph) parataraṃ(n) nānyat, kiñcidasti dhanañjaya।
mayi sarvamidaṃ(m) protaṃ(m), sūtre maṇigaṇā iva॥7.7॥
column4: There is nothing else besides Me, Arjuna. Like clusters of yarn-beads formed by knots on a thread, all this is threaded on Me.
column5: There is nothing else besides Me, Arjuna. Like clusters of beads strung on a thread, all this is threaded on Me.

column3:
raso'hamapsu  kaunteya,  prabhāsmi  śaśisūryayoḥ।
praṇavaḥ(s) sarvavedeṣu, śabdaḥ(kh) khe pauruṣaṃ(n) nṛṣu॥7.8॥
column4: Arjuna, I am the sapidity in water and the radiance in the moon and the sun; I am the sacred syllable OM in all the Vedas, the sound in ether, and virility in men.
column5: Arjuna, I am the sapidity in water and the radiance in the moon and the sun; I am the sacred syllable OM in all the Vedas, the sound in ether, and virility in men.

column3:
puṇyo gandhaḥ(ph) pṛthivyāṃ(ñ) ca, tejaścāsmi vibhāvasau।
jīvanaṃ(m)  sarvabhūteṣu,  tapaścāsmi  tapasviṣu॥7.9॥
column4: I am the pure odour (the subtle principle of smell) in the earth and the brightness in fire; nay, I am the life in all beings and austerity in the ascetics.
column5: I am the pure odour (the subtle principle of smell) in the earth and the brightness in fire; nay, I am the life in all beings and austerity in the ascetics.

column3:
bījaṃ(m) māṃ(m) sarvabhūtānāṃ(v̐), viddhi pārtha sanātanam।
buddhirbuddhimatāmasmi,      tejastejasvināmaham॥7.10॥
column4: Arjuna, know Me the eternal seed of all beings. I am the intelligence of the intelligent; the glory of the glorious am I.
column5: Arjuna, know Me the eternal seed of all beings. I am the intelligence of the intelligent; the glory of the glorious am I.

column3:
balaṃ(m) balavatāṃ(ñ) cāhaṃ(ṅ), kāmarāgavivarjitam।
dharmāviruddho bhūteṣu, kāmo'smi bharatarṣabha॥7.11॥
column4: Arjuna, of the mighty I am the might, free from passion and desire; in beings I am the sexual desire not conflicting with virtue or scriptural injunctions.
column5: Arjuna, of the mighty I am the might, free from passion and desire; in beings I am the sexual desire not conflicting with virtue or scriptural injunctions.

column3:
ye  caiva  sāttvikā  bhāvā,   rājasāstāmasāśca ye।
matta eveti tānviddhi,  na  tvahaṃ(n) teṣu te mayi॥7.12॥
column4: Whatever other entities there are, born of Sattva (the quality of goodness), and those that are born of Rajas (the principle of activity) and Tamas (the principle of inertia), know them all as evolved from Me alone. In reality, however, neither do I exist in them, nor do they in Me.
column5: Whatever other entities there are, born of Sattva (the quality of goodness), and those that are born of Rajas (the principle of activity) and Tamas (the principle of inertia), know them all as evolved from Me alone. In reality, however, neither do I exist in them, nor do they in Me.

column3:
tribhirguṇamayairbhāvaiḥ(r), ebhiḥ(s) sarvamidaṃ(ñ) jagat।
mohitaṃ(n) nābhijānāti, māmebhyaḥ(ph) paramavyayam॥7.13॥
column4: The whole of this creation is deluded by these objects evolved from the three modes of Prakṛti- Sattva, Rajas and Tamas; that is why the world fails to recognize Me, standing apart from these, the Imperishable.
column5: The whole of this creation is deluded by these objects evolved from the three modes of Prakṛti- Sattva, Rajas and Tamas; that is why the world fails to recognize Me, standing apart from these, the Imperishable.

column3:
daivī  hyeṣā  guṇamayī,  mama  māyā  duratyayā।
māmeva ye prapadyante, māyāmetāṃ(n) taranti te॥7.14॥
column4: For, this most wonderful Māyā (veil) of Mine, consisting of the three Guņas (modes of Nature), is extremely difficult to breakthrough; those, however, who constantly adore Me alone, are able to cross it.
column5: For, this most wonderful Māyā (veil) of Mine, consisting of the three Guṇas (modes of Nature), is extremely difficult to break through; those, however, who constantly adore Me alone, are able to cross it.

column3:
na māṃ(n) duṣkṛtino mūḍhāḥ(ph), prapadyante narādhamāḥ।
māyayāpahṛtajñānā,   āsuraṃ(m)   bhāvamāśritāḥ॥7.15॥
column4: Those whose wisdom has been carried away by Māyā, and are of demoniac nature, such foolish and vile men of evil deeds do not adore Me.
column5: Those whose wisdom has been carried away by Māyā, and are of demoniac nature, such foolish and vile men of evil deeds do not adore Me.

column3:
caturvidhā bhajante māṃ(ñ), janāḥ(s) sukṛtino'rjuna।
ārto    jijñāsurarthārthī,  jñānī  ca bharatarṣabha॥7.16॥
column4: Four types of devotees of noble deeds worship Me, Arjuna, the seeker after worldly possessions, the afflicted, the seeker for knowledge, and the man of wisdom, O best of Bharatas.
column5: Four types of devotees of noble deeds worship Me, Arjuna, the seeker after worldly possessions, the afflicted, the seeker for knowledge, and the man of wisdom, O best of Bharatas.

column3:
teṣāṃ(ñ)   jñānī   nityayukta,   ekabhaktirviśiṣyate।
priyo hi jñānino'tyartham, ahaṃ(m) sa ca mama priyaḥ॥7.17॥
column4: Of these, the best is the man of wisdom, ever established in identity with Me and possessed of exclusive devotion. For, I am extremely dear to the wise man who knows Me in reality, and he is extremely dear to Me.
column5: Of these, the best is the man of wisdom, ever established in identity with Me and possessed of exclusive devotion. For, I am extremely dear to the wise man who knows Me in reality, and he is extremely dear to Me.

column3:
udārāḥ(s)  sarva  evaite,  jñānī  tvātmaiva me matam।
āsthitaḥ(s) sa hi yuktātmā, māmevānuttamāṃ(ṅ) gatim॥7.18॥
column4: Indeed, all these are noble, but the man of wisdom is My very self; such is My view. For such a devotee, who has his mind and intellect merged in Me, is firmly established in Me alone as the highest goal.
column5: Indeed, all these are noble, but the man of wisdom is My very self; such is My view. For such a devotee, who has his mind and intellect merged in Me, is firmly established in Me alone as the highest goal.

column3:
bahūnāṃ(ñ) janmanāmante, jñānavānmāṃ(m) prapadyate।
vāsudevaḥ(s) sarvamiti, sa mahātmā sudurlabhaḥ॥7.19॥
column4: In the very last of all births the enlightened person worships Me by realizing that all this is God. Such a great soul is very rare indeed.
column5: In the very last of all births the enlightened person worships Me by realizing that all this is God. Such a great soul is very rare indeed.

column3:
kāmaistaistairhṛtajñānāḥ(ph), prapadyante'nyadevatāḥ।
taṃ(n) taṃ(n) niyamamāsthāya, prakṛtyā niyatāḥ(s) svayā॥7.20॥
column4: Those whose wisdom has been carried away by various desires, being prompted by their own nature, worship other deities, adopting norms relating to each.
column5: Those whose wisdom has been carried away by various desires, being prompted by their own nature, worship other deities, adopting norms relating to each.

column3:
yo yo yāṃ(y̐) yāṃ(n) tanuṃ(m) bhaktaḥ(ś), śraddhayārcitumicchati।
tasya tasyācalāṃ(m) śraddhāṃ(n), tāmeva vidadhāmyaham॥7.21॥
column4: Whatever celestial form a devotee (craving for some worldly object) chooses to worship with reverence, I stabilize the faith of that particular devotee in that very form.
column5: Whatever celestial form a devotee (craving for some worldly object) chooses to worship with reverence, I stabilize the faith of that particular devotee in that very form.

column3:
sa  tayā  śraddhayā  yuktaḥ(s),  tasyārādhanamīhate।
labhate ca tataḥ(kh) kāmān, mayaiva vihitānhi tān॥7.22॥
column4: Endowed with such faith, he worships that particular deity and obtains through that deity without doubt his desired enjoyments as verily ordained by Me.
column5: Endowed with such faith, he worships that particular deity and obtains through that deity without doubt his desired enjoyments as verily ordained by Me.

column3:
antavattu phalaṃ(n) teṣāṃ(n), tadbhavatyalpamedhasām।
devāndevayajo   yānti,  madbhaktā   yānti   māmapi॥7.23॥
column4: The fruit gained by these people of small understanding, however, is perishable. The worshippers of gods attain the gods; whereas My devotees, howsoever they worship Me, eventually come to Me and Me alone.
column5: The fruit gained by these people of small understanding, however, is perishable. The worshippers of gods attain the gods; whereas My devotees, howsoever they worship Me, eventually come to Me and Me alone.

column3:
avyaktaṃ(v̐) vyaktimāpannaṃ(m), manyante māmabuddhayaḥ।
paraṃ(m)    bhāvamajānanto,  mamāvyayamanuttamam॥7.24॥
column4: Not knowing My supreme nature, unsurpassable and undecaying, the ignorant persons regard Me, who am the Supreme Spirit beyond the reach of mind and senses, and the embodiment of Truth, Knowledge and Bliss, to have assumed a finite form through birth as an ordinary human being.
column5: Not knowing My supreme nature, unsurpassable and undecaying, the ignorant persons regard Me, who am the Supreme Spirit beyond the reach of mind and senses, and the embodiment of Truth, Knowledge and Bliss, to have assumed a finite form through birth as an ordinary human being.

column3:
nāhaṃ(m) prakāśaḥ(s) sarvasya, yogamāyāsamāvṛtaḥ।
mūḍho'yaṃ(n) nābhijānāti, loko māmajamavyayam॥7.25॥
column4: Veiled by My Yogamāyā, My divine potency, I am not manifest to all. Hence these ignorant folk fail to recognize Me, the birthless and imperishable Supreme Deity i.e., consider Me as subject to birth and death.
column5: Veiled by My Yogamāyā, My divine potency, I am not manifest to all. Hence these ignorant folk fail to recognize Me, the birthless and imperishable Supreme Deity i.e., consider Me as subject to birth and death.

column3:
vedāhaṃ(m)  samatītāni,  vartamānāni  cārjuna।
bhaviṣyāṇi ca bhūtāni, māṃ(n) tu veda na kaścana॥7.26॥
column4: Arjuna, I know all beings, past as well as present, nay, even those that are yet to come; but none, devoid of faith and devotion, knows Me.
column5: Arjuna, I know all beings, past as well as present, nay, even those that are yet to come; but none who are devoid of faith and devotion know Me.

column3:
icchādveṣasamutthena,   dvandvamohena   bhārata।
sarvabhūtāni  sammohaṃ(m),  sarge  yānti parantapa॥7.27॥
column4: O valiant Arjuna, through delusion in the form of pairs of opposites (such as pleasure and pain etc.,) born of desire and aversion, all living creatures in this world are falling a prey to infatuation.
column5: O valiant Arjuna, through delusion in the form of pairs of opposites (such as pleasure and pain, etc.) born of desire and aversion, all living creatures in this world are falling a prey to infatuation.

column3:
yeṣāṃ(n) tvantagataṃ(m) pāpaṃ(ñ), janānāṃ(m) puṇyakarmaṇām।
te dvandvamohanirmuktā, bhajante māṃ(n) dṛḍhavratāḥ॥7.28॥
column4: But those men of virtuous deeds, whose sins have come to an end, being freed from delusion in the form of pairs of opposites born of attraction and repulsion, worship Me with a firm resolve in every way.
column5: But those men of virtuous deeds, whose sins have come to an end, being freed from delusion in the form of pairs of opposites born of attraction and repulsion, worship Me with a firm resolve in every way.

column3:
jarāmaraṇamokṣāya,    māmāśritya   yatanti   ye।
te brahma tadviduḥ(kh) kṛtsnam, adhyātmaṃ(ṅ) karma cākhilam॥7.29॥
column4: They who, having taken refuge in Me, strive for deliverance from old age and death, know Brahma (the Absolute), the whole Adhyātma (the totality of Jīvas or embodied souls), and the entire field of Karma (action) as well as My integral being, comprising Adhibhūta (the field of Matter),
column5: They who, having taken refuge in Me, strive for deliverance from old age and death, know Brahma (the Absolute), the whole Adhyātma (the totality of Jīvas or embodied souls), and the entire field of Karma (action) as well as My integral being, comprising Adhibhūta (the field of Matter),

column3:
sādhibhūtādhidaivaṃ(m) māṃ(m), sādhiyajñaṃ(ñ) ca ye viduḥ।
prayāṇakāle'pi  ca  māṃ(n), te  viduryuktacetasaḥ॥7.30॥
column4: Adhidaiva (Brahmā) and Adhiyajña (the unmanifest Divinity dwelling in the heart of all beings as their witness). And they who, possessed of a steadfast mind, know thus even at the hour of death, they too know Me alone.
column5: Adhidaiva (Brahmā) and Adhiyajña (the unmanifest Divinity dwelling in the heart of all beings as their witness). And they who, possessed of a steadfast mind, know thus even at the hour of death, they too know Me alone.

column3:
Om tatsaditi śrīmadbhagavadgītāsu upaniṣatsu brahmavidyāyāṃ(y̐)
 yogaśāstre śrīkṛṣṇārjunasaṃvāde jñānavijñānayogo nāma 
saptamo'dhyāyaḥ || 
|| OM ŚRĪKṚṢṆĀRPAṆAMASTU ||

om śrīparamātmane namaḥ
śrīmadbhagavadgītā
atha aṣṭamo'dhyāyaḥ

arjuna uvāca
kiṃ(n) tadbrahma kimadhyātmaṃ(ṅ), kiṃ(ṅ) karma puruṣottama।
adhibhūtaṃ(ñ) ca kiṃ(m) proktam, adhidaivaṃ(ṅ) kimucyate॥8.1॥
column4:
Arjuna said: 
Kṛṣṇā, what is that Brahma (Absolute), what is Adhyātma ( Spirit), and what is Karma (Action)? What is called Adhibhūta (Matter) and what is termed as Adhidaiva (Divine Intelligence)?
column5: Arjuna said: Kṛṣṇā, what is that Brahma (Absolute), what is Adhyātma ( Spirit), and what is Karma (Action)? What is called Adhibhūta (Matter) and what is termed as Adhidaiva (Divine Intelligence)?

column3:
adhiyajñaḥ(kh) kathaṃ(ṅ) ko'tra, dehe'sminmadhusūdana।
prayāṇakāle ca  kathaṃ(ñ),  jñeyo'si niyatātmabhiḥ॥8.2॥
column4: Kṛṣṇa, who is Adhiyajña here and how does he dwell in the body? And how are you to be realized at the time of death by those of steadfast mind?
column5: Kṛṣṇa, who is Adhiyajña here and how does he dwell in the body? And how are you to be realized at the time of death by those of steadfast mind?

column3:
śrībhagavānuvāca 
akṣaraṃ(m) brahma paramaṃ(m), svabhāvo'dhyātmamucyate।
bhūtabhāvodbhavakaro,  visargaḥ(kh)  karmasañjñitaḥ॥8.3॥
column4:
Śrī Bhagavan said: 
The supreme Indestructible is Brahma, one's own Self (the individual soul) is called AdhyAtṃā; and the Primal resolve of God (Visarga), which brings forth the existence of beings, is called Karma (Action).
column5: Śrī Bhagavan said: The supreme Indestructible is Brahma, one's own Self (the individual soul) is called AdhyAtṃā; and the Primal resolve of God (Visarga), which brings forth the existence of beings, is called Karma (Action).

column3:
adhibhūtaṃ(ṅ) kṣaro bhāvaḥ(ph), puruṣaścādhidaivatam।
adhiyajño'hamevātra,   dehe  dehabhṛtāṃ(v̐)   vara॥8.4॥
column4: All perishable objects are Adhibhūta, the shining Puruşa (Brahma) is Adhidaiva and in this body I Myself, dwelling as the inner witness, am Adhiyajña, O Arjuna !
column5: All perishable objects are Adhibhūta, the shining Puruşa (Brahma) is Adhidaiva and in this body I Myself, dwelling as the inner witness, am Adhiyajña, O Arjuna !

column3:
antakāle ca  māmeva,  smaranmuktvā kalevaram।
yaḥ(ph) prayāti sa madbhāvaṃ(y̐), yāti nāstyatra saṃśayaḥ॥8.5॥
column4: He who departs from the body, thinking of Me alone even at the time of death, attains My state; there is no doubt about it.
column5: He who departs from the body, thinking of Me alone even at the time of death, attains My state; there is no doubt about it.

column3:
yaṃ(y̐) yaṃ(v̐) vāpi smaranbhāvaṃ(n), tyajatyante kalevaram।
taṃ(n)  tamevaiti  kaunteya,  sadā  tadbhāvabhāvitaḥ॥8.6॥
column4: Arjuna, thinking of whatever entity one leaves the body at the time of death, that and that alone one attains, being ever absorbed in its thought.
column5: Arjuna, thinking of whatever entity one leaves the body at the time of death, that and that alone one attains, being ever absorbed in its thought..

column3:
tasmātsarveṣu  kāleṣu,  māmanusmara  yudhya  ca।
mayyarpitamanobuddhiḥ(r), māmevaiṣyasyasaṃśayam॥8.7॥
column4: Therefore, Arjuna, think of Me at all times and fight. With mind and reason thus set on Me, you will doubtless come to Me.
column5: Therefore, Arjuna, think of Me at all times and fight. With mind and reason thus set on Me, you will doubtless come to Me.

column3:
abhyāsayogayuktena,      cetasā   nānyagāminā।
paramaṃ(m) puruṣaṃ(n) divyaṃ(y̐), yāti pārthānucintayan॥8.8॥
column4: Arjuna, he who with his mind disciplined through Yoga in the form of practice of meditation and thinking of nothing else, is constantly engaged in contemplation of God attains the supremely effulgent Divine Purușa (God).
column5: Arjuna, he who with his mind disciplined through Yoga in the form of practice of meditation and thinking of nothing else, is constantly engaged in contemplation of God attains the supremely effulgent Divine Purușa (God).

column3:
kaviṃ(m) purāṇamanuśāsitāram,aṇoraṇīyāṃsamanusmaredyaḥ।
sarvasya dhātāramacintyarūpam,ādityavarṇaṃ(n) tamasaḥ(ph) parastāt॥8.9॥
column4: He who contemplates on the all-knowing, ageless Being, the Ruler of all, subtler than the subtle, the universal sustainer, possessing a form beyond human conception, effulgent like the sun and far beyond the darkness of ignorance.
column5: He who contemplates on the all-knowing, ageless Being, the Ruler of all, subtler than the subtle, the universal sustainer, possessing a form beyond human conception, effulgent like the sun and far beyond the darkness of ignorance..

column3:
prayāṇakāle    manasācalena,bhaktyā    yukto    yogabalena   caiva।
bhruvormadhye  prāṇamāveśya  samyak,sa taṃ(m) paraṃ(m) puruṣamupaiti divyam॥8.10॥
column4: Having by the power of Yoga firmly held the life-breath in the space between the two eyebrows even at the time of death, and then contemplating on God with a steadfast mind, full of devotion, he reaches verily that supreme divine Purușa (God).
column5: Having by the power of Yoga firmly held the life-breath in the space between the two eyebrows even at the time of death, and then contemplating on God with a steadfast mind, full of devotion, he reaches verily that supreme divine Purușa (God).

column3:
yadakṣaraṃ(v̐)     vedavido      vadanti, viśanti     yadyatayo    vītarāgāḥ।
yadicchanto   brahmacaryaṃ(ñ)   caranti, tatte  padaṃ(m)  saṅgraheṇa  pravakṣye॥8.11॥
column4: I shall tell you briefly about that Supreme goal (viz., God, who is an embodiment of Truth, Knowledge and Bliss), which the knowers of the Veda term as the Indestructible, which striving recluses, free from passion, merge into, and desiring which the celibates practise Brahmacarya.
column5: I shall tell you briefly about that Supreme goal (viz., God, who is an embodiment of Truth, Knowledge and Bliss), which the knowers of the Veda term as the Indestructible, which striving recluses, free from passion, merge into, and desiring which the celibates practise Brahmacarya.

column3:
sarvadvārāṇi  saṃyamya,  mano  hṛdi  nirudhya  ca।
mūrdhnyādhāyātmanaḥ(ph) prāṇam, āsthito yogadhāraṇām॥8.12॥
column4: Having controlled all the senses, and firmly holding the mind in the heart, and then drawing the life-breath to the head, and thus remaining steadfast in Yogic concentration on God
column5: Having controlled all the senses, and firmly holding the mind in the heart, and then drawing the life-breath to the head, and thus remaining steadfast in Yogic concentration on God

column3:
omityekākṣaraṃ(m) brahma, vyāharanmāmanusmaran।
yaḥ(ph) prayāti tyajandehaṃ(m), sa yāti paramāṃ(ṅ) gatim॥8.13॥
column4: he who leaves body and departs uttering the one Indestructible Brahma, OM, and dwelling on Me in My absolute aspect, reaches the supreme goal.
column5: he who leaves body and departs uttering the one Indestructible Brahma, OM, and dwelling on Me in My absolute aspect, reaches the supreme goal.

column3:
ananyacetāḥ(s) satataṃ(y̐), yo māṃ(m) smarati nityaśaḥ।
tasyāhaṃ(m) sulabhaḥ(ph) pārtha, nityayuktasya yoginaḥ॥8.14॥
column4: Arjuna, whosoever always and constantly thinks of Me with undivided mind, to that Yogi ever absorbed in Me I am easily attainable.
column5: Arjuna, whosoever always and constantly thinks of Me with undivided mind, to that Yogi ever absorbed in Me I am easily attainable.

column3:
māmupetya  punarjanma,  duḥkhālayamaśāśvatam।
nāpnuvanti mahātmānaḥ(s), saṃsiddhiṃ(m) paramāṃ(ṅ) gatāḥ॥8.15॥
column4: Great souls, who have attained the highest perfection, having come to Me, are no more subject to transitory rebirth, which is the abode of sorrow, and transient by nature.
column5: Great souls, who have attained the highest perfection, having come to Me, are no more subject to transitory rebirth, which is the abode of sorrow, and transient by nature.

column3:
ābrahmabhuvanāllokāḥ(ph),   punarāvartino'rjuna।
māmupetya  tu  kaunteya,  punarjanma  na  vidyate॥8.16॥
column4: Arjuna, all the worlds from Brahmaloka (the heavenly realm of the Creator, Brahma) downwards are liable to birth and rebirth. But, O son of Kunti, on attaining Me there is no rebirth (For, while I am beyond Time, regions like Brahmaloka, being conditioned by time, are transitory).
column5: Arjuna, all the worlds from Brahmaloka (the heavenly realm of the Creator, Brahma) downwards are liable to birth and rebirth. But, O son of Kunti, on attaining Me there is no rebirth (For, while I am beyond Time, regions like Brahmaloka, being conditioned by time, are transitory).

column3:
sahasrayugaparyantam,   aharyadbrahmaṇo   viduḥ।
rātriṃ(y̐)  yugasahasrāntāṃ(n),  te'horātravido  janāḥ॥8.17॥
column4: Those Yogis, who know from realization Brahma's day as covering a thousand Mahayugas, and so his night as extending to another thousand Mahayugas, know the reality about Time.
column5: Those Yogis, who know from realization Brahma's day as covering a thousand Mahayugas, and so his night as extending to another thousand Mahayugas, know the reality about Time.

column3:
avyaktādvyaktayaḥ(s) sarvāḥ(ph), prabhavantyaharāgame।
rātryāgame      pralīyante,    tatraivāvyaktasañjñake॥8.18॥
column4: All embodied beings emanate from the Unmanifest (i.e., Brahma's subtle body) at the coming of the cosmic day; at the cosmic nightfall they merge into the same subtle body of Brahma, known as the Unmanifest.
column5: All embodied beings emanate from the Unmanifest (i.e., Brahma's subtle body) at the coming of the cosmic day; at the cosmic nightfall they merge into the same subtle body of Brahma, known as the Unmanifest.

column3:
bhūtagrāmaḥ(s) sa evāyaṃ(m), bhūtvā bhūtvā pralīyate।
rātryāgame'vaśaḥ(ph) pārtha, prabhavatyaharāgame॥8.19॥
column4: Arjuna, this multitude of beings, being born again and again, is dissolved under compulsion of its nature at the coming of the cosmic night and rises again at the commencement of cosmic day.
column5: Arjuna, this multitude of beings, being born again and again, is dissolved under compulsion of its nature at the coming of the cosmic night and rises again at the commencement of cosmic day.

column3:
parastasmāttu      bhāvo'nyo-'vyakto'vyaktātsanātanaḥ।
yaḥ(s) sa  sarveṣu  bhūteṣu,  naśyatsu na vinaśyati॥8.20॥
column4: Far beyond even this unmanifest, there is yet another unmanifest Existence, that Supreme Divine Person, who does not perish even though all beings perish.
column5: Far beyond even this unmanifest, there is yet another unmanifest Existence, that Supreme Divine Person, who does not perish even though all beings perish.

column3:
avyakto'kṣara ityuktaḥ(s), tamāhuḥ(ph) paramāṃ(ṅ) gatim।
yaṃ(m) prāpya na nivartante, taddhāma paramaṃ(m) mama॥8.21॥
column4: The same unmanifest which has been spoken of as the Indestructible, is also called the supreme goal; that again is My supreme Abode, attaining which they return not to this mortal world.
column5: The same unmanifest which has been spoken of as the Indestructible, is also called the supreme goal; that again is My supreme Abode, attaining which they return not to this mortal world.

column3:
puruṣaḥ(s) sa paraḥ(ph) pārtha, bhaktyā labhyastvananyayā।
yasyāntaḥsthāni bhūtāni, yena sarvamidaṃ(n) tatam॥8.22॥
column4: Arjuna, that eternal unmanifest supreme Puruṣa in whom all beings reside and by whom all this is pervaded, is attainable only through exclusive Devotion.
column5: Arjuna, that eternal unmanifest supreme Puruṣa in whom all beings reside and by whom all this is pervaded, is attainable only through exclusive Devotion.

column3:
yatra     kāle    tvanāvṛttim,    āvṛttiṃ(ñ)   caiva    yoginaḥ।
prayātā yānti taṃ(ṅ) kālaṃ(v̐), vakṣyāmi bharatarṣabha॥8.23॥
column4: Arjuna, I shall now tell you the time (path) departing when Yogis do not return, and also the time (path) departing when they do return.
column5: Arjuna, I shall now tell you the time (path) departing when Yogis do not return, and also the time (path) departing when they do return

column3:
agnirjyotirahaḥ(ś) śuklaḥ(ṣ), ṣaṇmāsā uttarāyaṇam।
tatra prayātā gacchanti, brahma brahmavido janāḥ॥8.24॥
column4: (Of the two paths) the one is that in which, are stationed the all-effulgent fire-god and the deities presiding over daylight, the bright fortnight, and the six months of the northward course of the sun respectively; proceeding along it after death, Yogīs who have known Brahma, being successively by the above gods, finally reach Brahma.
column5: (Of the two paths) the one is that in which, are stationed the all-effulgent fire-god and the deities presiding over daylight, the bright fortnight, and the six months of the northward course of the sun respectively; proceeding along it after death, Yogīs who have known Brahma, being successively by the above gods, finally reach Brahma.

column3:
dhūmo rātristathā kṛṣṇaḥ(ṣ), ṣaṇmāsā dakṣiṇāyanam।
tatra cāndramasaṃ(ñ) jyotiḥ(r), yogī prāpya nivartate॥8.25॥
column4: The other path is that wherein are stationed the gods presiding over smoke, night, the dark fortnight, and the six months of the southward course of the sun; the Yogi (devoted to action with an interested motive) taking to this path after death is led by the above gods, one after another, and attaining the lustre of the moon (and enjoying the fruit of his meritorious deeds in heaven) returns to this mortal world.
column5: The other path is that wherein are stationed the gods presiding over smoke, night, the dark fortnight, and the six months of the southward course of the sun; the Yogi (devoted to action with an interested motive) taking to this path after death is led by the above gods, one after another, and attaining the lustre of the moon (and enjoying the fruit of his meritorious deeds in heaven) returns to this mortal world

column3:
śuklakṛṣṇe  gatī  hyete,  jagataḥ(ś) śāśvate mate।
ekayā    yātyanāvṛttim,    anyayāvartate   punaḥ॥8.26॥
column4: For these two paths of the world, the bright and the dark, are considered to be eternal. Proceeding by one of them, one reaches the supreme state from which there is no return; and proceeding by the other, one returns to the mortal world, i.e., becomes subject to birth and death once more.
column5: For these two paths of the world, the bright and the dark, are considered to be eternal. Proceeding by one of them, one reaches the supreme state from which there is no return; and proceeding by the other, one returns to the mortal world, i.e., becomes subject to birth and death once more.

column3:
naite  sṛtī  pārtha  jānan,  yogī muhyati kaścana।
tasmātsarveṣu   kāleṣu,   yogayukto  bhavārjuna॥8.27॥
column4: Knowing thus the secret of these two paths, O son of Kunti, no Yogī gets deluded. Therefore, Arjuna, at all times be steadfast in Yoga in the form of equanimity (i.e., strive constantly for My realization).
column5: Knowing thus the secret of these two paths, O son of Kunti, no Yogī gets deluded. Therefore, Arjuna, at all times be steadfast in Yoga in the form of equanimity (i.e., strive constantly for My realization).

column3:
vedeṣu   yajñeṣu   tapaḥ(s)   su  caiva,dāneṣu   yatpuṇyaphalaṃ(m)   pradiṣṭam।
atyeti      tatsarvamidaṃ(v̐)      viditvā,yogī   paraṃ(m)   sthānamupaiti  cādyam॥8.28॥
column4: The Yogi, realizing this profound truth, doubtless transcends all the rewards enumerated for the study of the Vedas as well as for performance of sacrifices, austerities and charities and attains the supreme and primal state.
column5: The Yogi, realizing this profound truth, doubtless transcends all the rewards enumerated for the study of the Vedas as well as for performance of sacrifices, austerities and charities and attains the supreme and primal state.

column3:
Om tatsaditi śrīmadbhagavadgītāsu upaniṣatsu brahmavidyāyāṃ(y̐) yogaśāstre śrīkṛṣṇārjunasaṃvāde akṣarabrahmayogo nāmaaṣṭamo'dhyāyaḥ || 

|| OM ŚRĪKṚṢṆĀRPAṆAMASTU ||
column4: Thus, in the Upanişad sung by the Lord, the Science of Brahma, the scripture of Yoga, the dialogue between Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna, ends the eighth chapter entitled "The Yoga of the Indestructible Brahma."
column5: Thus, in the Upanişad sung by the Lord, the Science of Brahma, the scripture of Yoga, the dialogue between Śrī Kṛṣṇa and Arjuna, ends the eighth chapter entitled "The Yoga of the Indestructible Brahma."

column3:
om śrīparamātmane namaḥ
śrīmadbhagavadgītā
atha navamo'dhyāyaḥ

śrībhagavānuvāca 
idaṃ(n) tu te guhyatamaṃ(m), pravakṣyāmyanasūyave।
jñānaṃ(v̐) vijñānasahitaṃ(y̐), yajjñātvā mokṣyase'śubhāt॥9.1॥
column4:
Śrī Bhagavān said : 
To you, who are devoid of the carping spirit, I shall now unfold the most secret knowledge of Nirguņa Brahma along with the knowledge of manifest Divinity, knowing which you shall be free from the evil of worldly existence.
column5: Śrī Bhagavān said : To you, who are devoid of the carping spirit, I shall now unfold the most secret knowledge of Nirguņa Brahma along with the knowledge of manifest Divinity, knowing which you shall be free from the evil of worldly existence.

column3:
rājavidyā     rājaguhyaṃ(m),   pavitramidamuttamam।
pratyakṣāvagamaṃ(n) dharmyaṃ(m), susukhaṃ(ṅ) kartumavyayam॥9.2॥
column4: This knowledge (of both the Nirguņa and Saguņa aspects of Divinity) is a sovereign secret, supremely holy, most excellent, directly enjoyable, attended with virtue, very easy to practice and imperishable.
column5: This knowledge (of both the Nirguņa and Saguņa aspects of Divinity) is a sovereign secret, supremely holy, most excellent, directly enjoyable, attended with virtue, very easy to practice and imperishable.

column3:
aśraddadhānāḥ(ph) puruṣā, dharmasyāsya parantapa।
aprāpya māṃ(n) nivartante, mṛtyusaṃsāravartmani॥9.3॥
column4: Arjuna, people having no faith in this Dharma, failing to reach Me, continue to revolve in the path of the world of birth and death.
column5: Arjuna, people having no faith in this Dharma, failing to reach Me, continue to revolve in the path of the world of birth and death.

column3:
mayā tatamidaṃ(m) sarvaṃ(ñ), jagadavyaktamūrtinā।
matsthāni sarvabhūtāni, na cāhaṃ(n) teṣvavasthitaḥ॥9.4॥
column4: The whole of this universe is permeated by Me as unmanifest Divinity, like ice by water and all beings dwell on the idea within Me. But, really speaking, I am not present in them.
column5: The whole of this universe is permeated by Me as unmanifest Divinity, like ice by water and all beings dwell on the idea within Me. But, really speaking, I am not present in them.

column3:
na ca matsthāni bhūtāni, paśya me yogamaiśvaram,
bhūtabhṛnna ca bhūtastho, mamātmā bhūtabhāvanaḥ॥9.5॥
column4: Nay, all those beings abide not in Me; but behold the wonderful power of My divine Yoga; though the Sustainer and Creator of beings, Myself in reality dwell not in those beings.
column5: Nay, all those beings abide not in Me; but behold the wonderful power of My divine Yoga; though the Sustainer and Creator of beings, Myself in reality dwell not in those beings.

column3:
yathākāśasthito nityaṃ(v̐), vāyuḥ(s) sarvatrago mahān।
tathā   sarvāṇi   bhūtāni,   matsthānītyupadhāraya॥9.6॥
column4: Just as the extensive air, which is moving everywhere, (being born of ether) ever remains in ether, likewise, know that all beings, who have originated from My Saṅkalpa, abide in Me.
column5: Just as the extensive air, which is moving everywhere, (being born of ether) ever remains in ether, likewise, know that all beings, who have originated from My Saṅkalpa, abide in Me.

column3:
sarvabhūtāni kaunteya,   prakṛtiṃ(y̐) yānti māmikām।
kalpakṣaye   punastāni,   kalpādau   visṛjāmyaham॥9.7॥
column4: Arjuna, during the Final Dissolution all beings enter My Prakṛti (the prime cause), and at the beginning of creation, I send them forth again.
column5: Arjuna, during the Final Dissolution all beings enter My Prakṛti (the prime cause), and at the beginning of creation, I send them forth again.

column3:
prakṛtiṃ(m) svāmavaṣṭabhya, visṛjāmi punaḥ(ph) punaḥ।
bhūtagrāmamimaṃ(ṅ) kṛtsnam, avaśaṃ(m) prakṛtervaśāt॥9.8॥
column4: Wielding My nature I procreate again and again, according to their respective Karmas, all this multitude of beings subject to the sway of their own nature.
column5: Wielding My nature I procreate again and again, according to their respective Karmas, all this multitude of beings subject to the sway of their own nature.

column3:
na ca māṃ(n) tāni karmāṇi, nibadhnanti dhanañjaya।
udāsīnavadāsīnam,   asaktaṃ(n)   teṣu   karmasu॥9.9॥
column4: Arjuna, those actions, however, do not bind Me, unattached as I am to such actions, and standing apart as it were.
column5: Arjuna, those actions, however, do not bind Me, unattached as I am to such actions, and standing apart as it were.

column3:
mayādhyakṣeṇa   prakṛtiḥ(s),  sūyate sacarācaram।
hetunānena     kaunteya,     jagadviparivartate॥9.10॥
column4: Arjuna, under My aegis, Nature brings forth the whole creation, consisting of both sentient and insentient beings; it is due to this cause that the wheel of ṣamsara is going round.
column5: Arjuna, under My aegis, Nature brings forth the whole creation, consisting of both sentient and insentient beings; it is due to this cause that the wheel of ṣamsara is going round.

column3:
avajānanti māṃ(m) mūḍhā, mānuṣīṃ(n) tanumāśritam।
paraṃ(m)  bhāvamajānanto,  mama  bhūtamaheśvaram॥9.11॥
column4: Not Knowing My supreme nature, fools deride Me, the overlord of the entire creation, who have assumed the human form. That is to say, they take Me, who have appeared in human form through My 'Yogamaya' for deliverance of the world, as an ordinary mortal.
column5: Not Knowing My supreme nature, fools deride Me, the overlord of the entire creation, who have assumed the human form. That is to say, they take Me, who have appeared in human form through My 'Yogamaya' for deliverance of the world, as an ordinary mortal.

column3:
moghāśā  moghakarmāṇo,  moghajñānā  vicetasaḥ।
rākṣasīmāsurīṃ(ñ) caiva, prakṛtiṃ(m) mohinīṃ(m) śritāḥ॥9.12॥
column4: Those bewildered persons with vain hopes, futile actions and fruitless knowledge, have embraced a fiendish, demoniacal and delusive nature.
column5: Those bewildered persons with vain hopes, futile actions and fruitless knowledge, have embraced a fiendish, demoniacal and delusive nature.

column3:
mahātmānastu māṃ(m) pārtha, daivīṃ(m) prakṛtimāśritāḥ।
bhajantyananyamanaso,  jñātvā  bhūtādimavyayam॥9.13॥
column4: On the other hand, Arjuna, great souls who have adopted the divine nature, knowing Me as the prime source of all beings and the imperishable eternal, worship Me constantly with one pointedness of mind.
column5: On the other hand, Arjuna, great souls who have adopted the divine nature, knowing Me as the prime source of all beings and the imperishable eternal, worship Me constantly with one pointedness of mind.

column3:
satataṃ(ṅ) kīrtayanto māṃ(y̐), yatantaśca dṛḍhavratāḥ।
namasyantaśca māṃ(m) bhaktyā, nityayuktā upāsate॥9.14॥
column4: Constantly chanting My names and glories and striving for My realization, and bowing again and again to Me, those devotees of firm resolve, ever united with me through meditation, worship Me with single-minded devotion.
column5: Constantly chanting My names and glories and striving for My realization, and bowing again and again to Me, those devotees of firm resolve, ever united with me through meditation, worship Me with single-minded devotion.

column3:
jñānayajñena   cāpyanye,   yajanto    māmupāsate।
ekatvena  pṛthaktvena,  bahudhā  viśvatomukham॥9.15॥
column4: Others, who follow the path of Knowledge, betake themselves to Me through yajña of Knowledge, worshipping Me in My absolute, formless aspect as one with themselves; while still others worship Me in My Universal Form in many ways, taking Me to be diverse in manifold celestial forms.
column5: Others, who follow the path of Knowledge, betake themselves to Me through yajña of Knowledge, worshipping Me in My absolute, formless aspect as one with themselves; while still others worship Me in My Universal Form in many ways, taking Me to be diverse in manifold celestial forms.

column3:
ahaṃ(ṅ) kraturahaṃ(y̐) yajñaḥ(s), svadhāhamahamauṣadham।
mantro'hamahamevājyam, ahamagnirahaṃ(m) hutam॥9.16॥
column4: I am the Vedic ritual, I am the sacrifice, I am the offering to the departed; I am the herbage and food grains; I am the sacred mantra, I am the clarified butter, I am the sacred fire, and I am verily the act of offering oblations into the fire.
