column5: I am the Vedic ritual, I am the sacrifice, I am the offering to the departed; I am the herbage and food grains; I am the sacred mantra, I am the clarified butter, I am the sacred fire, and I am verily the act of offering oblations into the fire.

column3:
pitāhamasya  jagato,  mātā   dhātā  pitāmahaḥ।
vedyaṃ(m)   pavitramoṅkāra,  ṛksāma  yajureva  ca॥9.17॥
column4: I am the sustainer and ruler of this universe, its father, mother and grandfather, the one worth knowing, the purifier, the sacred syllable OM, and the three Vedas - Ṛig, Yajuṣ and ṣama.
column5: I am the sustainer and ruler of this universe, its father, mother and grandfather, the one worth knowing, the purifier, the sacred syllable OM, and the three Vedas - Ṛig, Yajuṣ and ṣama.

column3:
gatirbhartā prabhuḥ(s) sākṣī, nivāsaḥ(ś) śaraṇaṃ(m) suhṛt।
prabhavaḥ(ph) pralayaḥ(s) sthānaṃ(n), nidhānaṃ(m) bījamavyayam॥9.18॥
column4: I am the supreme goal, sustainer, lord, witness, abode, refuge, well-wisher seeking no return, origin and end, resting-place, store-house to which all beings return at the time of universal destruction, and the imperishable seed.
column5: I am the supreme goal, sustainer, lord, witness, abode, refuge, well-wisher seeking no return, origin and end, resting-place, store-house to which all beings return at the time of universal destruction, and the imperishable seed.

column3:
tapāmyahamahaṃ(v̐)  varṣaṃ(n),  nigṛhṇāmyutsṛjāmi ca।
amṛtaṃ(ñ)  caiva  mṛtyuśca,  sadasaccāhamarjuna॥9.19॥
column4: I radiate heat as the sun, and hold back as well as send forth showers, Arjuna. I am immortality as well as death; even so, I am being and also non-being.
column5: I radiate heat as the sun, and hold back as well as send forth showers, Arjuna. I am immortality as well as death; even so, I am being and also non-being.

column3:
traividyā  māṃ(m)  somapāḥ(ph)  pūtapāpā,
yajñairiṣṭvā   svargatiṃ(m)  prārthayante।
te      puṇyamāsādya       surendralokam,
aśnanti         divyāndivi        devabhogān॥9.20॥
column4: Those who perform action with some interested motive as laid down in these three Vedas and drink the sap of the Soma plant, and have thus been purged of sin, worshipping Me through sacrifices, seek access to heaven; attaining Indra's paradise as the result of their virtuous deeds, they enjoy the celestial pleasures of gods in heaven.
column5: Those who perform action with some interested motive as laid down in these three Vedas and drink the sap of the Soma plant, and have thus been purged of sin, worshipping Me through sacrifices, seek access to heaven; attaining Indra's paradise as the result of their virtuous deeds, they enjoy the celestial pleasures of gods in heaven.

column3:
te taṃ(m) bhuktvā svargalokaṃ(v̐) viśālaṃ(ṅ),
kṣīṇe puṇye martyalokaṃ(v̐) viśanti।
evaṃ(n) trayīdharmamanuprapannā,
gatāgataṃ(ṅ)   kāmakāmā   labhante॥9.21॥
column4: Having enjoyed the extensive heaven-world, they return to this world of mortals on the stock of their merits being exhausted. Thus devoted to the ritual with interested motive, recommended by the three Vedas as the means of attaining heavenly bliss, and seeking worldly enjoyments, they repeatedly come and go (i.e., ascend to heaven by virtue of their merits and return to earth when their fruit has been enjoyed).
column5: Having enjoyed the extensive heaven-world, they return to this world of mortals on the stock of their merits being exhausted. Thus devoted to the ritual with interested motive, recommended by the three Vedas as the means of attaining heavenly bliss, and seeking worldly enjoyments, they repeatedly come and go (i.e., ascend to heaven by virtue of their merits and return to earth when their fruit has been enjoyed).

column3:
ananyāścintayanto māṃ(y̐), ye janāḥ(ph) paryupāsate।
teṣāṃ(n) nityābhiyuktānāṃ(y̐), yogakṣemaṃ(v̐) vahāmyaham॥9.22॥
column4: The devotees, however, who loving no one else constantly think of Me, and worship Me in a disinterested spirit, to those ever united in thought with Me, I bring full security and personally attend to their needs.
column5: The devotees, however, who loving no one else constantly think of Me, and worship Me in a disinterested spirit, to those ever united in thought with Me, I bring full security and personally attend to their needs.

column3:
ye'pyanyadevatā bhaktā,  yajante  śraddhayānvitāḥ।
te'pi māmeva  kaunteya,   yajantyavidhipūrvakam॥9.23॥
column4: Arjuna, even those devotees who, endowed with faith, worship other gods (with some interested motive) worship Me alone, though with a mistaken approach.
column5: Arjuna, even those devotees who, endowed with faith, worship other gods (with some interested motive) worship Me alone, though with a mistaken approach.

column3:
ahaṃ(m) hi sarvayajñānāṃ(m), bhoktā ca prabhureva ca।
na  tu  māmabhijānanti,  tattvenātaścyavanti  te॥9.24॥
column4: For, I am the enjoyer and also the lord of all sacrifices; but they who do not know Me in reality as the Supreme Deity, they fall i.e., return to life on earth.
column5: For, I am the enjoyer and also the lord of all sacrifices; but they who do not know Me in reality as the Supreme Deity, they fall i.e., return to life on earth.

column3:
yānti  devavratā   devān,  pitṝnyānti  pitṛvratāḥ।
bhūtāni  yānti  bhūtejyā, yānti  madyājino'pi  mām॥9.25॥
column4: Those who are votaries of gods, go to gods, those who are votaries of manes, reach the manes; those who adore the spirits, reach the spirits and those who worship Me, come to Me alone.
column5: Those who are votaries of gods, go to gods, those who are votaries of manes, reach the manes; those who adore the spirits, reach the spirits and those who worship Me, come to Me alone.

column3:
patraṃ(m) puṣpaṃ(m) phalaṃ(n) toyaṃ(y̐), yo me bhaktyā prayacchati।
tadahaṃ(m)  bhaktyupahṛtam,  aśnāmi  prayatātmanaḥ॥9.26॥
column4: Whosoever offers Me with love a leaf, a flower, a fruit or water, I, appear in person before that selfless devotee of sinless mind, and delightfully partake of that article offered by him with love.
column5: Whosoever offers Me with love a leaf, a flower, a fruit or water, I, appear in person before that selfless devotee of sinless mind, and delightfully partake of that article offered by him with love.

column3:
yatkaroṣi     yadaśnāsi,  yajjuhoṣi  dadāsi   yat।
yattapasyasi  kaunteya,  tatkuruṣva  madarpaṇam॥9.27॥
column4: Arjuna, whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer as oblation to the sacred fire, whatever you bestow as a gift, whatever you do by way of penance, offer all that to Me.
column5: Arjuna, whatever you do, whatever you eat, whatever you offer as oblation to the sacred fire, whatever you bestow as a gift, whatever you do by way of penance, offer all that to Me.

column3:
śubhāśubhaphalairevaṃ(m), mokṣyase karmabandhanaiḥ।
sannyāsayogayuktātmā,   vimukto   māmupaiṣyasi॥9.28॥
column4: With your mind thus established in the Yoga of renunciation (offering of all actions to Me), you will be freed from the bondage of action in the form of good and evil results; thus freed from them, you will attain Me.
column5: With your mind thus established in the Yoga of renunciation (offering of all actions to Me), you will be freed from the bondage of action in the form of good and evil results; thus freed from them, you will attain Me.

column3:
samo'haṃ(m) sarvabhūteṣu, na me dveṣyo'sti na priyaḥ।
ye bhajanti tu māṃ(m) bhaktyā, mayi te teṣu cāpyaham॥9.29॥
column4: I am equally present in all beings; there is none hateful or dear to Me. They, however, who devoutly worship Me abide in Me; and I too stand revealed to them.
column5: I am equally present in all beings; there is none hateful or dear to Me. They, however, who devoutly worship Me abide in Me; and I too stand revealed to them.

column3:
api   cetsudurācāro,   bhajate  māmananyabhāk।
sādhureva sa mantavyaḥ(s), samyagvyavasito hi saḥ॥9.30॥
column4: Even if the vilest sinner worships Me with exclusive devotion, he should be regarded a saint; for, he has rightly resolved. (He is positive in his belief that there is nothing like devoted worship of God).
column5: Even if the vilest sinner worships Me with exclusive devotion, he should be regarded a saint; for, he has rightly resolved. (He is positive in his belief that there is nothing like devoted worship of God).

column3:
kṣipraṃ(m) bhavati dharmātmā, śaśvacchāntiṃ(n) nigacchati।
kaunteya pratijānīhi, na me bhaktaḥ(ph) praṇaśyati॥9.31॥
column4: Speedily he becomes virtuous and attains abiding peace. Know it for certain, Arjuna, that My devotee never perishes.
column5: Speedily he becomes virtuous and attains abiding peace. Know it for certain, Arjuna, that My devotee never perishes.

column3:
māṃ(m) hi pārtha vyapāśritya, ye'pi syuḥ(ph) pāpayonayaḥ।
striyo vaiśyāstathā śūdrā(s), te'pi yānti parāṃ(ṅ) gatim॥9.32॥
column4: Arjuna, women, Vaiśyās (members of the trading and agriculturist classes), śūdrās (those belonging to the labour and artisan classes), as well as those of impious birth (such as the pariah), whoever they may be, taking refuge in Me, they too attain the supreme goal.
column5: Arjuna, women, Vaiśyās (members of the trading and agriculturist classes), śūdrās (those belonging to the labour and artisan classes), as well as those of impious birth (such as the pariah), whoever they may be, taking refuge in Me, they too attain the supreme goal.

column3:
kiṃ(m) punarbrāhmaṇāḥ(ph) puṇyā, bhaktā rājarṣayastathā।
anityamasukhaṃ(l̐) lokam, imaṃ(m) prāpya bhajasva mām॥9.33॥
column4: How much more, then, if they be holy Brahmaņas and royal sages devoted to Me! Therefore, having obtained this joyless and transient human life, constantly worship Me.
column5: How much more, then, if they be holy Brahmaņas and royal sages devoted to Me! Therefore, having obtained this joyless and transient human life, constantly worship Me.

column3:
manmanā bhava madbhakto, madyājī māṃ(n) namaskuru।
māmevaiṣyasi yuktvaivam, ātmānaṃ(m) matparāyaṇaḥ॥9.34॥
column4: Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, worship Me and make obeisance to Me; thus linking yourself with Me and entirely depending on Me, you shall come to Me.
column5: Fix your mind on Me, be devoted to Me, worship Me and make obeisance to Me; thus linking yourself with Me and entirely depending on Me, you shall come to Me.

column3:
om tatsaditi śrīmadbhagavadgītāsu upaniṣatsu
brahmavidyāyāṃ(y̐) yogaśāstre śrīkṛṣṇārjunasaṃvāde
rājavidyārājaguhyayogo nāma navamo'dhyāyaḥ॥
॥OM ŚRĪKṚṢṆĀRPAṆAMASTU॥

om śrīparamātmane namaḥ
śrīmadbhagavadgītā
atha daśamo'dhyāyaḥ

śrībhagavānuvāca
bhūya eva mahābāho, śṛṇu me paramaṃ(v̐) vacaḥ।
yatte'haṃ(m)  prīyamāṇāya,  vakṣyāmi  hitakāmyayā॥10.1॥
column4:
Śrī Bhagavān said : 
Arjuna, hear once again My supreme word, which I shall speak to you,
who are so loving, out of solicitude for your welfare.
column5: Śrī Bhagavān said : Arjuna, hear once again My supreme word, which I shall speak to you, who are so loving, out of solicitude for your welfare.

column3:
na me viduḥ(s) suragaṇāḥ(ph), prabhavaṃ(n) na maharṣayaḥ।
ahamādirhi devānāṃ(m), maharṣīṇāṃ(ñ) ca sarvaśaḥ॥10.2॥
column4: Neither gods nor the great sages know the secret of My birth (i.e., My manifestation in humanor other forms out of mere sport); for, I am the prime cause in all respects of gods as well as ofthe great seers.
column5: Neither gods nor the great sages know the secret of My birth (i.e., My manifestation in human or other forms out of mere sport); for, I am the prime cause in all respects of gods as well as of the great seers.

column3:
yo māmajamanādiṃ(ñ) ca, vetti lokamaheśvaram।
asaṃmūḍhaḥ(s) sa martyeṣu, sarvapāpaiḥ(ph) pramucyate॥10.3॥
column4: He who knows Me in reality as birthless and without beginning, and as the supreme Lord of the Universe, he, undeluded among men, is purged of all sins.
column5: He who knows Me in reality as birthless and without beginning, and as the supreme Lord of the Universe, he, undeluded among men, is purged of all sins.

column3:
buddhirjñānamasammohaḥ, kṣamā satyaṃ(n) damaḥ(ś) śamaḥ।
sukhaṃ(n) duḥkhaṃ(m) bhavo'bhāvo, bhayaṃ(ñ) cābhayameva ca॥10.4॥
column4: Reason, right knowledge, unclouded understanding, forbearance, veracity, control over the senses and mind, joy and sorrow, evolution and dissolution, fear and fearlessness,
column5: Reason, right knowledge, unclouded understanding, forbearance, veracity, control over the senses and mind, joy and sorrow, evolution and dissolution, fear and fearlessness,

column3:
ahiṃsā samatā tuṣṭiḥ(s), tapo dānaṃ(y̐) yaśo'yaśaḥ।
bhavanti bhāvā bhūtānāṃ(m), matta eva pṛthagvidhāḥ॥10.5॥
column4: non-violence, equanimity, contentment, austerity, charity, fame and obloquy-these diverse traits of creatures emanate from Me alone.
column5: non-violence, equanimity, contentment, austerity, charity, fame and obloquy- these diverse traits of creatures emanate from Me alone.

column3:
maharṣayaḥ(s) sapta pūrve, catvāro manavastathā।
madbhāvā mānasā jātā, yeṣāṃ(l̐) loka imāḥ(ph) prajāḥ॥10.6॥
column4: The seven great seers, their four elders (Sanaka and others), and the fourteen Manus or progenitors of mankind (such as Svayambhuva and his successors), who were all devoted to Me, were born of My will; from them all these creatures in the world have descended.
column5: The seven great seers, their four elders (Sanaka and others), and the fourteen Manus or progenitors of mankind (such as Svayambhuva and his successors), who were all devoted to Me, were born of My will; from them all these creatures in the world have descended.

column3:
etāṃ(v̐) vibhūtiṃ(y̐) yogaṃ(ñ) ca, mama yo vetti tattvataḥ।
so'vikampena  yogena,  yujyate  nātra  saṃśayaḥ॥10.7॥
column4: He who knows in reality this supreme divine glory and supernatural power of Mine, gets established in Me through unfaltering devotion; of this there is no doubt.
column5: He who knows in reality this supreme divine glory and supernatural power of Mine, gets established in Me through unfaltering devotion; of this there is no doubt.

column3:
ahaṃ(m) sarvasya prabhavo, mattaḥ(s) sarvaṃ(m) pravartate।
iti matvā bhajante māṃ(m), budhā bhāvasamanvitāḥ॥10.8॥
column4: I am the source of all creation and everything in the world moves because of Me; knowing thus, the wise, full of devotion, constantly worship Me.
column5: I am the source of all creation and everything in the world moves because of Me; knowing thus, the wise, full of devotion, constantly worship Me.

column3:
maccittā madgataprāṇā, bodhayantaḥ(ph) parasparam।
kathayantaśca māṃ(n) nityaṃ(n), tuṣyanti ca ramanti ca॥10.9॥
column4: With their minds fixed on Me, and their lives surrendered to Me, conversing and enlightening one another about My glories, My devotees ever remain contented and take delight in Me.
column5: With their minds fixed on Me, and their lives surrendered to Me, conversing and enlightening one another about My glories, My devotees ever remain contented and take delight in Me.

column3:
teṣāṃ(m) satatayuktānāṃ(m), bhajatāṃ(m) prītipūrvakam।
dadāmi buddhiyogaṃ(n) taṃ(y̐), yena māmupayānti te॥10.10॥
column4: On those ever united through meditation with Me and worshipping Me with love, I confer that Yoga of wisdom by which they come to Me.
column5: On those ever united through meditation with Me and worshipping Me with love, I confer that Yoga of wisdom by which they come to Me.

column3:
teṣāmevānukampārtham, ahamajñānajaṃ(n) tamaḥ।
nāśayāmyātmabhāvastho,   jñānadīpena  bhāsvatā॥10.11॥
column4: In order to bestow My compassion on them, I, dwelling in their hearts, dispel their darkness born of ignorance by the illuminating lamp of knowledge.
column5: In order to bestow My compassion on them, I, dwelling in their hearts, dispel their darkness born of ignorance by the illuminating lamp of knowledge.

column3:
arjuna uvāca
paraṃ(m) brahma paraṃ(n) dhāma, pavitraṃ(m) paramaṃ(m) bhavān।
puruṣaṃ(m) śāśvataṃ(n) divyam, ādidevamajaṃ(v̐) vibhum॥10.12॥
column4: Arjuna said: You are the transcendent Eternal, the supreme Abode and the greatest purifier; all the seers speak of You as the eternal divine Puruṣa, the primal Deity, unborn and all-pervading.
column5: Arjuna said: You are the transcendent Eternal, the supreme Abode and the greatest purifier; all the seers speak of You as the eternal divine Puruṣa, the primal Deity, unborn and all-pervading.

column3:
āhustvāmṛṣayaḥ(s)   sarve,  devarṣirnāradastathā।
asito devalo vyāsaḥ(s), svayaṃ(ñ) caiva bravīṣi me॥10.13॥
column4: Likewise speak the celestial sage Nārada, the sages Asita and Devala and the great sage vyāsa; and Yourself too proclaim this to me.
column5: Likewise speak the celestial sage Nārada, the sages Asita and Devala and the great sage Vyāsa and Yourself too proclaim this to me.

column3:
sarvametadṛtaṃ(m) manye, yanmāṃ(v̐) vadasi keśava।
na hi te bhagavanvyaktiṃ(v̐), vidurdevā na dānavāḥ॥10.14॥
column4: Kṛṣṇā, I believe as true all that You tell me. Lord, neither demons nor gods are aware of Your manifestations.
column5: Kṛṣṇa, I believe as true all that You tell me. Lord, neither demons nor gods are aware of Your manifestations.

column3:
svayamevātmanātmānaṃ(v̐), vettha tvaṃ(m) puruṣottama।
bhūtabhāvana   bhūteśa,   devadeva   jagatpate॥10.15॥
column4: O Creator of beings, O Ruler of creatures, god of gods, the Lord of the universe, O supreme Puruṣa, You alone know what You are by Yourself.
column5: O Creator of beings, O Ruler of creatures, god of gods, the Lord of the universe, O supreme Puruṣa, You alone know what You are by Yourself.

column3:
vaktumarhasyaśeṣeṇa,   divyā  hyātmavibhūtayaḥ।
yābhirvibhūtibhirlokān, imāṃstvaṃ(v̐) vyāpya tiṣṭhasi॥10.16॥
column4: Therefore, You alone can describe in full Your divine glories, whereby You pervade all these worlds.
column5: Therefore, You alone can describe in full Your divine glories, whereby You pervade all these worlds.

column3:
kathaṃ(v̐) vidyāmahaṃ(y̐) yogiṃs, tvāṃ(m) sadā paricintayan।
keṣu  keṣu  ca  bhāveṣu,  cintyo'si  bhagavanmayā॥10.17॥
column4: O Master of Yoga, through what process of continuous meditation shall I know You? And in what particular forms, O Lord, are You to be meditated upon by me?
column5: O Master of Yoga, through what process of continuous meditation shall I know You? And in what particular forms, O Lord, are You to be meditated upon by me?

column3:
vistareṇātmano  yogaṃ(v̐),  vibhūtiṃ(ñ)  ca janārdana।
bhūyaḥ(kh) kathaya tṛptirhi, śṛṇvato nāsti me'mṛtam॥10.18॥
column4: Kṛṣṇā, tell me once more in detail Your power of Yoga and Your glory; for I know no satiety in hearing Your nectar-like words.
column5: Kṛṣṇa,, tell me once more in detail Your power of Yoga and Your glory; for I know no satiety in hearing Your nectar-like words.

column3:
śrībhagavānuvāca
hanta  te  kathayiṣyāmi,  divyā  hyātmavibhūtayaḥ।
prādhānyataḥ(kh) kuruśreṣṭha, nāstyanto vistarasya me॥10.19॥
column4: Śrī Bhagavān said: Arjuna, now I shall tell you My prominent divine glories; for there is no limit to My manifestations.
column5: Śrī Bhagavān said: Arjuna, now I shall tell you My prominent divine glories; for there is no limit to My manifestations.

column3:
ahamātmā     guḍākeśa,    sarvabhūtāśayasthitaḥ।
ahamādiśca madhyaṃ(ñ) ca, bhūtānāmanta eva ca॥10.20॥
column4: Arjuna, I am the universal Self seated in the hearts of all beings; so, I alone am the beginning, the middle and also the end of all beings.
column5: Arjuna, I am the universal Self seated in the hearts of all beings; so, I alone am the beginning, the middle and also the end of all beings.

column3:
ādityānāmahaṃ(v̐) viṣṇuḥ(r), jyotiṣāṃ(m) raviraṃśumān।
marīcirmarutāmasmi,  nakṣatrāṇāmahaṃ(m)  śaśī॥10.21॥
column4: I am Visņu among the twelve sons of Aditi, and the radiant sun among the luminaries; I am the glow of the Maruts (the forty-nine wind-gods), and the moon, the lord of the stars.
column5: I am Viṣṇu among the twelve sons of Aditi, and the radiant sun among the luminaries; I am the glow of the Maruts (the forty-nine wind-gods), and the moon, the lord of the stars.

column3:
vedānāṃ(m) sāmavedo'smi, devānāmasmi vāsavaḥ।
indriyāṇāṃ(m) manaścāsmi, bhūtānāmasmi cetanā॥10.22॥
column4: Among the Vedas, I am the ṣamaveda; among the gods, I am Indra. Among the organs of perception i.e., senses, I am the mind; and I am the consciousness (life-energy) in living beings.
column5: Among the Vedas I am the Sāmaveda; among the gods I am Indra. Among the organs of perception i.e., senses, I am the mind; and I am the consciousness (life-energy) in living beings.

column3:
rudrāṇāṃ(m) śaṅkaraścāsmi, vitteśo yakṣarakṣasām।
vasūnāṃ(m) pāvakaścāsmi, meruḥ(ś) śikhariṇāmaham॥10.23॥
column4: Among the eleven Rudrās (gods of destruction), I am Siva; and among the yakṣas and rakṣasās, I am the lord of riches (Kubera). Among the eight Vasus, I am the god of fire; and among the mountains, I am the Meru.
column5: Among the eleven Rudrās (gods of destruction), I am Śiva and among the yakṣas and rakṣasās, I am the lord of riches (Kubera). Among the eight Vasus, I am the god of fire; and among the mountains, I am the Meru.

column3:
purodhasāṃ(ñ) ca mukhyaṃ(m) māṃ(v̐), viddhi pārtha bṛhaspatim।
senānīnāmahaṃ(m) skandaḥ(s), sarasāmasmi sāgaraḥ॥10.24॥
column4: Among the priests, Arjuna, know Me to be their chief, Bṛhaspati. Among warrior-chiefs, I am Skanda (the generalissimo of the gods); and among the reservoirs of water, I am the ocean.
column5: Among the priests, Arjuna, know Me to be their chief, Bṛhaspati. Among warrior-chiefs, I am Skanda (the generalissimo of the gods); and among the reservoirs of water, I am the ocean.

column3:
maharṣīṇāṃ(m) bhṛgurahaṃ(ṅ), girāmasmyekamakṣaram।
yajñānāṃ(ñ) japayajño'smi, sthāvarāṇāṃ(m) himālayaḥ॥10.25॥
column4: Among the great seers, I am Bhrgu; among words, I am the sacred syllable OM; among sacrifices, I am the sacrifice of Japa (muttering of sacred formulas); and among the immovables, the Himalaya.
column5: Among the great seers, I am Bhṛgu; among words, I am the sacred syllable OM; among sacrifices, I am the sacrifice of Japa (muttering of sacred formulas); and among the immovables, the Himalaya.

column3:
aśvatthaḥ(s) sarvavṛkṣāṇāṃ(n), devarṣīṇāṃ(ñ) ca nāradaḥ।
gandharvāṇāṃ(ñ) citrarathaḥ(s), siddhānāṃ(ṅ) kapilo muniḥ॥10.26॥
column4: Among all trees, I am Aśvattha (the holy fig tree); among the celestial sages, Nārada; among the Gandharvas (celestial musicians), Citraratha, and among the Siddhas, I am the sage Kapila.
column5: Among all trees, I am Aśvattha (the holy fig tree); among the celestial sages, Nārada; among the Gandharvas (celestial musicians), Citraratha, and among the Siddhas, I am the sage Kapila.

column3:
uccaiḥśravasamaśvānāṃ(v̐), viddhi māmamṛtodbhavam।
airāvataṃ(ṅ) gajendrāṇāṃ(n), narāṇāṃ(ñ) ca narādhipam॥10.27॥
column4: Among horses, know me to be the celestial horse Uccaiḥśrava, begotten of the churning of the ocean along with nectar; among mighty elephants, Airāvata (Indra's elephant); and among men, the king.
column5: Among horses, know me to be the celestial horse Uccaiḥśrava, begotten of the churning of the ocean along with nectar; among mighty elephants, Airāvata (Indra's elephant); and among men, the king.

column3:
āyudhānāmahaṃ(v̐) vajraṃ(n) dhenūnāmasmi kāmadhuk।
prajanaścāsmi kandarpaḥ(s), sarpāṇāmasmi vāsukiḥ॥10.28॥
column4: Among weapons, I am the thunderbolt; among cows, I am the celestial cow Kāmadhenu (the cow of plenty). I am Kandarpa, the generative passion which leads to procreation (as enjoined by the scriptures); among serpents, I am Vāsuki.
column5: Among weapons, I am the thunderbolt; among cows, I am the celestial cow Kāmadhenu (the cow of plenty). I am Kandarpa, the generative passion which leads to procreation (as enjoined by the scriptures); among serpents, I am Vāsuki.

column3:
anantaścāsmi  nāgānāṃ(v̐),  varuṇo  yādasāmaham।
pitṝṇāmaryamā  cāsmi,  yamaḥ(s) saṃyamatāmaham॥10.29॥
column4: Among Nāgās (a special class of serpents), I am the serpent-god Ananta; and I am Varuņa, the lord of aquatic creatures. Among the manes, I am Aryama (the head of the Pitṝs); and among rulers, I am Yama (the god of death).
column5: Among Nāgās (a special class of serpents), I am the serpent-god Ananta; and I am Varuṇa, the lord of aquatic creatures. Among the manes, I am Aryama (the head of the Pitṝs); and Among those who discipline/control, I am Yama.(the god of death).

column3:
prahlādaścāsmi daityānāṃ(ṅ), kālaḥ(kh) kalayatāmaham।
mṛgāṇāṃ(ñ) ca mṛgendro'haṃ(v̐), vainateyaśca pakṣiṇām॥10.30॥
column4: Among the Daityas, I am the great devotee Prahlāda; and of calculators, I am Time; among quadrupeds, I am the lion; and among birds, I am Garuda.
column5: Among the Daitya demons I am the devoted Prahlāda, among subduers I am time, among beasts I am the lion, and among birds I am Garuḍa.

column3:
pavanaḥ(ph) pavatāmasmi, rāmaḥ(ś) śastrabhṛtāmaham।
jhaṣāṇāṃ(m)  makaraścāsmi,  srotasāmasmi jāhnavī॥10.31॥
column4: Among purifiers, I am the wind; among wielders of arms, I am Śrī Rāma. Among fishes, I am the alligator; and among streams, I am the Ganges.
column5: Among purifiers, I am the wind; among wielders of arms, I am Śrī Rāma. Among fishes, I am the crocodile, and among streams, I am the Ganges.

column3:
sargāṇāmādirantaśca, madhyaṃ(ñ) caivāhamarjuna।
adhyātmavidyā vidyānāṃ(v̐), vādaḥ(ph) pravadatāmaham॥10.32॥
column4: Arjuna, I am the beginning, the middle and the end of all creations. Of all knowledge, I am the knowledge of the soul (metaphysics); among disputants, I am the right type of discussion.
column5: Arjuna, I am the beginning, the middle and the end of all creations. Of all knowledge, I am the knowledge of the soul (metaphysics); among disputants, I am the right type of discussion.

column3:
akṣarāṇāmakāro'smi,  dvandvaḥ(s)  sāmāsikasya ca।
ahamevākṣayaḥ(kh) kālo, dhātāhaṃ(v̐) viśvatomukhaḥ॥10.33॥
column4:
Among the sounds represented by the various letters, I am 'A' (the sound represented by the first letter of the alphabet); of the different kinds of compounds in grammar, I am the copulative
compound. I am verily the endless Time (the devourer of Time, God); I am the sustainer of all, having My face on all sides.
column5: Among the sounds represented by the various letters, I am 'A' (the sound represented by the first letter of the alphabet); of the different kinds of compounds in grammar, I am the copulative compound. I am verily the endless Time (the devourer of Time, God); I am the sustainer of all, having My face on all sides.

column3:
mṛtyuḥ(s) sarvaharaścāham, udbhavaśca bhaviṣyatām।
kīrtiḥ(ś) śrīrvākca nārīṇāṃ(m), smṛtirmedhā dhṛtiḥ kṣamā॥10.34॥
column4: I am the all-destroying Death that annihilates all, and the origin of all that are to be born. Of feminities, I am Kīrti, Śrī, Vāk, Smṛti, Medhā, Dhṛti and Kṣama (the goddesses presiding over glory, prosperity, speech, memory, intelligence, fortitude and forbearance, respectively).
column5: I am the all-destroying Death that annihilates all, and the origin of all that are to be born. Of feminities, I am Kīrti, Śrī, Vāk, Smṛti, Medhā, Dhṛti and Kṣama (the goddesses presiding over glory, prosperity, speech, memory, intelligence, fortitude and forbearance, respectively).

column3:
bṛhatsāma tathā sāmnāṃ(ṅ), gāyatrī chandasāmaham।
māsānāṃ(m) mārgaśīrṣo'ham, ṛtūnāṃ(ṅ) kusumākaraḥ॥10.35॥
column4: Likewise, among the Srutis that can be sung, I am the variety known as Bṛhatṣama; while among the Vedic hymns, I am the hymn known as Gāyatrī. Again, among the twelve months of the Hindu calendar, I am the month known as 'Margasirṣa' (corresponding approximately to November-December); and among the six seasons (successively appearing in India in the course of a year), I am the spring season.
column5: Likewise, among the Śrutis that can be sung, I am the variety known as Bṛhatṣama; while among the Vedic hymns, I am the hymn known as Gāyatrī. Again, among the twelve months of the Hindu calendar, I am the month known as 'Mārgaśīrṣa' (corresponding approximately to November-December); and among the six seasons (successively appearing in India in the course of a year), I am the spring season.

column3:
dyutaṃ(ñ)  chalayatāmasmi,  tejastejasvināmaham।
jayo'smi vyavasāyo'smi, sattvaṃ(m) sattvavatāmaham॥10.36॥
column4: I am gambling among deceitful practices, and the glory of the glorious. I am the victory of the victorious, the resolve of the resolute, the goodness of the good.
column5: I am gambling among deceitful practices, and the glory of the glorious. I am the victory of the victorious, the resolve of the resolute, the goodness of the good.

column3:
vṛṣṇīnāṃ(v̐) vāsudevo'smi, pāṇḍavānāṃ(n) dhanañjayaḥ।
munīnāmapyahaṃ(v̐) vyāsaḥ(kh), kavīnāmuśanā kaviḥ॥10.37॥
column4: I am Kṛṣṇā among the Vṛṣṇīs, Arjuna among the sons of Paņḍu, vyāsa among the sages, and the sage Sukracarya among the wise.
column5: I am Vāsudeva among the Vṛṣṇīs, Arjuna among the sons of Paņḍu, Vyāsa among the sages, and the sage Śukrācārya among the wise.

column3:
daṇḍo    damayatāmasmi,   nītirasmi   jigīṣatām।
maunaṃ(ñ) caivāsmi guhyānāṃ(ñ), jñānaṃ(ñ) jñānavatāmaham॥10.38॥
column4: I am the subduing power of rulers; I am righteousness in those who seek to conquer. Of things to be kept secret, I am the custodian in the form of reticence; and I am the wisdom of the wise.
column5: I am the subduing power of rulers; I am righteousness in those who seek to conquer. Of things to be kept secret, I am the custodian in the form of reticence; and I am the wisdom of the wise.

column3:
yaccāpi sarvabhūtānāṃ(m), bījaṃ(n) tadahamarjuna।
na tadasti vinā yatsyān, mayā bhūtaṃ(ñ) carācaram॥10.39॥
column4: Arjuna, I am even that, which is the seed of all life. For there is no creature, moving or unmoving, which can exist without Me.
column5: Arjuna, I am even that, which is the seed of all life. For there is no creature, moving or unmoving, which can exist without Me.

column3:
nānto'sti mama divyānāṃ(v̐), vibhūtīnāṃ(m) parantapa।
eṣa  tūddeśataḥ(ph)  prokto,  vibhūtervistaro  mayā॥10.40॥
column4: Arjuna, there is no limit to My divine manifestations. This is only a brief description by Me of the extent of My glory.
column5: Arjuna, there is no limit to My divine manifestations. This is only a brief description by Me of the extent of My glory.

column3:
yadyadvibhūtimatsattvaṃ(m), śrīmadūrjitameva vā।
tattadevāvagaccha tvaṃ(m), mama tejoṃ'śasambhavam॥10.41॥
column4: Every such being as is glorious, brilliant and powerful, know that to be a part manifestation of  My glory.
column5: Every such being as is glorious, brilliant and powerful, know that to be a part manifestation of  My glory.

column3:
athavā bahunaitena,  kiṃ(ñ)  jñātena  tavārjuna।
viṣṭabhyāhamidaṃ(ṅ) kṛtsnam, ekāṃśena sthito jagat॥10.42॥
column4: Or, what will you gain by knowing all this in detail, Arjuna? Suffice it to say that I hold this entire universe by a fraction of My Yogic Power.
column5: Or, what will you gain by knowing all this in detail, Arjuna? Suffice it to say that I hold this entire universe by a fraction of My Yogic Power.

column3:
Om tatsaditi śrīmadbhagavadgītāsu upaniṣatsu
brahmavidyāyāṃ(y̐) yogaśāstre śrīkṛṣṇārjunasaṃvāde
vibhūtiyogo nāma daśamo'dhyāyaḥ ||
column4:
Thus, in the Upaniṣad sung by the Lord, the Science of Brahma, the scripture of Yoga, the
dialogue between Śrī Kṛṣṇā and Arjuna, ends the tenth chapter entitled "The Yoga of Divine Glories."
column5:
Thus, in the Upaniṣad sung by the Lord, the Science of Brahma, the scripture of Yoga, the
dialogue between Śrī Kṛṣṇā and Arjuna, ends the tenth chapter entitled "The Yoga of Divine Glories."

column3:
om śrīparamātmane namaḥ
Śrīmadbhagavadgītā
athaikādaśo'dhyāyaḥ

arjuna uvāca
madanugrahāya paramaṃ(ṅ), guhyamadhyātmasañjñitam।
yattvayoktaṃ(v̐) vacastena, moho'yaṃ(v̐) vigato mama॥11.1॥
column4: By this, profound discourse of spiritual wisdom that you have delivered, out of compassion for me, my darkness has been dispelled.
column5: By this, profound discourse of spiritual wisdom that you have delivered, out of compassion for me, my darkness has been dispelled.

column3:
bhavāpyayau hi bhūtānāṃ(m), śrutau vistaraśo mayā।
tvattaḥ(kh) kamalapatrākṣa, māhātmyamapi cāvyayam॥11.2॥
column4: From You, O lotus-eyed, I have heard in detail, an account of the origin and dissolution of creation and also of your immortal glory.
column5: From You, O lotus-eyed, I have heard in detail, an account of the origin and dissolution of creation and also of your immortal glory.

column3:
evametadyathāttha tvam,  ātmānaṃ(m) parameśvara।
draṣṭumicchāmi te rūpam, aiśvaraṃ(m) puruṣottama॥11.3॥
column4: O Lord Supreme, You are precisely what You have declared Yourself to be. But, I long to see your cosmic divine form, O greatest of persons.
column5: O Lord Supreme, You are precisely what You have declared Yourself to be. But, I long to see your cosmic divine form, O greatest of persons.

column3:
manyase  yadi  tacchakyaṃ(m),  mayā  draṣṭumiti  prabho।
yogeśvara  tato  me tvaṃ(n),  darśayātmānamavyayam॥11.4॥
column4: Lord if you , think that this cosmic form could possibly be seen by me, then, O Lord of Yoga, reveal to me that imperishable form.
column5: Lord if you , think that this cosmic form could possibly be seen by me, then, O Lord of Yoga, reveal to me that imperishable form.

column3:
śrībhagavānuvāca
paśya   me   pārtha   rūpāṇi,    śataśo'tha   sahasraśaḥ।
nānāvidhāni      divyāni,     nānāvarṇākṛtīni     ca॥11.5॥
column4: Behold My forms, O Pārtha (Arjuna), hundreds and thousands, multifarious and divine, of various colours, sizes and shapes.
column5: Behold My forms, O Pārtha (Arjuna), hundreds and thousands, multifarious and divine, of various colours, sizes and shapes.

column3:
paśyādityānvasūnrudrān,     aśvinau       marutastathā।
bahūnyadṛṣṭapūrvāṇi,    paśyāścaryāṇi      bhārata॥11.6॥
column4: Behold in Me, Arjuna, the twelve sons of Aditi, the eight Vasus, the eleven Rudrās (gods of destruction), the two Aśvinīkumāras (the twin-born physicians of gods) and the forty-nine Maruts (wind-gods) and many more marvels, never revealed before.
column5: Behold in Me, Arjuna, the twelve sons of Aditi, the eight Vasus, the eleven Rudrās (gods of destruction), the two Aśvinīkumāras (the twin-born physicians of gods) and the forty-nine Maruts (wind-gods) and many more marvels, never revealed before.

column3:
ihaikasthaṃ(ñ)  jagatkṛtsnaṃ(m),  paśyādya  sacarācaram।
mama  dehe  guḍākeśa,  yaccānyaddraṣṭumicchasi॥11.7॥
column4: O conqueror of sleep Arjuna, now hebold within this body of Mine, the entire universe consisting of both animate and inanimate beings and whatever else you wish to see,
column5: O conqueror of sleep Arjuna, now hebold within this body of Mine, the entire universe consisting of both animate and inanimate beings and whatever else you wish to see,

column3:
na tu māṃ(m) śakyase draṣṭum, anenaiva svacakṣuṣā।
divyaṃ(n) dadāmi te cakṣuḥ(ph), paśya me yogamaiśvaram॥11.8॥
column4: But you cannot view Me, with these human eyes of yours; therefore I grant you divine vision with which, behold My divine power of Yoga.
column5: But you cannot view Me, with these human eyes of yours; therefore I grant you divine vision with which, behold My divine power of Yoga.

column3:
sañjaya uvāca
evamuktvā   tato   rājan,   mahāyogeśvaro   hariḥ।
darśayāmāsa  pārthāya,  paramaṃ(m)  rūpamaiśvaram॥11.9॥
column4: Sañjaya said:O King, having spoken thus, the Supreme Lord of Yoga, Hari (Krsna), showed to Pärtha (Arjuna) His supremely divine form (Viśvarūpa).
column5: Sañjaya said:O King, having spoken thus, the Supreme Lord of Yoga, Hari (Krsna), showed to Pärtha (Arjuna) His supremely divine form (Viśvarūpa).

column3:
anekavaktranayanam,     anekādbhutadarśanam।
anekadivyābharaṇaṃ(n),     divyānekodyatāyudham॥11.10॥
column4: That Supreme Deity of countless months and eyes, presenting many a wonderful sight, decked with unlimited divine ornaments. wielding many divine weapons –
column5: That Supreme Deity of countless months and eyes, presenting many a wonderful sight, decked with unlimited divine ornaments. wielding many divine weapons –

column3:
divyamalyāmbaradharaṃ(n), divyagandhānulepanam।
sarvāścaryamayaṃ(n) devam, anantaṃ(v̐) viśvatomukham॥11.11॥
column4: wearing divine garlands and clothes, besmeared all over with divine perfumes, all wonderful and infinite with faces on all sides. Such a divine cosmic form, the Lord revealed to Arjuna.
column5: wearing divine garlands and clothes, besmeared all over with divine perfumes, all wonderful and infinite with faces on all sides. Such a divine cosmic form, the Lord revealed to Arjuna.

column3:
divisūryasahasrasya,    bhavedyugapadutthitā।
yadi bhāḥ(s) sadṛśī sā syād, bhāsastasya mahātmanaḥ॥11.12॥
column4: If the effulgence of a thousand suns blazed forth all at once in the sky, that would hardly match the radiance of the mighty Lord.
column5: If the effulgence of a thousand suns blazed forth all at once in the sky, that would hardly match the radiance of the mighty Lord.

column3:
tatraikasthaṃ(ñ) jagatkṛtsnaṃ(m), pravibhaktamanekadhā।
apaśyaddevadevasya,      śarīre     pāṇḍavastadā॥11.13॥
column4: Then Arjuna, saw unfolded in that Supreme Deity, the whole universe with its many divisions concentrated at one place.
column5: Then Arjuna, saw unfolded in that Supreme Deity, the whole universe with its many divisions concentrated at one place.

column3:
tataḥ(s)   sa   vismayāviṣṭo,   hṛṣṭaromā   dhanañjayaḥ।
praṇamya    śirasā    devaṃ(ṅ),   kṛtāñjalirabhāṣata॥11.14॥
column4: Then Dhananjaya, struck with wonder and his hair standing on end, bowed before the divine Lord, and with joined palms, addressed Him, thus.
column5: Then Dhananjaya, struck with wonder and his hair standing on end, bowed before the divine Lord, and with joined palms, addressed Him, thus.

column3:
arjuna uvāca

paśyāmi   devāṃstava  deva  dehe,
sarvāṃstathā bhūtaviśeṣasaṅghān।
brahmāṇamīśaṃ(ṅ) kamalāsanastham,
ṛṣīṃśca sarvānuragāṃśca divyān॥11.15॥
column4: Arjuna said: O Lord, I see within Your body all the gods and multitude of different beings; observe Brahmä seated on His lotus-seat, Siva and all the sages and celestial serpents.
column5: Arjuna said: O Lord, I see within Your body all the gods and multitude of different beings; observe Brahmä seated on His lotus-seat, Siva and all the sages and celestial serpents.

column3:
anekabāhūdaravaktranetraṃ(m),  
paśyāmi tvāṃ(m) sarvato'nantarūpam।
nāntaṃ(n) na madhyaṃ(n) na punastavādiṃ(m),
paśyāmi   viśveśvara     viśvarūpa॥11.16॥
column4: O Lord of all universe, I behold You, endless to forms on all sides, with numerous arms, bellies, faces, and eyes. O Universal Form (Viswarūpa), I see, neither Your beginning nor middle nor end.
column5: O Lord of all universe, I behold You, endless to forms on all sides, with numerous arms, bellies, faces, and eyes. O Universal Form (Viswarūpa), I see, neither Your beginning nor middle nor end.

column3:
kirīṭinaṃ(ṅ) gadinaṃ(ñ) cakriṇaṃ(ñ) ca,
tejorāśiṃ(m) sarvato dīptimantam।
paśyāmi tvāṃ(n) durnirīkṣyaṃ(m) samantād-
dīptānalārkadyutimaprameyam॥11.17॥
column4: I see You, wearing a diadem, holding a mace and discus, a mass of effulgence, shining around having a brilliance like the blazing fire and sun, dazzling and boundless on all sides.
column5: I see You, wearing a diadem, holding a mace and discus, a mass of effulgence, shining around having a brilliance like the blazing fire and sun, dazzling and boundless on all sides.

column3:
tvamakṣaraṃ(m) paramaṃ(v̐) veditavyaṃ(n),
tvamasya viśvasya paraṃ(n) nidhānam।
tvamavyayaḥ(ś) śāśvatadharmagoptā,
sanātanastvaṃ(m)  puruṣo  mato  me॥11.18॥
column4: You are I recognise, the Imperishable, the Supreme Being (Akşara) to be realized; You are the ultimate refuge of this universe; the protector of eternal Dharma (duty and righteousness) and You are the eternally imperishable Being.
column5: You are I recognise, the Imperishable, the Supreme Being (Akşara) to be realized; You are the ultimate refuge of this universe; the protector of eternal Dharma (duty and righteousness) and You are the eternally imperishable Being.

column3:
anādimadhyāntamanantavīryam,     
anantabāhuṃ(m) śaśisūryanetram।
paśyāmi tvāṃ(n) dīptahutāśavaktraṃ(m),
svatejasā viśvamidaṃ(n) tapantam॥11.19॥
column4: I see You, without beginning, middle or end, infinite in power, of infinite arms, the sun and the moon being Your eyes, the blazing fire Your mouth; warming the universe, with Your radiance.
column5: I see You, without beginning, middle or end, infinite in power, of infinite arms, the sun and the moon being Your eyes, the blazing fire Your mouth; warming the universe, with Your radiance.

column3:
dyāvāpṛthivyoridamantaraṃ(m)  hi,
vyāptaṃ(n) tvayaikena diśaśca sarvāḥ।
dṛṣṭvādbhutaṃ(m) rūpamugraṃ(n) tavedaṃ(l̐),
lokatrayaṃ(m) pravyathitaṃ(m) mahātman॥11.20॥
column4: The space between the heaven and earth and all the spheres is pervaded by you, alone. Seeing this marvellous and frightening form of Yours, O Great-Soul, the three worlds feel greatly alarmed.
column5: The space between the heaven and earth and all the spheres is pervaded by you, alone. Seeing this marvellous and frightening form of Yours, O Great-Soul, the three worlds feel greatly alarmed.

column3:
amī hi tvāṃ(m) surasaṅghā viśanti,
kecidbhītāḥ(ph) prāñjalayo gṛṇanti।
svastītyuktvā maharṣisiddhasaṅghāḥ(s),
stuvanti tvāṃ(m) stutibhiḥ(ph) puṣkalābhiḥ॥11.21॥
column4: Those multitudes of gods merge in You; some in awe with folded bands are chanting Your names and glories; bands of great sages and Siddhas (emancipated souls) cry "Hail Highest Majesty", and shower praises on You with excellent hymns.
column5: Those multitudes of gods merge in You; some in awe with folded bands are chanting Your names and glories; bands of great sages and Siddhas (emancipated souls) cry "Hail Highest Majesty", and shower praises on You with excellent hymns.

column3:
rudrādityā  vasavo  ye  ca  sādhyā,
viśve'śvinau marutaścoṣmapāśca।
gandharvayakṣāsurasiddhasaṅghā,
vīkṣante tvāṃ(v̐) vismitāścaiva sarve॥11.22॥
column4: The (eleven) Rudras, (twelve) Adityas, (eight) Vasus, (twelve) Sādhyas, (ten) Viśvedevas, (two) Aśvinīkumāras, (forty-nine) Maruts, manes, hosts of Gandharvas, Yakşas, Asuras and Siddhas-they all, gaze at You, in amazement.
column5: The (eleven) Rudras, (twelve) Adityas, (eight) Vasus, (twelve) Sādhyas, (ten) Viśvedevas, (two) Aśvinīkumāras, (forty-nine) Maruts, manes, hosts of Gandharvas, Yakşas, Asuras and Siddhas-they all, gaze at You, in amazement.

column3:
rūpaṃ(m) mahatte bahuvaktranetraṃ(m),
mahābāho    bahubāhūrupādam।
bahūdaraṃ(m) bahudaṃṣṭrākarālaṃ(n),
dṛṣṭvā lokāḥ(ph) pravyathitāstathāham॥11.23॥
column4: O mighty-armed, seeing Your immeasurable and frightening form with numerous mouths, eyes, arms, thighs, feet, bellies and fearful teeth, the worlds are terror-stricken, and so am I.
column5: O mighty-armed, seeing Your immeasurable and frightening form with numerous mouths, eyes, arms, thighs, feet, bellies and fearful teeth, the worlds are terror-stricken, and so am I.

column3:
nabhaḥspṛśaṃ(n) dīptamanekavarṇaṃ(v̐),
vyāttānanaṃ(n)  dīptaviśālanetram।
dṛṣṭvā hi tvāṃ(m) pravyathitāntarātmā,
dhṛtiṃ(n) na vindāmi śamaṃ(ñ) ca viṣṇo॥11.24॥
column4: When I see Your form, touching the sky, effulgent in many colours, with mouths wide open, with large shining eyes, my heart trembles with fear, I lose courage and find no peace, O Vişnu.
column5: When I see Your form, touching the sky, effulgent in many colours, with mouths wide open, with large shining eyes, my heart trembles with fear, I lose courage and find no peace, O Vişnu.

column3:
daṃṣṭrākarālāni    ca    te     mukhāni,
dṛṣṭvaiva      kālānalasannibhāni।
diśo  na  jāne  na  labhe  ca  śarma,
prasīda       deveśa     jagannivāsa॥11.25॥
column4: Seeing Your mouths, with fearful teeth, resembling the raging fires at the time of universal destruction, I know not, the cardinal directions, nor do I find peace. Therefore, be kind to me, O Lord of the gods and the shelter, of the universe.
column5: Seeing Your mouths, with fearful teeth, resembling the raging fires at the time of universal destruction, I know not, the cardinal directions, nor do I find peace. Therefore, be kind to me, O Lord of the gods and the shelter, of the universe.

column3:
amī ca tvāṃ(n) dhṛtarāṣṭrasya putrāḥ(s),
sarve sahaivāvanipālasaṅghaiḥ।
bhīṣmo droṇaḥ(s) sūtaputrastathāsau,
sahāsmadīyairapi yodhamukhyaiḥ॥11.26॥
column4: All the sons of Dhytarastra, with hosts of kings of the earth, Bhīşma, Drona, Karna, with the chief warriors on our side as well,
column5: All the sons of Dhytarastra, with hosts of kings of the earth, Bhīşma, Drona, Karna, with the chief warriors on our side as well,

column3:
vaktrāṇi     te     tvaramāṇā   viśanti,
daṃṣṭrākarālāni     bhayānakāni।
kecidvilagnā         daśanāntareṣu,
sandṛśyante cūrṇitairuttamāṅgaiḥ॥11.27॥
column4: -- are rushing headlong into Your mouths with terrible teeth and fearful to look at. Some of these are seen sticking in the gaps between the teeth, with their heads crushed.
column5: -- are rushing headlong into Your mouths with terrible teeth and fearful to look at. Some of these are seen sticking in the gaps between the teeth, with their heads crushed.

column3:
yathā nadīnāṃ(m) bahavo’mbuvegāḥ(s),
samudramevābhimukhā dravanti।
tathā     tavāmī    naralokavīrā,
viśanti     vaktrāṇyabhivijvalanti॥11.28॥
column4: As diverse torrents of rivers rush towards an ocean, so do these warriors of the mortal world, enter into Your blazing mouths,
column5: As diverse torrents of rivers rush towards an ocean, so do these warriors of the mortal world, enter into Your blazing mouths,

column3:
yathā pradīptaṃ(ñ) jvalanaṃ(m) pataṅgā,
viśanti nāśāya samṛddhavegāḥ,
tathaiva   nāśāya   viśanti   lokāḥ(s),
tavāpi   vaktrāṇi   samṛddhavegāḥ॥11.29॥
column4: As moths out of their folly rush headlong into the burning fire for destruction, even so, do these people, hurriedly speed into Your mouths for their annihilation.
column5: As moths out of their folly rush headlong into the burning fire for destruction, even so, do these people, hurriedly speed into Your mouths for their annihilation.

column3:
lelihyase  grasamānaḥ(s)  samantāl,
lokānsamagrānvadanairjvaladbhiḥ।
tejobhirāpūrya   jagatsamagraṃ(m),
bhāsastavogrāḥ(ph)  pratapanti viṣṇo॥11.30॥
column4: Devouring all the worlds through Your flaming mouths, and licking them on all sides. Your fiery rays, fill the whole world with radiance, and heat it. O Vişnu.
column5: Devouring all the worlds through Your flaming mouths, and licking them on all sides. Your fiery rays, fill the whole world with radiance, and heat it. O Vişnu.

column3:
ākhyāhi  me  ko  bhavānugrarūpo,
namo'stu  te  devavara  prasīda,
vijñātumicchāmi bhavantamādyaṃ(n),
na  hi  prajānāmi  tava  pravṛttim॥11.31॥
column4: Tell me, who You are, so fierce in form. I how deeply to You, Ο Supreme Deity, be kind. I wish to know You, the Primal One, in your essence (tattva), for I do not understand Your objective.
column5: Tell me, who You are, so fierce in form. I how deeply to You, Ο Supreme Deity, be kind. I wish to know You, the Primal One, in your essence (tattva), for I do not understand Your objective.

column3:
śrībhagavānuvāca
kālo'smi    lokakṣayakṛtpravṛddho,
lokān samāhartumiha pravṛttaḥ।
ṛte'pi tvāṃ(n) na bhaviṣyanti sarve,
ye'vasthtitāḥ(ph) pratyanīkeṣu yodhāḥ॥11.32॥
column4: I am the mighty world-destroying kāla now engaged in wiping out the world. Even without you the warriors arrayed in the hostile army will not survive.
column5: I am the mighty world-destroying kāla now engaged in wiping out the world. Even without you the warriors arrayed in the hostile army will not survive.

column3:
tasmāttvamuttiṣṭha   yaśo   labhasva,
jitvā śatrūnbhuṅkṣva rājyaṃ(m) samṛddham,
mayaivaite nihatāḥ(ph) pūrvameva,
nimittamātraṃ(m) bhava savyasācin॥11.33॥
column4: Therefore, arise and win glory in conquering the foes, and experience the pleasure of the affluent kingdom. These warriors stand already slain by Me; and you are merely an instrument, O Savyasācin (Savyasācin-One who can shoot arrows with the left hand also).
column5: Therefore, arise and win glory in conquering the foes, and experience the pleasure of the affluent kingdom. These warriors stand already slain by Me; and you are merely an instrument, O Savyasācin (Savyasācin-One who can shoot arrows with the left hand also).

column3:
droṇaṃ(ñ) ca bhīṣmaṃ(ñ) ca jayadrathaṃ(ñ) ca,
karṇaṃ(n) tathānyānapi yodhavīrān।
mayā hatāṃstvaṃ(ñ) jahi mā vyathiṣṭhā,
yudhyasva  jetāsi  raṇe  sapatnān॥34॥
column4: Slay, Dropa, Bhīşma, Jayadratha, Karpa and other brave warriors who are already doomed to be killed by Me. Be not afraid. Fight, and you will conquer your enemies, in battle.
column5: Slay, Dropa, Bhīşma, Jayadratha, Karpa and other brave warriors who are already doomed to be killed by Me. Be not afraid. Fight, and you will conquer your enemies, in battle.

column3:
sañjaya uvāca
etacchrutvā  vacanaṃ(ṅ)  keśavasya,
kṛtāñjalirvepamānaḥ(kh)   kirīṭī।
namaskṛtvā bhūya evāha kṛṣṇaṃ(m),
sagadgadaṃ(m) bhītabhītaḥ(ph) praṇamya॥11.35॥
column4: Sañjaya said: Having heard these words of Lord Kesava, the crowned one (Arjuna), with folded hands, trembling, prostrating himself over again overwhelmed with fear addressed Lord Krsna, in a choked voice, after bowing down.
column5: Sañjaya said: Having heard these words of Lord Kesava, the crowned one (Arjuna), with folded hands, trembling, prostrating himself over again overwhelmed with fear addressed Lord Krsna, in a choked voice, after bowing down.

column3:
arjuna uvāca
sthāne  hṛṣīkeśa  tava  prakīrtyā,
jagatprahṛṣyatyanurajyate  ca।
rakṣāṃsi  bhītāni  diśo  dravanti,
sarve namasyanti ca siddhasaṅghāḥ॥11.36॥
column4: O Omniscient Lord, it is but apt that the universe exults, and is filled with love by chanting Your names and glory; terrified demons are fleeing in all directions, and all the hosts of Siddhas (perfected souls) are bowing to you.
column5: O Omniscient Lord, it is but apt that the universe exults, and is filled with love by chanting Your names and glory; terrified demons are fleeing in all directions, and all the hosts of Siddhas (perfected souls) are bowing to you.

column3:
kasmācca te na nameranmahātman,
garīyase  brahmaṇo'pyādikartre,
ananta      deveśa   jagannivāsa,
tvamakṣaraṃ(m) sadasattatparaṃ(y̐) yat॥11.37॥
column4: O Great Soul, why should they not bow to You, the greatest of all, the progenitor, even of the Brahmā? O Infinite one, O Lord of the gods, Abode of the universe, You are eternal. You are the being (real), the non-being (unreal), and that, which is beyond, both being and non-being viz., the Imperishable Brahma.
column5: O Great Soul, why should they not bow to You, the greatest of all, the progenitor, even of the Brahmā? O Infinite one, O Lord of the gods, Abode of the universe, You are eternal. You are the being (real), the non-being (unreal), and that, which is beyond, both being and non-being viz., the Imperishable Brahma.

column3:
tvamādidevaḥ(ph) puruṣaḥ(ph) purāṇaḥ(s),
tvamasya viśvasya paraṃ(n) nidhānam।
vettāsi vedyaṃ(ñ) ca paraṃ(ñ) ca dhāma,
tvayā tataṃ(v̐) viśvamanantarūpa॥11.38॥
column4: You are the Primeval God, the primordial spirit. You are the ultimate shelter of the universe, you are the knower, the knowable and the Supreme Abode. This universe is fully pervaded by you, Being of infinite forms.
column5: You are the Primeval God, the primordial spirit. You are the ultimate shelter of the universe, you are the knower, the knowable and the Supreme Abode. This universe is fully pervaded by you, Being of infinite forms.

column3:
vāyuryamo'gnirvaruṇaḥ(ś) śaśāṅkaḥ(ph),
prajāpatistvaṃ(m) prapitāmahaśca।
namo namaste'stu sahasrakṛtvaḥ(ph),
punaśca  bhūyo'pi  namo  namaste॥11.39॥
column4: You are the God of wind, God of death (yama). God of fire and water, the moon-god, Prajapati, and the great grandfather of beings. Salutations to You, salutations a thousand time and again salutations to You.
column5: You are the God of wind, God of death (yama). God of fire and water, the moon-god, Prajapati, and the great grandfather of beings. Salutations to You, salutations a thousand time and again salutations to You.

column3:
namaḥ(ph) purastādatha pṛṣṭhataste,
namo'stu  te   sarvata   eva  sarva।
anantavīryāmitavikramastvaṃ(m),
sarvaṃ(m) samāpnoṣi tato'si sarvaḥ॥11.40॥
column4: O Lord of infinite prowess, my salutations to You from the front, the rear and from all sides. O All in all! You, who possess limitless might, and pervade the world, You are omnipresent.
column5: O Lord of infinite prowess, my salutations to You from the front, the rear and from all sides. O All in all! You, who possess limitless might, and pervade the world, You are omnipresent.

column3:
sakheti matvā prasabhaṃ(y̐) yaduktaṃ(m),
he  kṛṣṇa  he  yādava he sakheti।
ajānatā mahimānaṃ(n) tavedaṃ(m),
mayā  pramādātpraṇayena  vā'pi॥11.41॥
column4: Whatever I may have said due to carelessness or love, addressing You as "O Krşņa, O Yadava, O Friend", thinking of You only as a friend ignorant of Your greatness; and the way in which I may have shown any disrespect to You in jest,--
column5: Whatever I may have said due to carelessness or love, addressing You as "O Krşņa, O Yadava, O Friend", thinking of You only as a friend ignorant of Your greatness; and the way in which I may have shown any disrespect to You in jest,--

column3:
yaccāvahāsārthamasatkṛto'si,
vihāraśayyāsanabhojaneṣu,
eko'thavāpyacyuta tatṣamakṣaṃ(n),
tatkṣāmaye tvāmahamaprameyam. 11.42
column4: - while playing, reposing, sitting or dining, either alone or in company-I crave forgiveness from You, Who are infinite, O infallible Lord.
column5: * while playing, reposing, sitting or dining, either alone or in company-I crave forgiveness from You, Who are infinite, O infallible Lord.

column3:
pitāsi    lokasya    carācarasya,
tvamasya pūjyaśca gururgarīyān।
na tvatsamo'styabhyadhikaḥ(kh) kuto'nyo,
lokatraye'pyapratimaprabhāva॥11.43॥
column4: You are the Father, also the Great Teacher of this animate and inanimate creation and are supremely adorable. O Lord, manifesting incomparable glory, in all the three worlds, there is none equal to You; who could then possibly excel You?
column5: You are the Father, also the Great Teacher of this animate and inanimate creation and are supremely adorable. O Lord, manifesting incomparable glory, in all the three worlds, there is none equal to You; who could then possibly excel You?

column3:
tasmātpraṇamya praṇidhāya kāyaṃ(m),
prasādaye tvāmahamīśamīḍyam,
piteva putrasya sakheva sakhyuḥ(ph),
priyaḥ(ph) priyāyārhasi deva soḍhum॥11.44॥
column4: Therefore, O adorable Lord, bowing deeply and prostrating before You, I implore You to forgive me. Bear with me, O Lord as a father with a son, as a friend with a friend, or as a lover with his beloved, and forgive my faults.
column5: Therefore, O adorable Lord, bowing deeply and prostrating before You, I implore You to forgive me. Bear with me, O Lord as a father with a son, as a friend with a friend, or as a lover with his beloved, and forgive my faults.

column3:
adṛṣṭapūrvaṃ(m)   hṛṣito'smi  dṛṣṭvā,
bhayena ca pravyathitaṃ(m) mano me।
tadeva me darśaya deva rūpaṃ(m),
prasīda     deveśa   jagannivāsa॥11.45॥
column4: I rejoice that I have witnessed what was never revealed before, but my mind is confused with fear. Reveal to me kindly that divine form (the placid form of Vişnu), O Lord of the gods, O Abode of the universe.
column5: I rejoice that I have witnessed what was never revealed before, but my mind is confused with fear. Reveal to me kindly that divine form (the placid form of Vişnu), O Lord of the gods, O Abode of the universe.

column3:
kirīṭinaṃ(ṅ) gadinaṃ(ñ) cakrahastam,
icchāmi tvāṃ(n) draṣṭumahaṃ(n) tathaiva।
tenaiva    rūpeṇa  caturbhujena,
sahasrabāho     bhava    viśvamūrte॥11.46॥
column4: I wish to see You as before, crowned, holding a mace and a discus in two of Your hands, in Your former state only, having four-arms, O thousand-armed, O Universal Manifestation.
column5: I wish to see You as before, crowned, holding a mace and a discus in two of Your hands, in Your former state only, having four-arms, O thousand-armed, O Universal Manifestation.

column3:
śrībhagavānuvāca

mayā prasannena tavārjunedaṃ(m),
rūpaṃ(m) paraṃ(n) darśitamātmayogāt।
tejomayaṃ(v̐) viśvamanantamādyaṃ(y̐),
yanme tvadanyena na dṛṣṭapūrvam॥11.47॥
column4: The Blessed Lord said:Arjuna, being pleased with you, I have shown you through My power of Yoga, this supreme, shining primal and infinite Universal Form, which none but you, has ever seen.
column5: The Blessed Lord said:Arjuna, being pleased with you, I have shown you through My power of Yoga, this supreme, shining primal and infinite Universal Form, which none but you, has ever seen.

column3:
na vedayajñādhyayanairna dānaiḥ(r),
na ca kriyābhirna tapobhirugraiḥ।
evaṃrūpaḥ(ś) śakya ahaṃ(n) nṛloke,
draṣṭuṃ(n) tvadanyena kurupravīra॥11.48॥
column4: Arjuna, in this mortal world, I could not be seen in this form, by anyone, other than you, either through the study of the Vedas, or of rituals, or by gifts, sacrifices or austere penances.
column5: Arjuna, in this mortal world, I could not be seen in this form, by anyone, other than you, either through the study of the Vedas, or of rituals, or by gifts, sacrifices or austere penances.

column3:
mā te vyathā mā ca vimūḍhabhāvo,
dṛṣṭvā rūpaṃ(ṅ) ghoramīdṛṅmamedam।
vyapetabhīḥ(ph) prītamanāḥ(ph) punastvaṃ(n),
tadeva me rūpamidaṃ(m) prapaśya॥11.49॥
column4: On seeing such a frightening form of Mine, be neither afraid nor bewildered. With a fearless and complacent mind, behold once again the same four-armed Form of Mine.
column5: On seeing such a frightening form of Mine, be neither afraid nor bewildered. With a fearless and complacent mind, behold once again the same four-armed Form of Mine.

column3:
sañjaya uvāca

ityarjunaṃ(v̐)    vāsudevastathoktvā,
svakaṃ(m) rūpaṃ(n) darśayāmāsa bhūyaḥ।
āśvāsayāmāsa    ca    bhītamenaṃ(m),
bhūtvā punaḥ(s) saumyavapurmahātmā॥11.50॥
column4: Sañjaya said: Having spoken thus to Arjuna, Lord Vasudeva, showed again His usual four-armed form and assumed the two-armed gentle appearance, thus the great soul gave solace to Arjuna, who was frightened.
column5: Sañjaya said: Having spoken thus to Arjuna, Lord Vasudeva, showed again His usual four-armed form and assumed the two-armed gentle appearance, thus the great soul gave solace to Arjuna, who was frightened.

column3:
arjuna uvāca

dṛṣṭvedaṃ(m) mānuṣaṃ(m) rūpaṃ(n), tava saumyaṃ(ñ) janārdana।
idānīmasmi saṃvṛttaḥ(s), sacetāḥ(ph) prakṛtiṃ(ṅ) gataḥ॥11.51॥
column4: Arjuna said: Having seen this gentle human form of Yours, I am now composed and am restored to my normal nature.
column5: Arjuna said: Having seen this gentle human form of Yours, I am now composed and am restored to my normal nature.

column3:
śrībhagavānuvāca

sudurdarśamidaṃ(m) rūpaṃ(n), dṛṣṭavānasi yanmama।
devā apyasya rūpasya, nityaṃ(n) darśanakāṅkṣiṇaḥ॥11.52॥
column4: The Blessed Lord said: It is exceedingly difficult to observe this form of Mine which you have seen. Even, the gods are always, keen to behold it.
column5: The Blessed Lord said: It is exceedingly difficult to observe this form of Mine which you have seen. Even, the gods are always, keen to behold it.

column3:
nāhaṃ(v̐)  vedairna  tapasā,  na  dānena  na  cejyayā।
śakya evaṃvidho draṣṭuṃ(n), dṛṣṭavānasi māṃ(y̐) yathā॥11.53॥
column4: Neither by the study of Vedas, nor by penance, nor charity, or ritual could I be viewed in this form, as you have.
column5: Neither by the study of Vedas, nor by penance, nor charity, or ritual could I be viewed in this form, as you have.

column3:
bhaktyā tvananyayā śakya, ahamevaṃvidho'rjuna।
jñātuṃ(n) draṣṭuṃ(ñ) ca tatvena, praveṣṭuṃ(ñ) ca parantapa॥11.54॥
column4: By unswerving devotion only can I be seen in this form (with four-arms) and known in its essence and even its merger, O scorcher of foes.
column5: By unswerving devotion only can I be seen in this form (with four-arms) and known in its essence and even its merger, O scorcher of foes.

column3:
matkarmakṛnmatparamo, madbhaktaḥ(s) saṅgavarjitaḥ।
nirvairaḥ(s) sarvabhūteṣu, yaḥ(s) sa māmeti pāṇḍava॥11.55॥
column4: O Pandava (Arjuna), he who acts for My sake, depends on Me, is devoted to Me, has no attachment and is free from malice, towards all beings, reaches Me.
column5: O Pandava (Arjuna), he who acts for My sake, depends on Me, is devoted to Me, has no attachment and is free from malice, towards all beings, reaches Me.

column3:
Om tatsaditi śrīmadbhagavadgītāsu upaniṣatsu brahmavidyāyāṃ(y̐)
 yogaśāstre śrīkṛṣṇārjunasaṃvāde viśvarūpadarśanayogo 
nāmaikādaśo'dhyāyaḥ ||

om śrīparamātmane namaḥ
śrīmadbhagavadgītā
atha dvādaśo'dhyāyaḥ

arjuna uvāca
evaṃ(m)  satatayuktā  ye,  bhaktāstvāṃ(m)  paryupāsate।
ye cāpyakṣaramavyaktaṃ(n), teṣāṃ(ṅ) ke yogavittamāḥ॥12.1॥
column4: Arjuna said : The devotees exclusively and constantly devoted to you in the manner stated just earlier, adore You as possessed of form and attributes, and those who adore as the supreme reality only the indestructible unmanifest Brahma (who is Truth, Knowledge and Bliss solidified) of these two types of worshippers who are the best knowers of Yoga?
column5:
Arjuna said :
 The devotees exclusively and constantly devoted to you in the manner stated just earlier, adore You as possessed of form and attributes, and those who adore as the supreme reality only the indestructible unmanifest Brahma (who is Truth, Knowledge and Bliss solidified) of these two types of worshippers who are the best knowers of Yoga?

column3:
śrībhagavānuvāca
mayyāveśya   mano   ye   māṃ(n),   nityayuktā   upāsate।
śraddhayā   parayopetāḥ(s),   te   me   yuktatamā   matāḥ॥12.2॥
column4: Śrī Bhagavān said : I consider them to be the best Yogīs, who endowed with supreme faith, and ever united through meditation with Me, worship Me with their mind centred on Me.
column5:
Śrī Bhagavān said : 
I consider them to be the best Yogīs, who endowed with supreme faith, and ever united through meditation with Me, worship Me with their mind centred on Me.

column3:
ye  tvakṣaramanirdeśyaṃ,  avyaktaṃ(m)  paryupāsate।
sarvatragamacintyaṃ(ñ)  ca,  kūṭasthamacalaṃ(n)  dhruvam॥12.3॥
column4: Those, however, who fully controlling all their senses and even-minded towards all, and devoted to the welfare of all beings, constantly adore as their very self the unthinkable,
column5: Those, however, who fully controlling all their senses and even-minded towards all, and devoted to the welfare of all beings, constantly adore as their very self the unthinkable,.

column3:
sanniyamyendriyagrāmaṃ(m),  sarvatra  samabuddhayaḥ।
te   prāpnuvanti   māmeva,   sarvabhūtahite   ratāḥ॥12.4॥
column4: Controlling all their senses, equal-minded towards all, and devoted to the welfare of all beings, assuredly they come to Me only.
column5: Controlling all their senses, equal-minded towards all, and devoted to the welfare of all beings, assuredly they come to Me only.

column3:
kleśo'dhikatarasteṣāṃ,     avyaktāsaktacetasām।
avyaktā   hi   gatirduḥkhaṃ(n),   dehavadbhiravāpyate॥12.5॥
column4: Of course, the strain is greater for those who have their mind attached to the Unmanifest, as attunement with the Unmanifest is attained with difficulty by the body-conscious people.
column5: Of course, the strain is greater for those who have their mind attached to the Unmanifest, as attunement with the Unmanifest is attained with difficulty by the body-conscious people.

column3:
ye  tu  sarvāṇi  karmāṇi,  mayi  sannyasya  matparāḥ।
ananyenaiva   yogena,   māṃ(n)   dhyāyanta   upāsate॥12.6॥
column4: On the other hand, those depending exclusively on Me, and surrendering all actions to Me, worship Me (God with attributes), constantly meditating on Me with single-minded devotion.
column5: On the other hand, those depending exclusively on Me, and surrendering all actions to Me, worship Me (God with attributes), constantly meditating on Me with single-minded devotion.

column3:
teṣāmahaṃ(m)  samuddhartā,  mṛtyusaṃsārasāgarāt।
bhavāmi     nacirātpārtha,      mayyāveśitacetasām॥12.7॥
column4: O Arjuna! Who fix their mind on Me, I rescue them from the ocean of birth and death.
column5: O Arjuna! Who fix their mind on Me, I rescue them from the ocean of birth and death.

column3:
mayyeva   mana   ādhatsva,   mayi   buddhiṃ(n)   niveśaya।
nivasiṣyasi   mayyeva,   ata   ūrdhvaṃ(n)   na   saṃśayaḥ॥12.8॥
column4: Therefore, fix your mind on Me, and establish your intellect in Me alone; thereafter you will abide solely in Me. There is no doubt about it.
column5: Therefore, fix your mind on Me, and establish your intellect in Me alone; thereafter you will abide solely in Me. There is no doubt about it.

column3:
atha cittaṃ(m) samādhātuṃ(n), na śaknoṣi mayi sthiram।
abhyāsayogena   tato,   māmicchāptuṃ(n)   dhanañjaya॥12.9॥
column4: If you cannot steadily fix the mind on Me, O Arjuna!  then seek to attain Me through the Yoga of practice.
column5: If you cannot steadily fix the mind on Me, O Arjuna!  then seek to attain Me through the Yoga of practice..

column3:
abhyāse'pyasamartho'si,    matkarmaparamo    bhava।
madarthamapi   karmāṇi,    kurvansiddhimavāpsyasi॥12.10॥
column4: If you are unable even to the pursuit of such practice, be intent to work for Me; you shall attain perfection (in the shape of My realization) even by performing actions for My sake.
column5: If you are unable even to the pursuit of such practice, be intent to work for Me; you shall attain perfection (in the shape of My realization) even by performing actions for My sake.

column3:
athaitadapyaśakto'si,   kartuṃ(m)   madyogamāśritaḥ।
sarvakarmaphalatyāgaṃ(n),  tataḥ(kh)  kuru  yatātmavān॥12.11॥
column4: If, taking recourse to the Yoga of My realization, you are unable even to do this, then, subduing your mind and intellect etc., relinquish the fruit of all actions.
column5: If, taking recourse to the Yoga of My realization, you are unable even to do this, then, subduing your mind and intellect etc., relinquish the fruit of all actions.

column3:
śreyo  hi  jñānamabhyāsāj,  jñānāddhyānaṃ(v̐)  viśiṣyate।
dhyānātkarmaphalatyāgaḥ(s),   tyāgācchāntiranantaram॥12.12॥
column4: Knowledge is better than practice without discernment, meditation on God is superior to knowledge, and renunciation of the fruit of actions is even superior to meditation; for, peace immediately follows from renunciation.
column5: Knowledge is better than practice without discernment, meditation on God is superior to knowledge, and renunciation of the fruit of actions is even superior to meditation; for, peace immediately follows from renunciation.

column3:
adveṣṭā   sarvabhūtānāṃ(m),   maitraḥ(kh)   karuṇa   eva   ca।
nirmamo   nirahaṅkāraḥ(s),   samaduḥkhasukhaḥ   kṣamī॥12.13॥
column4: He who is free from malice towards all beings, friendly and compassionate, and Free from the feelings of 'I' and 'mine', balanced in joy and sorrow & forgiving by nature.
column5: He who is free from malice towards all beings, friendly and compassionate, and Free from the feelings of 'I' and 'mine', balanced in joy and sorrow & forgiving by nature.

column3:
santuṣṭaḥ(s)   satataṃ(y̐)   yogī,   yatātmā   dṛḍhaniścayaḥ।
mayyarpitamanobuddhiḥ(r), yo madbhaktaḥ(s) sa me priyaḥ॥12.14॥
column4: The Yogī who is Ever-concentrated and mentally united with Me, nay, who has subdued his mind, senses and body, has a firm resolve, and has surrendered his mind and reason to Me—that devotee of Mine is dear to Me.
column5: The Yogī who is Ever-concentrated and mentally united with Me, nay, who has subdued his mind, senses and body, has a firm resolve, and has surrendered his mind and reason to Me—that devotee of Mine is dear to Me.

column3:
yasmānnodvijate    loko,    lokānnodvijate    ca    yaḥ।
harṣāmarṣabhayodvegaiḥ(r), mukto yaḥ(s) sa ca me priyaḥ॥12.15॥
column4: He who is not a source of annoyance to his fellow-creatures, and who in his turn does not feel vexed with his fellow creatures, and who is free from delight and envy, perturbation and fear, is dear to Me.
column5: He who is not a source of annoyance to his fellow-creatures, and who in his turn does not feel vexed with his fellow creatures, and who is free from delight and envy, perturbation and fear, is dear to Me.

column3:
anapekṣaḥ(ś)    śucirdakṣa,    udāsīno    gatavyathaḥ।
sarvārambhaparityāgī,  yo  madbhaktaḥ(s)  sa  me  priyaḥ॥12.16॥
column4: He who wants nothing, who is both internally and externally pure, is wise and impartial and has risen above all the distractions, and who renounces the sense of doership in all undertakings—such a devotee of Mine is dear to Me.
column5: He who wants nothing, who is both internally and externally pure, is wise and impartial and has risen above all the distractions, and who renounces the sense of doership in all undertakings—such a devotee of Mine is dear to Me.

column3:
yo   na    hṛṣyati    na    dveṣṭi,    na    śocati   na   kāṅkṣati।
śubhāśubhaparityāgī,   bhaktimānyaḥ(s)  sa   me   priyaḥ॥12.17॥
column4: He who neither rejoice nor hates, nor grieves , nor desires, and who renounces both good and evil actions and is full of devotion, is dear to Me.
column5: He who neither rejoice nor hates, nor grieves , nor desires, and who renounces both good and evil actions and is full of devotion, is dear to Me.

column3:
samaḥ(ś)   śatrau   ca   mitre   ca,   tathā   mānāpamānayoḥ।
śītoṣṇasukhaduḥkheṣu,    samaḥ(s)   saṅgavivarjitaḥ॥12.18॥
column4: He who deals equally with friend and foe, and is the same in honour and ignominy, who is alike in heat and cold, pleasure and pain and other contrary experiences, and is free from attachment.
column5: He who deals equally with friend and foe, and is the same in honour and ignominy, who is alike in heat and cold, pleasure and pain and other contrary experiences, and is free from attachment.

column3:
tulyanindāstutirmaunī,    santuṣṭo    yena    kenacit।
aniketaḥ(s)  sthiramatiḥ(r),  bhaktimānme  priyo  naraḥ॥12.19॥
column4: He who takes praise & criticism alike, and is given to contemplation and is contented with any means of subsistence available, entertaining no sense of ownership and attachment in respect of his dwelling-place and is full of devotion to Me, that person is dear to Me.
column5: He who takes praise & criticism alike, and is given to contemplation and is contented with any means of subsistence available, entertaining no sense of ownership and attachment in respect of his dwelling-place and is full of devotion to Me, that person is dear to Me.

column3:
ye   tu   dharmyāmṛtamidaṃ(y̐),   yathoktaṃ(m)   paryupāsate।
śraddadhānā   matparamā,   bhaktāste'tīva   me    priyāḥ॥12.20॥
column4: Those devotees, however, who partake in a disinterested way of this nectar of pious wisdom set forth above, endowed with faith and solely devoted to Me, they are extremely dear to me.
column5: Those devotees, however, who partake in a disinterested way of this nectar of pious wisdom set forth above, endowed with faith and solely devoted to Me, they are extremely dear to me.

column3:
om tatsaditi śrīmadbhagavadgītāsu upaniṣatsu brahmavidyāyāṃ(y̐) 
yogaśāstre śrīkṛṣṇārjunasaṃvāde bhaktiyogo nāma 
dvādaśo'dhyāyaḥ ||

om śrīparamātmane namaḥ
śrīmadbhagavadgītā
atha trayodaśo'dhyāyaḥ

śrībhagavānuvāca
idaṃ(m)   śarīraṃ(ṅ)   kaunteya,   kṣetramityabhidhīyate।
etadyo   vetti   taṃ(m)  prāhuḥ,   kṣetrajña   iti   tadvidaḥ॥13.1॥
column4:
Śrī Bhagavān said: 
This body Arjuna, is termed as the Field (kṣetra) and he knows it, is called the knower of the field (kṣetrajna) by the sages discerning the truth about both.
column5: Śrī Bhagavān said: This body Arjuna, is termed as the Field (kṣetra) and he knows it, is called the knower of the field (kṣetrajna) by the sages discerning the truth about both.

column3:
kṣetrajñaṃ(ñ)   cāpi   māṃ(v̐)   viddhi,   sarvakṣetreṣu bhārata।
kṣetrakṣetrajñayorjñānaṃ(y̐), yattajjñānaṃ(m) mataṃ(m) mama॥13.2॥
column4: Know Myself to be the kṣetrajna (individual soul) in all the kṣetras (fields), Arjuna. And it is the knowledge of the field (kṣetra), and knower (kṣetrajna), (ie. of Matter with its evolutes and the Spirit) which I consider as true knowledge.
column5: Know Myself to be the kṣetrajna (individual soul) in all the kṣetras (fields), Arjuna. And it is the knowledge of the field (kṣetra), and knower (kṣetrajna), (ie. of Matter with its evolutes and the Spirit) which I consider as true knowledge.

column3:
tatkṣetraṃ(y̐)  yacca  yādṛkca,  yadvikāri  yataśca  yat।
sa   ca   yo   yatprabhāvaśca,   tatsamāsena   me   śṛṇu॥13.3॥
column4: What that Field (kṣetra) is and what is its nature, what are its modifications, and from what causes what effects have arisen, and also who its knower (kṣetrajna) is, and what is His glory - hear all this from Me in brief.
column5: What that Field (kṣetra) is and what is its nature, what are its modifications, and from what causes what effects have arisen, and also who its knower (kṣetrajna) is, and what is His glory - hear all this from Me in brief.

column3:
ṛṣibhirbahudhā gītaṃ(ñ) chandobhirvividhaiḥ(ph) pṛthak।
brahmasūtrapadaiścaiva,        hetumadbhirviniścitaiḥ॥13.4॥
column4: The truth, about the kṣetra and kṣetrajna, has been expounded by the seers in manifold ways; again it has been stated separately in different Vedic chants and also in the conclusive and reasoned texts of the Brahmasūtras.
column5: The truth, about the kṣetra and kṣetrajna, has been expounded by the seers in manifold ways; again it has been stated separately in different Vedic chants and also in the conclusive and reasoned texts of the Brahmasūtras.

column3:
mahābhūtānyahaṅkāro,    buddhiravyaktameva   ca।
indriyāṇi  daśaikaṃ(ñ)  ca,  pañca  cendriyagocarāḥ॥13.5॥
column4: The five elements, the ego, the intellect, the unmanifest (Primordial Matter), the ten organs of perception and action, the mind and five objects of sense ( sound, touch, colour, taste and smell).
column5: The five elements, the ego, the intellect, the unmanifest (Primordial Matter), the ten organs of perception and action, the mind and five objects of sense ( sound, touch, colour, taste and smell).

column3:
icchā dveṣaḥ(s) sukhaṃ(n) duḥkhaṃ(m), saṅghātaścetanā dhṛtiḥ।
etatkṣetraṃ(m)       samāsena,     savikāramudāhṛtam॥13.6॥
column4: Also desire, aversion, pleasure, pain, the physical body consciousness (life-breath), firmness: these comprise the kṣetra, with its modifications, described briefly.
column5: Also desire, aversion, pleasure, pain, the physical body consciousness (life-breath), firmness: these comprise the kṣetra, with its modifications, described briefly.

column3:
amānitvamadambhitvam,     ahiṃsā     kṣāntirārjavam।
ācāryopāsanaṃ(m)   śaucaṃ(m),  sthairyamātmavinigrahaḥ॥13.7॥
column4: Absence of pride, freedom from hypocrisy, non-violence, forgiveness, uprightness of speech and mind etc, devout service of the perceptor, internal and external purity, steadfastness of mind and control of body, mind and the senses.
column5: Absence of pride, freedom from hypocrisy, non-violence, forgiveness, uprightness of speech and mind etc, devout service of the perceptor, internal and external purity, steadfastness of mind and control of body, mind and the senses.

column3:
indriyārtheṣu         vairāgyam,       anahaṅkāra   eva   ca।
janmamṛtyujarāvyādhi,       duḥkhadoṣānudarśanam॥13.8॥
column4: Dispassion towards the objects of enjoyment of this world and the next, and also absence of egotism;n constant perception of evil and pain and evils inherent in birth, death, old age and disease.
column5: Dispassion towards the objects of enjoyment of this world and the next, and also absence of egotism;n constant perception of evil and pain and evils inherent in birth, death, old age and disease.

column3:
asaktiranabhiṣvaṅgaḥ(ph),         putradāragṛhādiṣu।
nityaṃ(ñ)     ca     samacittatvam,     iṣṭāniṣṭopapattiṣu॥13.9॥
column4: Absence of attachment and the sense of mineness in respect of son, wife, home etc., and constant equipoise of mind both in favourable and unfavourable circumstances.
column5: Absence of attachment and the sense of mineness in respect of son, wife, home etc., and constant equipoise of mind both in favourable and unfavourable circumstances.

column3:
mayi         cānanyayogena,         bhaktiravyabhicāriṇī।
viviktadeśasevitvam,              aratirjanasaṃsadi॥13.10॥
column4: Unflinching devotion in Me through exclusive attachment, living in secluded and holy places, and finding no delight in the company of worldly people;
column5: Unflinching devotion in Me through exclusive attachment, living in secluded and holy places, and finding no delight in the company of worldly people;

column3:
adhyātmajñānanityatvaṃ(n),      tattvajñānārthadarśanam।
etajjñānamiti      proktam,      ajñānaṃ(y̐)    yadato'nyathā॥13.11॥
column4: Constancy, in Self-knowledge and seeing God everywhere as the object of true knowledge-all this is declared to be knowledge, and what is contraryto it, is called ignorance.
column5: Constancy, in Self-knowledge and seeing God everywhere as the object of true knowledge-all this is declared to be knowledge, and what is contraryto it, is called ignorance.

column3:
jñeyaṃ(y̐)      yattatpravakṣyāmi,yajjñātvāmṛtamaśnute।
anādimatparaṃ(m)      brahma,     nasattannāsaducyate॥13.12॥
column4: I shall speak to you at length about which ought to be known, and knowing which one attains Supreme Bliss. That supreme Brahma, who is the lord of the (two) beginningless entities - ( Prakṛti and Jiva) is said to be neither Sat (being) nor Asat (non-being)
column5: I shall speak to you at length about which ought to be known, and knowing which one attains Supreme Bliss. That supreme Brahma, who is the lord of the (two) beginningless entities - (Prakṛti and Jiva) is said to be neither Sat (being) nor Asat (non-being)

column3:
sarvataḥ(ph)     pāṇipādaṃ(n)  tat,sarvato'kṣiśiromukham।
sarvataḥ(ś)       śrutimalloke,    sarvamāvṛtyatiṣṭhati॥13.13॥
column4: It has hands and feet on all sides, eyes, head and mouth in all directions, and ears all around; for it stands pervading all in the universe.
column5: It has hands and feet on all sides, eyes, head and mouth in all directions, and ears all around; for it stands pervading all in the universe.

column3:
sarvendriyaguṇābhāsaṃ(m),         sarvendriyavivarjitam।
asaktaṃ(m)   sarvabhṛccaiva,   nirguṇaṃ(ṅ)    guṇabhoktṛ  ca॥13.14॥
column4: Through perceiving all sense objects, it is really speaking devoid of all senses. Nay, though unattached, It is the sustainer of all nonetheless; and though attributeless, It is the enjoyer of Guṇas, the three modes of Prakṛti.
column5: Through perceiving all sense objects, it is really speaking devoid of all senses. Nay, though unattached, It is the sustainer of all nonetheless; and though attributeless, It is the enjoyer of Guṇas, the three modes of Prakṛti.

column3:
bahirantaśca     bhūtānām,    acaraṃ(ñ)    carameva   ca।
sūkṣmatvāttadavijñeyaṃ(n),   dūrasthaṃ(ñ)   cāntike   ca   tat॥13.15॥
column4: He exists, without and within, all beings and constitutes the moving and the unmoving creation as well. And by reason of its subtlety, it is incomprehensible. It is close at hand and stands afar, too.
column5: He exists, without and within, all beings and constitutes the moving and the unmoving creation as well. And by reason of its subtlety, it is incomprehensible. It is close at hand and stands afar, too.

column3:
avibhaktaṃ(ñ)    ca   bhūteṣu,    vibhaktamiva    ca    sthitam।
bhūtabhartṛ   ca   tajjñeyaṃ(ṅ),   grasiṣṇu   prabhaviṣṇu   ca॥13.16॥
