column4: Though integral like space in its undivided aspect, it appears divided as it were, in all animate and all inanimate beings. And the Godhead, which is the only object worth knowing, is the sustainer of beings (as Viṣṇu), the destroyer (as Rudra) and the creator of all (as Brahma).
column5: Though integral like space in its undivided aspect, it appears divided as it were, in all animate and all inanimate beings. And the Godhead, which is the only object worth knowing, is the sustainer of beings (as Viṣṇu), the destroyer (as Rudra) and the creator of all (as Brahma).

column3:
jyotiṣāmapi   tajjyotiḥ(s),   tamasaḥ(ph)   paramucyate।
jñānaṃ(ñ) jñeyaṃ(ñ) jñānagamyaṃ(m), hṛdi sarvasya viṣṭhitam॥13.17॥
column4: That supreme Brahma is said to be the light of all lights, and entirely beyond Maya. That Godhead is knowledge itself, worth knowing and worth attaining through real wisdom, and is particularly abiding in the hearts of all.
column5: That supreme Brahma is said to be the light of all lights, and entirely beyond Maya. That Godhead is knowledge itself, worth knowing and worth attaining through real wisdom, and is particularly abiding in the hearts of all.

column3:
iti kṣetraṃ(n)  tathā  jñānaṃ(ñ),  jñeyaṃ(ñ) coktaṃ(m) samāsataḥ।
madbhakta      etadvijñāya,    madbhāvāyopapadyate॥13.18॥
column4: Thus the truth of the kṣetra (body) and knowledge, as well as of the object worth knowing, ie., God, has been briefly described; and knowing  this in reality, My devotee attains to My being.
column5: Thus the truth of the kṣetra (body) and knowledge, as well as of the object worth knowing, ie., God, has been briefly described; and knowing  this in reality, My devotee attains to My being.

column3:
prakṛtiṃ(m)   puruṣaṃ(ñ)   caiva,   viddhyanādī   ubhāvapi।
vikārāṃśca      guṇāṃścaiva,   viddhi  prakṛtisambhavān॥13.19॥
column4: Prakṛti (matter) and Puruṣa (Spirit), know both these as beginningless. And know all modifications such as likes and dislikes etc., and all objects constituted of the three Guṇas as born of Prakṛti.
column5: Prakṛti (matter) and Puruṣa (Spirit), know both these as beginningless. And know all modifications such as likes and dislikes etc., and all objects constituted of the three Guṇas as born of Prakṛti.

column3:
kāryakaraṇakartṛtve,     hetuḥ(ph)     prakṛtirucyate।
puruṣaḥ(s)   sukhaduḥkhānāṃ(m),   bhoktṛtve  heturucyate॥13.20॥
column4: Prakṛti is said to be responsible for bringing forth the evolutes and the instruments; while the individual soul is declared to be responsible for the experience of joys and sorrows.
column5: Prakṛti is said to be responsible for bringing forth the evolutes and the instruments; while the individual soul is declared to be responsible for the experience of joys and sorrows.

column3:
puruṣaḥ(ph)   prakṛtistho   hi,   bhuṅkte   prakṛtijānguṇān।
kāraṇaṃ(ṅ)    guṇasaṅgo'sya,    sadasadyonijanmasu॥13.21॥
column4: Only the Puruṣa in association with Prakṛti experiences objects of the nature of the three Guṇas evolved from Prakṛti and it is attachment with these Guṇas that is responsible for the birth of this soul in good and evil wombs.
column5: Only the Puruṣa in association with Prakṛti experiences objects of the nature of the three Guṇas evolved from Prakṛti and it is attachment with these Guṇas that is responsible for the birth of this soul in good and evil wombs.

column3:
upadraṣṭānumantā    ca,    bhartā    bhoktā   maheśvaraḥ।
paramātmeti   cāpyukto,   dehe'sminpuruṣaḥ(ph)  paraḥ॥13.22॥
column4: The Spirit dwelling in this body, is really the same as the Supreme. He has been spoken of as the Witness, the true Guide, the Sustainer of all, the Experiencer (as the embodied soul), the Overlord and the Absolute as well.
column5: The Spirit dwelling in this body, is really the same as the Supreme. He has been spoken of as the Witness, the true Guide, the Sustainer of all, the Experiencer (as the embodied soul), the Overlord and the Absolute as well.

column3:
ya evaṃ(v̐) vetti puruṣaṃ(m), prakṛtiṃ(ñ) ca guṇaiḥ(s) saha।
sarvathā      vartamāno'pi,    na     sa    bhūyo'bhijāyate॥13.23॥
column4: He who thus knows the Puruṣa (Spirit) and Prakṛti (Nature) together with the Guṇas-even though performing his duties in everyway, is not born again.
column5: He who thus knows the Puruṣa (Spirit) and Prakṛti (Nature) together with the Guṇas-even though performing his duties in everyway, is not born again.

column3:
dhyānenātmani        paśyanti,      kecidātmānamātmanā।
anye   sāṅkhyena    yogena,    karmayogena   cāpare॥13.24॥
column4: Some by meditation behold the supreme Spirit in the heart with the help of their refined and sharp intellect; others realize it through the discipline of Knowledge, and still others, through the discipline of Action, i.e., Karmayoga.
column5: Some by meditation behold the supreme Spirit in the heart with the help of their refined and sharp intellect; others realize it through the discipline of Knowledge, and still others, through the discipline of Action, i.e., Karmayoga.

column3:
anye    tvevamajānantaḥ(ś),    śrutvānyebhya    upāsate।
te'pi      cātitarantyeva,     mṛtyuṃ(m)     śrutiparāyaṇāḥ॥13.25॥
column4: Other dull-witted persons, however, not knowing thus, worship even as they have heard from others i.e., the knowers of truth; and even those who are thus devoted to what they have heard, are able to cross the ocean of mundane existence in the shape of death.
column5: Other dull-witted persons, however, not knowing thus, worship even as they have heard from others i.e., the knowers of truth; and even those who are thus devoted to what they have heard, are able to cross the ocean of mundane existence in the shape of death.

yāvatsañjāyate  kiñcit,  sattvaṃ(m)  sthāvarajaṅgamam।
kṣetrakṣetrajñasaṃyogāt,   tadviddhi   bharatarṣabha॥13.26॥
column4: Arjuna, whatsoever being, the moving or unmoving, is born, know it as emanated through the union of kṣetra (Matter) and the kṣetrajna (Spirit).
column5: Arjuna, whatsoever being, the moving or unmoving, is born, know it as emanated through the union of kṣetra (Matter) and the kṣetrajna (Spirit).

column3:
samaṃ(m)  sarveṣu  bhūteṣu, tiṣṭhantaṃ(m) parameśvaram।
vinaśyatsvavinaśyantaṃ(y̐), yaḥ(ph) paśyati sa paśyati॥13.27॥
column4: He alone truly sees, who sees the supreme Lord as imperishable and abiding equally in all perishable beings, both animate and inanimate.
column5: He alone truly sees, who sees the supreme Lord as imperishable and abiding equally in all perishable beings, both animate and inanimate.

column3:
samaṃ(m)   paśyanhi   sarvatra,   samavasthitamīśvaram।
na  hinastyātmanātmānaṃ(n),  tato  yāti  parāṃ(ṅ)  gatim॥13.28॥
column4: For, by seeing the Supreme Lord equally present in all, he does not kill the Self by himself, and there by attains the supreme state.
column5: For, by seeing the Supreme Lord equally present in all, he does not kill the Self by himself, and there by attains the supreme state.

column3:
prakṛtyaiva   ca   karmāṇi,   kriyamāṇāni   sarvaśaḥ।
yaḥ(ph) paśyati tathātmānam, akartāraṃ(m) sa paśyati॥13.29॥
column4: He who sees that all actions are performed in every way by nature (Prakṛti) and the Self as the non-doer, he alone verily sees.
column5: He who sees that all actions are performed in every way by nature (Prakṛti) and the Self as the non-doer, he alone verily sees.

column3:
yadā    bhūtapṛthagbhāvam,    ekasthamanupaśyati।
tata   eva   ca   vistāraṃ(m),   brahma   sampadyate   tadā॥13.30॥
column4: The moment man perceives the diversified existence of beings as rooted in the one supreme Spirit, and the spreading forth of all beings from the same, that very moment he attains Brahma (who is Truth, Consciousness and Bliss solidified).
column5: The moment man perceives the diversified existence of beings as rooted in the one supreme Spirit, and the spreading forth of all beings from the same, that very moment he attains Brahma (who is Truth, Consciousness and Bliss solidified).

column3:
anāditvānnirguṇatvāt,        paramātmāyamavyayaḥ।
śarīrastho'pi    kaunteya,    na    karoti    na    lipyate॥13.31॥
column4: Arjuna, being without beginning and without attributes, this indestructible supreme Spirit, though dwelling in the body, in fact does nothing, nor gets tainted.
column5: Arjuna, being without beginning and without attributes, this indestructible supreme Spirit, though dwelling in the body, in fact does nothing, nor gets tainted.

column3:
yathā sarvagataṃ(m) saukṣmyād, ākāśaṃ(n) nopalipyate।
sarvatrāvasthito     dehe,    tathātmā    nopalipyate॥13.32॥
column4: As the all-pervading ether is not contaminated by reason of its subtlety, though permeating the body, the Self is not affected by the attributes of the body due to Its attributeless character.
column5: As the all-pervading ether is not contaminated by reason of its subtlety, though permeating the body, the Self is not affected by the attributes of the body due to Its attributeless character.

column3:
yathā prakāśayatyekaḥ(kh), kṛtsnaṃ(l̐) lokamimaṃ(m) raviḥ।
kṣetraṃ(ṅ) kṣetrī tathā kṛtsnaṃ(m), prakāśayati bhārata॥13.33॥
column4: Arjuna, as the one sun illumines this entire universe, so the one Atṃā (Spirit) illumines the whole kṣetra (Field).
column5: Arjuna, as the one sun illumines this entire universe, so the one Atṃā (Spirit) illumines the whole kṣetra (Field).

column3:
kṣetrakṣetrajñayorevam,    antaraṃ(ñ)    jñānacakṣuṣā।
bhūtaprakṛtimokṣaṃ(ñ)  ca,  ye  viduryānti  te  param॥13.34॥
column4: Those who thus perceive with the eye of wisdom, the difference between the kṣetra and kṣetrajna, and the phenomenon of liberation from Prakṛti with her evolutes, reach the supreme eternal Spirit.
column5: Those who thus perceive with the eye of wisdom, the difference between the kṣetra and kṣetrajna, and the phenomenon of liberation from Prakṛti with her evolutes, reach the supreme eternal Spirit.

column3:
Om tatsaditi śrīmadbhagavadgītāsu upaniṣatsu brahmavidyāyāṃ(y̐)
yogaśāstre śrīkṛṣṇārjunasaṃvāde kṣetrakṣetrajñavibhāgayogo nāma
trayodaśo'dhyāyaḥ ||

column4:
Thus, in the Upanisad sung by the Lord, the Science of Brahma, the scripture of Yoga, the
dialogue between Śrī Kṛṣṇā and Arjuna, ends the thirteenth chapter entitled "The Yoga of
discrimination between the Field and the Knower of the Field."
column5:
Thus, in the Upanisad sung by the Lord, the Science of Brahma, the scripture of Yoga, the

column3:

śrīmadbhagavadgītā

śrībhagavānuvāca 
paraṃ(m) bhūyaḥ(ph) pravakṣyāmi, jñānānāṃ(ñ) jñānamuttamam।
yajjñātvā  munayaḥ(s)  sarve,  parāṃ(m)  siddhimito  gatāḥ॥14.1॥
column4: Śrī Bhagavān said :I shall expound once more the supreme knowledge, the best of all knowledge, acquiring which all sages have attained highest perfection, being liberated from this mundane existence.
column5:
Śrī Bhagavān said :
I shall expound once more the supreme knowledge, the best of all knowledge, acquiring which all sages have attained highest perfection, being liberated from this mundane existence.

column3:
idaṃ(ñ)  jñānamupāśritya,  mama  sādharmyamāgatāḥ।
sarge'pi   nopajāyante,   pralaye   na   vyathanti   ca॥14.2॥
column4: Those who, by practising this knowledge have entered into My being, are not born again at the cosmic dawn, nor feel disturbed even during the cosmic dissolution (Pralaya).
column5: Those who, by practising this knowledge have entered into My being, are not born again at the cosmic dawn, nor feel disturbed even during the cosmic dissolution (Pralaya).

column3:
mama yonirmahadbrahma, tasmingarbhaṃ(n) dadhāmyaham।
sambhavaḥ(s) sarvabhūtānāṃ(n), tato bhavati bhārata॥14.3॥
column4: My primordial Nature, known as the great Brahma, is the womb of all creatures; in that womb I place the seed of all life. The creation of all beings follows from that union of Matter and Spirit, O Arjuna.
column5: My primordial Nature, known as the great Brahma, is the womb of all creatures; in that womb I place the seed of all life. The creation of all beings follows from that union of Matter and Spirit, O Arjuna.

column3:
sarvayoniṣu kaunteya, mūrtayaḥ(s) sambhavanti yāḥ।
tāsāṃ(m) brahma mahadyoniḥ(r), ahaṃ(m) bījapradaḥ(ph) pitā॥14.4॥
column4: Of all embodied beings that appear in all the species of various kinds, Arjuna, Prakṛti or Nature is the conceiving Mother, while I am the seed giving Father.
column5: Of all embodied beings that appear in all the species of various kinds, Arjuna, Prakṛti or Nature is the conceiving Mother, while I am the seed giving Father.

column3:
sattvaṃ(m)  rajastama  iti,  guṇāḥ(ph)  prakṛtisambhavāḥ।
nibadhnanti   mahābāho,  dehe   dehinamavyayam॥14.5॥
column4: Sattva, Rajas and Tamas-these three Guņas born of Nature tie down the imperishable soul to the body, Arjuna.
column5: Sattva, Rajas and Tamas-these three Guņas born of Nature tie down the imperishable soul to the body, Arjuna.

column3:
tatra  sattvaṃ(n)  nirmalatvāt,  prakāśakamanāmayam।
sukhasaṅgena   badhnāti,   jñānasaṅgena   cānagha॥14.6॥
column4: Of these, Sattva being immaculate, is illuminating and flawless, Arjuna; it binds through attachment to happiness and knowledge.
column5: Of these, Sattva being immaculate, is illuminating and flawless, Arjuna; it binds through attachment to happiness and knowledge.

column3:
rajo  rāgātmakaṃ(v̐)  viddhi,  tṛṣṇāsaṅgasamudbhavam।
tannibadhnāti   kaunteya,   karmasaṅgena   dehinam॥14.7॥
column4: Arjuna, know the quality of Rajas, which is of the nature of passion, as born of desire and attachment. It binds the soul through attachment to actions and their fruit.
column5: Arjuna, know the quality of Rajas, which is of the nature of passion, as born of desire and attachment. It binds the soul through attachment to actions and their fruit.

column3:
tamastvajñānajaṃ(v̐)  viddhi,  mohanaṃ(m)  sarvadehinām।
pramādālasyanidrābhiḥ(s),    tannibadhnāti    bhārata॥14.8॥
column4: And know Tamas, the deluder of all those who look upon the body as their own self, as born of ignorance. It binds the soul through error, sleep and sloth,  Arjuna.
column5: And know Tamas, the deluder of all those who look upon the body as their own self, as born of ignorance. It binds the soul through error, sleep and sloth,  Arjuna.

column3:
sattvaṃ(m)  sukhe  sañjayati,  rajaḥ(kh)  karmaṇi  bhārata।
jñānamāvṛtya   tu   tamaḥ(ph),   pramāde   sañjayatyuta॥14.9॥
column4: Sattva draws one to joy and Rajas to action; while Tamas, clouding wisdom, impels one to error, sleep and sloth Arjuna.
column5: Sattva draws one to joy and Rajas to action; while Tamas, clouding wisdom, impels one to error, sleep and sloth Arjuna.

column3:
rajastamaścābhibhūya,  sattvaṃ(m)  bhavati  bhārata।
rajaḥ(s) sattvaṃ(n) tamaścaiva, tamaḥ(s) sattvaṃ(m) rajastathā॥14.10॥
column4: Overpowering Rajas and Tamas, Arjuna, Sattva prevails; overpowering Sattva and Tamas, Rajas prevails; even so, overpowering Sattva and Rajas, Tamas prevails.
column5: Overpowering Rajas and Tamas, Arjuna, Sattva prevails; overpowering Sattva and Tamas, Rajas prevails; even so, overpowering Sattva and Rajas, Tamas prevails.

column3:
sarvadvāreṣu     dehe'smin,     prakāśa    upajāyate।
jñānaṃ(y̐)  yadā  tadā  vidyād,  vivṛddhaṃ(m)  sattvamityuta॥14.11॥
column4: When light and discernment dawn in this body, as well as in the mind and senses, then one should know that Sattva is predominant.
column5: When light and discernment dawn in this body, as well as in the mind and senses, then one should know that Sattva is predominant.

column3:
lobhaḥ(ph) pravṛttirārambhaḥ(kh), karmaṇāmaśamaḥ(s) spṛhā।
rajasyetāni      jāyante,    vivṛddhe     bharatarṣabha॥14.12॥
column4: With the preponderance of Rajas, Arjuna, greed, activity, undertaking of action with an interested motive, restlessness and a thirst for enjoyment make their appearance.
column5: With the preponderance of Rajas, Arjuna, greed, activity, undertaking of action with an interested motive, restlessness and a thirst for enjoyment make their appearance.

column3:
aprakāśo'pravṛttiśca,    pramādo    moha   eva   ca।
tamasyetāni    jāyante,    vivṛddhe    kurunandana॥14.13॥
column4: With the growth of Tamas, Arjuna, obtuseness of the mind and senses, disinclination to perform one's obligatory duties, frivolity and stupor-all these appear.
column5: With the growth of Tamas, Arjuna, obtuseness of the mind and senses, disinclination to perform one's obligatory duties, frivolity and stupor-all these appear.

column3:
yadā  sattve  pravṛddhe  tu,  pralayaṃ(y̐)  yāti  dehabhṛt।
tadottamavidāṃ(l̐)     lokān,     amalānpratipadyate॥14.14॥
column4: When a man dies during the preponderance of Sattva, he obtains the stainless ethereal worlds (heaven etc.,) attained by men of noble deeds.
column5: When a man dies during the preponderance of Sattva, he obtains the stainless ethereal worlds (heaven etc.,) attained by men of noble deeds.

column3:
rajasi   pralayaṃ(ṅ)   gatvā,   karmasaṅgiṣu   jāyate।
tathā    pralīnastamasi,    mūḍhayoniṣu    jāyate॥14.15॥
column4: Dying when Rajas predominates, he is born among those attached to action; even so, the man who has expired during the preponderance of Tamas is reborn in the species of the deluded creatures such as insects and beasts etc.
column5: Dying when Rajas predominates, he is born among those attached to action; even so, the man who has expired during the preponderance of Tamas is reborn in the species of the deluded creatures such as insects and beasts etc.

column3:
karmaṇaḥ(s) sukṛtasyāhuḥ(s), sāttvikaṃ(n) nirmalaṃ(m) phalam।
rajasastu phalaṃ(n) duḥkham, ajñānaṃ(n) tamasaḥ(ph) phalam॥14.16॥
column4: The reward of a righteous act, they say, is  Sāttvika i.e., faultless in the form of joy, wisdom and dispassion etc., sorrow is declared to be the fruit of a Rājasikā act and ignorance, the fruit of a Tāmasika act.
column5: The reward of a righteous act, they say, is  Sāttvika i.e., faultless in the form of joy, wisdom and dispassion etc., sorrow is declared to be the fruit of a Rājasikā act and ignorance, the fruit of a Tāmasika act.

column3:
sattvātsañjāyate   jñānaṃ(m),   rajaso   lobha   eva   ca।
pramādamohau   tamaso,    bhavato'jñānameva    ca॥14.17॥
column4: Wisdom follows from Sattva, and greed, undoubtedly, from Rajas; likewise, obstinate error, stupor and also ignorance follow from Tamas.
column5: Wisdom follows from Sattva, and greed, undoubtedly, from Rajas; likewise, obstinate error, stupor and also ignorance follow from Tamas.

column3:
ūrdhvaṃ(ṅ) gacchanti sattvasthā, madhye tiṣṭhanti rājasāḥ।
jaghanyaguṇavṛttisthā,  adho  gacchanti  tāmasāḥ॥14.18॥
column4: Those who abide in the quality of Sattva wend their way upwards; while those of a Rājasikā disposition stay in the middle. And those of a Tāmasika temperament, enveloped as they are in the effects of Tamoguņa, sink down.
column5: Those who abide in the quality of Sattva wend their way upwards; while those of a Rājasikā disposition stay in the middle. And those of a Tāmasika temperament, enveloped as they are in the effects of Tamoguņa, sink down.

column3:
nānyaṃ(ṅ) guṇebhyaḥ(kh) kartāraṃ(y̐), yadā draṣṭānupaśyati।
guṇebhyaśca paraṃ(v̐) vetti, madbhāvaṃ(m) so'dhigacchati॥14.19॥
column4: When the discerning person sees no one as doer other than the three Guṇas, and realizes Me, the supreme Spirit standing entirely beyond these Guṇas, he enters into My being.
column5: When the discerning person sees no one as doer other than the three Guṇas, and realizes Me, the supreme Spirit standing entirely beyond these Guṇas, he enters into My being.

column3:
guṇānetānatītya   trīn,   dehī   dehasamudbhavān।
janmamṛtyujarāduḥkhaiḥ(r),     vimukto'mṛtamaśnute॥14.20॥
column4: Having transcended the aforesaid three Guṇas, which have caused the body, and freed from birth, death, old age and all kinds of sorrow, the embodied soul attains supreme bliss.
column5: Having transcended the aforesaid three Guṇas, which have caused the body, and freed from birth, death, old age and all kinds of sorrow, the embodied soul attains supreme bliss.

column3:

kairliṅgaistrīnguṇānetān,    atīto    bhavati   prabho।
kimācāraḥ(kh)  kathaṃ(ñ)   caitāṃs,   trīnguṇānativartate॥14.21॥
column4: Arjuna said :What are the marks of him who has risen above the three Guṇas, and what is his conduct? And how, Lord, does he rise above the three Guṇas?
column5:
Arjuna said : 
What are the marks of him who has risen above the three Guṇas, and what is his conduct? And how, Lord, does he rise above the three Guṇas?

column3:

prakāśaṃ(ñ)  ca  pravṛttiṃ(ñ)  ca,  mohameva  ca  pāṇḍava।
na   dveṣṭi   saṃpravṛttāni,   na   nivṛttāni   kāṅkṣati॥14.22॥
column4: Śrī Bhagavān said :Arjuna, he who abhors not illumination (which is born of Sattva) and activity (which is born of Rajas) and even stupor (which is born of Tamas), when prevalent, nor longs for them when they have ceased.
column5:

 Arjuna, he who abhors not illumination (which is born of Sattva) and activity (which is born of Rajas) and even stupor (which is born of Tamas), when prevalent, nor longs for them when they have ceased.

column3:
udāsīnavadāsīno,    guṇairyo    na    vicālyate।
guṇā   vartanta    ityeva,    yo'vatiṣṭhati    neṅgate॥14.23॥
column4: He who, sitting like a witness, is not disturbed by the Guṇas, and who, knowing that the Guṇas alone move among the Guṇas, remains established in identity with God, and never falls off from that state.
column5: He who, sitting like a witness, is not disturbed by the Guṇas, and who, knowing that the Guṇas alone move among the Guṇas, remains established in identity with God, and never falls off from that state

samaduḥkhasukhaḥ(s) svasthaḥ(s), samaloṣṭāśmakāñcanaḥ।
tulyapriyāpriyo dhīraḥ(s), tulyanindātmasaṃstutiḥ॥14.24॥
column4: He who is ever established in the Self, takes pain and pleasure alike, regards a clod of earth, a stone and a piece of gold as equal in value, is possessed of wisdom, accepts the pleasant as well as the unpleasant in the same spirit, and views censure and praise alike.
column5: He who is ever established in the Self, takes pain and pleasure alike, regards a clod of earth, a stone and a piece of gold as equal in value, is possessed of wisdom, accepts the pleasant as well as the unpleasant in the same spirit, and views censure and praise alike.

column3:
mānāpamānayostulyaḥ(s),    tulyo    mitrāripakṣayoḥ।
sarvārambhaparityāgī,   guṇātītaḥ(s)    sa   ucyate॥14.25॥
column4: He who is equipoised in honour or ignominy, is alike towards a friend or an enemy, and has renounced the sense of doership in all undertakings, is said to have risen above the three Guṇas.
column5: He who is equipoised in honour or ignominy, is alike towards a friend or an enemy, and has renounced the sense of doership in all undertakings, is said to have risen above the three Guṇas.

column3:
māṃ(ñ)   ca   yo'vyabhicāreṇa,   bhaktiyogena   sevate।
sa   guṇānsamatītyaitān,   brahmabhūyāya   kalpate॥14.26॥
column4: He too who, constantly worships Me through the Yoga of exclusive devotion-transcending these three Guṇas, he becomes eligible for attaining Brahma.
column5: He too who, constantly worships Me through the Yoga of exclusive devotion-transcending these three Guṇas, he becomes eligible for attaining Brahma.

column3:
brahmaṇo   hi   pratiṣṭhāham,   amṛtasyāvyayasya   ca।
śāśvatasya   ca   dharmasya,   sukhasyaikāntikasya   ca॥14.27॥
column4: For, I am the substratum of the imperishable Brahma, of immortality, of the eternal Dharma and of unending immutable bliss.
column5: For, I am the substratum of the imperishable Brahma, of immortality, of the eternal Dharma and of unending immutable bliss.

śrībhagavānuvāca
ūrdhvamūlamadhaḥ(ś) śākham, aśvatthaṃ(m) prāhuravyayam।
chandāṃsi yasya parṇāni,  yastaṃ(v̐) veda sa vedavit॥15.1॥
column4: Srī Bhagavān said :He who knows the Pīpala tree ( in the form of creation); which is said to be imperishable,with its roots in the Primeval being (God), whose branch is represented by Brahmā (the Creator), and whose leaves are the Vedas, is the knower of the purport of Vedas.
column5:
Srī Bhagavān said: 
He who knows the Pīpala tree (in the form of creation); which is said to be imperishable, with its roots in the Primeval being (God), whose branch is represented by Brahmā (the Creator), and whose leaves are the Vedas, is the knower of the purport of Vedas.

column3:
adhaścordhvaṃ(m) prasṛtāstasya śākhā, 
guṇapravṛddhā    viṣayapravālāḥ।
adhaśca       mūlānyanusantatāni, 
karmānubandhīni     manuṣyaloke॥15.2॥
column4: Fed by the three Guṇas and having sense-objects for their tender leaves, the branches of the aforesaid tree (in the shape of different orders of creation) extend  both upwards and downwards; and it's roots which bind the soul according to its actions in the human body, are spread in all regions, higher as well as lower.
column5: Fed by the three Guṇas and having sense-objects for their tender leaves, the branches of the aforesaid tree (in the shape of different orders of creation) extend  both upwards and downwards; and it's roots which bind the soul according to its actions in the human body, are spread in all regions, higher as well as lower.

column3:
na rūpamasyeha tathopalabhyate, 
nānto na cādirna ca sampratiṣṭhā।
aśvatthamenaṃ(m) suvirūḍhamūlam, 
asaṅgaśastreṇa dṛḍhena chittvā॥15.3॥
column4: The nature of this tree of creation, does not on mature thought, turn out what it is represented to be; for it has neither beginning nor end, nor even stability. Therefore cutting down this Pīpala tree, which is m
column5: The nature of this tree of creation, does not on mature thought, turn out what it is represented to be; for it has neither beginning nor end, nor even stability. Therefore cut down this Pīpala tree, which is most firmly rooted, with the formidable axe of dispassion.

column3:
tataḥ(ph) padaṃ(n) tatparimārgitavyaṃ(y̐), 
yasmingatā na nivartanti bhūyaḥ।
tameva cādyaṃ(m) puruṣaṃ(m) prapadye,
yataḥ(ph) pravṛttiḥ(ph) prasṛtā purāṇī॥15.4॥
column4: Thereafter a man should diligently seek for that supreme state, viz.,God, having attained which they return no more to this world; and having fully resolved that he stands dedicated to the Primeval Being (God Nārāyaṇa) Himself, for whom the flow of this beginningless creation has progressed, he should dwell and meditate on Him.
column5: Thereafter a man should diligently seek for that supreme state, viz.,God, having attained which they return no more to this world; and having fully resolved that he stands dedicated to the Primeval Being (God Nārāyaṇa) Himself, for whom the flow of this beginningless creation has progressed, he should dwell and meditate on Him.

column3:
nirmānamohā         jitasaṅgadoṣā, 
adhyātmanityā       vinivṛttakāmāḥ।
dvandvairvimuktāḥ(s) sukhaduḥkhasañjñaiḥ(r),
gacchantyamūḍhāḥ(ph) padamavyayaṃ(n) tat॥15.5॥
column4: They who are free from pride and delusion, who have conquered the evil of attachment, and are constantly abiding in God, whose cravings have altogether ceased and who are completely immune to all pairs of opposites going by the names of pleasure and pain, and are undeluded, attain that supreme immortal state.
column5: They who are free from pride and delusion, who have conquered the evil of attachment, and are constantly abiding in God, whose cravings have altogether ceased and who are completely immune to all pairs of opposites going by the names of pleasure and pain, and are undeluded, attain that supreme immortal state.

column3:
na   tadbhāsayate   sūryo,   na   śaśāṅko   na   pāvakaḥ।
yadgatvā  na  nivartante,  taddhāma  paramaṃ(m)  mama॥15.6॥
column4: Neither the sun nor the moon nor fire can illumine that supreme self-effulgent state, attaining which they never return to this world; that is My supreme abode.
column5: Neither the sun nor the moon nor fire can illumine that supreme self-effulgent state, attaining which they never return to this world; that is My supreme abode.

column3:
mamaivāṃśo     jīvaloke,     jīvabhūtaḥ(s)     sanātanaḥ।
manaḥ(ṣ)    ṣaṣṭhānīndriyāṇi,    prakṛtisthāni    karṣati॥15.7॥
column4: The eternal Jīvātmā in this body is a fragment of My own Self; and it is that alone which draws around itself the  mind and the five senses, which abide inPrakṛti.
column5: The eternal Jīvātmā in this body is a fragment of My own Self; and it is that alone which draws around itself the  mind and the five senses, which abide in Prakṛti.

column3:
śarīraṃ(y̐)    yadavāpnoti,    yaccāpyutkrāmatīśvaraḥ?
gṛhītvaitāni        saṃyāti,       vāyurgandhānivāśayāt॥15.8॥
column4: Even as the wind wafts scents from their seat, so, too, the Jīvātmā, which is the controller of the body etc., taking the mind and the senses from the body which it leaves behind, forthwith migrates to the body which it acquires.
column5: Even as the wind wafts scents from their seat, so, too, the Jīvātmā, which is the controller of the body etc., taking the mind and the senses from the body which it leaves behind, forthwith migrates to the body which it acquires.

column3:
śrotraṃ(ñ) cakṣuḥ(s) sparśanaṃ(ñ) ca, rasanaṃ(ṅ) ghrāṇameva ca।
adhiṣṭhāya     manaścāyaṃ(v̐),     viṣayānupasevate॥15.9॥
column4: It is while dwelling in the sense of hearing, touch, taste and smell, as well as in the mind, that this Jīvātmā enjoys the objects of senses.
column5: It is while dwelling in the sense of hearing, touch, taste and smell, as well as in the mind, that this Jīvātmā enjoys the objects of senses.

column3:
utkrāmantaṃ(m) sthitaṃ(v̐) vāpi, bhuñjānaṃ(v̐) vā guṇānvitam,।
vimūḍhā     nānupaśyanti,     paśyanti     jñānacakṣuṣaḥ॥15.10॥
column4: The ignorant know not the soul departing from, of dwelling in the body, or enjoying the objects of senses, i.e., even when it is connected with the three Guṇas; only those endowed with the eyes of wisdom are able to realize it.
column5: The ignorant know not the soul departing from, or dwelling in the body, or enjoying the objects of senses, i.e., even when it is connected with the three Guṇas; only those endowed with the eyes of wisdom are able to realize it.

column3:
yatanto yoginaścainaṃ(m), paśyantyātmanyavasthitam।
yatanto'pyakṛtātmāno, nainaṃ(m) paśyantyacetasaḥ॥15.11॥
column4: Striving Yogīs too are able to realise this Self enshrined in their heart. The ignorant, however, whose heart has not been purified, know not this Self in spite of their best endeavours.
column5: Striving Yogīs too are able to realise this Self enshrined in their heart. The ignorant, however, whose heart has not been purified, know not this Self in spite of their best endeavours.

column3:
yadādityagataṃ(n)     tejo,      jagadbhāsayate'khilam,
yaccandramasi    yaccāgnau,    tattejo    viddhi    māmakam.॥15.12॥
column4: The radiance in the sun that illumines the entire world, and that which shines in the moon and that which shines in the fire too, know that radiance to be Mine.
column5: The radiance in the sun that illumines the entire world, and that which shines in the moon and that which shines in the fire too, know that radiance to be Mine.

column3:
gāmāviśya    ca    bhūtāni,    dhārayāmyahamojasā,
puṣṇāmi  cauṣadhīḥ(s)  sarvāḥ(s),  somo  bhūtvā  rasātmakaḥ.॥15.13॥
column4: And permeating the soil, it is I who support all creatures by My vital energy, and becoming the sapful moon, I nourish all plants.
column5: And permeating the soil, it is I who support all creatures by My vital energy, and becoming the sapful moon, I nourish all plants.

column3:
ahaṃ(v̐)   vaiśvānaro   bhūtvā,   prāṇināṃ(n)   dehamāśritaḥ।
prāṇāpānasamāyuktaḥ(ph),  pacāmyannaṃ(ñ)  caturvidham॥15.14॥
column4: Taking the form of fire, as Vaiśvānara, lodged in the body of all creatures and united with the Prāṇa (exhalation) and Apāna (inhalation) breaths, it is I who digest and assimilate the four kinds of food.
column5: Taking the form of fire, as Vaiśvānara, lodged in the body of all creatures and united with the Prāṇa (exhalation) and Apāna (inhalation) breaths, it is I who digest and assimilate the four kinds of food.

column3:
sarvasya cāhaṃ(m) hṛdi sanniviṣṭo, 
mattaḥ(s) smṛtirjñānamapohanaṃ(ñ) ca।
vedaiśca    sarvairahameva    vedyo, 
vedāntakṛdvedavideva       cāham॥15.15॥
column4: It is I who remain seated in the heart of all creatures as the inner controller of all; and it is I who am the source of memory, knowledge and ratiocinative faculty. Again, I am the only object worth knowing through the Vedas; I alone am the origin of Vedānta and the knower of the Vedas too.
column5: It is I who remain seated in the heart of all creatures as the inner controller of all; and it is I who am the source of memory, knowledge and ratiocinative faculty (intellectual capacity). Again, I am the only object worth knowing through the Vedas; I alone am the origin of Vedānta and the knower of the Vedas too.

column3: dvāvimau    puruṣau   loke,   kṣaraścākṣara   eva   ca। kṣaraḥ(s)   sarvāṇi   bhūtāni,   kūṭastho'kṣara    ucyate॥15.16॥
column4: The perishable and the imperishable too-- these are the two kinds of Puruṣas in this world. Of these, the bodies of all beings are spoken of as the perishable; while the Jīvātmā or the embodied soul is called imperishable.
column5: The perishable and the imperishable too-- these are the two kinds of Puruṣas in this world. Of these, the bodies of all beings are spoken of as the perishable; while the Jīvātmā or the embodied soul is called imperishable.

column3:
uttamaḥ(ph) puruṣastvanyaḥ(ph), paramātmetyudāhṛtaḥ।
yo    lokatrayamāviśya,    bibhartyavyaya    īśvaraḥ॥15.17॥
column4: Yet, the Supreme Person is other than these, who, having encompassed all the three worlds, upholds and maintains all, and has been spoken of as the imperishable Lord and the Supreme Spirit.
column5: Yet, the Supreme Person is other than these, who, having encompassed all the three worlds, upholds and maintains all, and has been spoken of as the imperishable Lord and the Supreme Spirit.

column3:
yasmātkṣaramatīto'ham,      akṣarādapi      cottamaḥ।
ato'smi   loke   vede   ca,   prathitaḥ(ph)   puruṣottamaḥ.॥15.18॥
column4: Since I am wholly beyond the perishable world of matter and Kṣetra, and am superior even to the imperishable soul, Jīvātmā, hence I am known as the Puruṣottama, the Supreme Self, in the world as well as in the Vedas.
column5: Since I am wholly beyond the perishable world of matter and Kṣetra, and am superior even to the imperishable soul, Jīvātmā, hence I am known as the Puruṣottama, the Supreme Self, in the world as well as in the Vedas.

column3:
yo    māmevamasammūḍho,    jānāti     puruṣottamam।
sa  sarvavidbhajati  māṃ(m),  sarvabhāvena  bhārata॥15.19॥
column4: Arjuna, the wise man who thus realizes Me as the Supreme Person--knowing all, he constantly worships Me (the all-pervading Lord) with his whole being.
column5: Arjuna, the wise man who thus realizes Me as the Supreme Person--knowing all, he constantly worships Me (the all-pervading Lord) with his whole being.

column3:
iti  guhyatamaṃ(m)  śāstram,  idamuktaṃ(m)  mayānagha,
etadbuddhvā   buddhimānsyāt,   kṛtakṛtyaśca   bhārata.॥15.20॥
column4: Arjuna, this most esoteric teaching has thus been imparted by Me; grasping it in essence man becomes wise and his mission in life is accomplished.
column5: Arjuna, this most esoteric teaching has thus been imparted by Me; grasping it in essence man becomes wise and his mission in life is accomplished.

column3:
om tatsaditi śrīmadbhagavadgītāsu upaniṣatsu brahmavidyāyāṃ(y̐)
yogaśāstre śrīkṛṣṇārjunasaṃvāde puruṣottamayogo nāma 
pañcadaśo'dhyāyaḥ ||

om śrīparamātmane namaḥ
śrīmadbhagavadgītā
atha ṣoḍaśo'dhyāyaḥ

śrībhagavānuvāca 
abhayaṃ(m) sattvasaṃśuddhiḥ(r), jñānayogavyavasthitiḥ,
dānaṃ(n) damaśca yajñaśca, svādhyāyastapa ārjavam  16.1
column4: Śrī Bhagavān said : Absolute fearlessness, perfect purity of mind, constant fixity in the Yoga of meditation for the sake of self-realization, and even so, charity in its  Sāttvika form, control of the senses, worship of God and other deities as well as of one’s elders including the performance of Agnihotra (pouring oblations into the sacred fire) and other sacred duties, study and teaching of the Vedas and other sacred books as well as the chanting of God’s names and glories, suffering hardships for the discharge of one’s sacred obligations and uprightness of mind as well as of the body and senses.
column5: Śrī Bhagavān said : Absolute fearlessness, perfect purity of mind, constant fixity (state of being fixed) in the Yoga of meditation for the sake of self-realization, and even so, charity in its Sāttvika form, control of the senses, worship of God and other deities as well as of one’s elders including the performance of Agnihotra (pouring oblations into the sacred fire) and other sacred duties, study and teaching of the Vedas and other sacred books as well as the chanting of God’s names and glories, suffering hardships for the discharge of one’s sacred obligations and uprightness of mind as well as of the body and senses.

column3:
ahiṃsā satyamakrodhaḥ(s), tyāgaḥ(ś) śāntirapaiśunam,
dayā bhūteṣvaloluptvaṃ(m), mārdavaṃ(m) hrīr acāpalam  16.2
column4: Non-violence in thought, word and deed, truthfulness and geniality of speech, absence of anger even on provocation, disclaiming doership in respect of actions, quietude or composure of mind, abstaining from slander, compassion towards all creatures, absence of attachment to the objects of senses even during their contact to the objects of senses, mildness, a sense of shame in transgressing the scriptures or social conventions, and abstaining from frivolous pursuits;
column5: Non-violence in thought, word and deed, truthfulness and geniality of speech, absence of anger even on provocation, disclaiming doership in respect of actions, quietude or composure of mind, abstaining from slander, compassion towards all creatures, absence of attachment to the objects of senses even during their contact with the senses, mildness, a sense of shame in transgressing the scriptures or social conventions, and abstaining from frivolous pursuits;

column3:
tejaḥ kṣamā dhṛtiḥ(ś) śaucam, adroho nātimānitā,
bhavanti saṃpadaṃ(n) daivīm, abhijātasya bhārata  16.3
column4: Sublimity, forgiveness, fortitude, external purity, bearing enmity to none and absence of self-esteem, these are, O Arjuna, the marks of him, who is born with divine endowments.
column5: Sublimity, forgiveness, fortitude, external purity, bearing enmity to none and absence of self-esteem, these are, O Arjuna, the marks of him, who is born with divine endowments.

column3:
dambho darpo'bhimānaśca, krodhaḥ(ph) pāruṣyameva ca,
ajñānaṃ(ñ) cābhijātasya, pārtha sampadamāsurīm    16.4
column4: Hypocrisy, arrogance, pride and anger, sternness and ignorance too - these are the marks of him, who is born with demoniac properties.
column5: Hypocrisy, arrogance, pride and anger, sternness and ignorance too - these are the marks of him, who is born with demoniac properties.

column3:
daivī sampadvimokṣāya, nibandhāyāsurī matā,
mā śucaḥ(s) saṃpadaṃ(n) daivīm, abhijāto'si pāṇḍava  16.5
column4: The divine endowment has been recognized as conducive to liberation, and the demoniac one as leading to bondage. Grieve not, Arjuna, for you are born with the divine propensities.
column5: The divine endowment has been recognized as conducive to liberation, and the demoniac one as leading to bondage. Grieve not, Arjuna, for you are born with the divine propensities.

column3:
dvau bhūtasargau loke'smin, daiva āsura eva ca,
daivo vistaraśaḥ(ph) prokta, āsuraṃ(m) pārtha me śṛṇu 16.6
column4: There are only two types of men in this world, Arjuna, the one possessing a divine nature and the other possessing a demoniac disposition. Of these, the type possessing divine nature has been dealt with at length; now hear in detail from Me about the type possessing demoniac disposition.
column5: There are only two types of men in this world, Arjuna, the one possessing a divine nature and the other possessing a demoniac disposition. Of these, the type possessing divine nature has been dealt with at length; now hear in detail from Me about the type possessing demoniac disposition.

column3:
pravṛttiṃ(ñ) ca nivṛttiṃ(ñ) ca, janā na vidurāsurāḥ,
na śaucaṃ(n) nāpi cācāro, na satyaṃ(n) teṣu vidyate 16.7
column4: Men possessing a demoniac disposition know not what right activity is, and what right abstinence from activity is. Hence they possess neither purity (external or internal) nor good conduct nor even truthfulness.
column5: Men possessing a demoniac disposition know not what right activity is, and what right abstinence from activity is. Hence they possess neither purity (external or internal) nor good conduct nor even truthfulness.

column3:
asatyamapratiṣṭhaṃ(n) te, jagadāhuranīśvaram, 
aparasparasambhūtaṃ(ṅ), kimanyatkāmahaitukam  16.8
column4: Men of demoniac disposition say this world is without any foundation, absolutely unreal and Godless, brought forth by mutual union of the male and female and hence conceived in lust; what else than this?
column5: Men of demoniac disposition say this world is without any foundation, absolutely unreal and Godless, brought forth by mutual union of the male and female and hence conceived in lust; what else than this?

column3:
etāṃ(n) dṛṣṭimavaṣṭabhya, naṣṭātmāno'lpabuddhayaḥ,
prabhavantyugrakarmāṇaḥ, kṣayāya jagato'hitāḥ  16.9
column4: Clinging to this false view, these slow - witted men of vile disposition and terrible deeds, are enemies of mankind, bent on destruction of the world.
column5: Clinging to this false view, these slow - witted men of vile disposition and terrible deeds, are enemies of mankind, bent on destruction of the world.

column3:
kāmamāśritya duṣpūraṃ(n), dambhamānamadānvitāḥ,
mohādgṛhītvāsadgrāhān, pravartante'śucivratāḥ  16.10
column4: Cherishing insatiable desires and embracing false doctrines through ignorance, these men of impure conduct move in this world, full of hypocrisy, pride and arrogance.
column5: Cherishing insatiable desires and embracing false doctrines through ignorance, these men of impure conduct move in this world, full of hypocrisy, pride and arrogance.

column3:
cintāmaparimeyāṃ(ñ) ca, pralayāntāmupāśritāḥ,
kāmopabhogaparamā, etāvaditi niścitāḥ  16.11
column4: Cherishing insatiable desires and embracing false doctrines through ignorance, these men of impure conduct move in this world, full of hypocrisy, pride and arrogance.
column5: Giving themselves up to innumerable cares ending only with death, they remain devoted to the enjoyment of sensuous pleasures and are firm in their belief that this is the highest limit of joy.

column3:
āśāpāśaśatairbaddhāḥ(kh), kāmakrodhaparāyaṇāḥ,
īhante kāmabhogārtham, anyāyenārthasañcayān  16.12
column4: Held in bondage by hundreds of ties of expectation and wholly giving themselves up to lust and anger, they strive to amass by unfair means hoards of money and other objects for the enjoyment of sensuous pleasures.
column5: Held in bondage by hundreds of ties of expectation and wholly giving themselves up to lust and anger, they strive to amass by unfair means hoards of money and other objects for the enjoyment of sensuous pleasures.

column3:
idamadya mayā labdham, imaṃ(m) prāpsye manoratham,
idamastīdamapi me, bhaviṣyati punardhanam  16.13
column4: They say to themselves, “This much has been secured by me today and now I shall realize this ambition. So much wealth is already with me and yet again this shall be mine”.
column5: They say to themselves, “This much has been secured by me today and now I shall realize this ambition. So much wealth is already with me and yet again this shall be mine”.

column3:
asau mayā hataḥ(ś) śatruḥ(r), haniṣye cāparānapi,
īśvaro'hamahaṃ(m) bhogī, siddho'haṃ(m) balavānsukhī  16.14
column4: “That enemy has been slain by me and I shall kill those others too. I am the Lord of all, and enjoyer of all power. I am endowed with all occult powers, and am mighty and happy.”
column5: “That enemy has been slain by me and I shall kill those others too. I am the Lord of all, and enjoyer of all power. I am endowed with all occult powers, and am mighty and happy.”

column3:
āḍhyo'bhijanavānasmi, ko'nyosti sadṛśo mayā,
yakṣye dāsyāmi modiṣya, ityajñānavimohitāḥ 16.15
column4: “I am wealthy and own a large family; who else is equal to me? I will sacrifice to Gods, will give alms, I will make merry.”
column5: “I am wealthy and own a large family; who else is equal to me? I will sacrifice to Gods, will give alms, I will make merry.” Thus deluded by ignorance, enveloped in the mesh of delusion and addicted to the enjoyment of sensuous pleasures, their mind bewildered by numerous thoughts, these men of devilish disposition fall into the foulest hell. (15-16)

column3:
anekacittavibhrāntā, mohajālasamāvṛtāḥ,
prasaktāḥ(kh) kāmabhogeṣu, patanti narake'śucau  16.16
column4: Thus deluded by ignorance, enveloped in the mesh of delusion and addicted to the enjoyment of sensuous pleasures, their mind bewildered by numerous thoughts, these men of devilish disposition fall into the foulest hell.
column5: “I am wealthy and own a large family; who else is equal to me? I will sacrifice to Gods, will give alms, I will make merry.” Thus deluded by ignorance, enveloped in the mesh of delusion and addicted to the enjoyment of sensuous pleasures, their mind bewildered by numerous thoughts, these men of devilish disposition fall into the foulest hell. (15-16)

column3:
ātmasaṃbhāvitāḥ(s) stabdhā, dhanamānamadānvitāḥ,
yajante nāmayajñaiste, dambhenāvidhipūrVākam  16.17
column4: Intoxicated by wealth and honour, those self-conceited and haughty men perform sacrifices only in name for ostentation, without following the sacred rituals.
column5: Intoxicated by wealth and honour, those self-conceited and haughty men perform sacrifices only in name for ostentation, without following the sacred rituals.

column3:
ahaṅkāraṃ(m) balaṃ(n) darpaṃ(ṅ), kāmaṃ(ṅ) krodhaṃ(ñ) ca saṃśritāḥ,
māmātmaparadeheṣu, pradviṣanto'bhyasūyakāḥ  16.18
column4: Given over to egotism, brute force, arrogance, lust and anger etc., and calumniating others, they despise Me (the in-dweller), dwelling in their own bodies, as in those of others.
column5: Given over to egotism, brute force, arrogance, lust and anger etc., and calumniating others, they despise Me (the in-dweller), dwelling in their own bodies, as in those of others.

column3:
tānahaṃ(n) dviṣataḥ(kh) krūrān, saṃsāreṣu narādhamān,
kṣipāmyajasRāmaśubhān, āsurīṣveva yoniṣu  16.19
column4: Those haters, sinful, cruel and vilest among men, I cast again and again into demoniacal wombs in this world.
column5: Those haters, sinful, cruel and vilest among men, I cast again and again into demoniacal wombs in this world.

column3:
āsurīṃ(y̐) yonimāpannā, mūḍhā janmani janmani,
māmaprāpyaiva kaunteya, tato yāntyadhamāṃ(ṅ) gatim.16.20
column4: Failing to reach Me, Arjuna, those stupid persons are born life after life in demoniac wombs and then verily sink down to a still lower plane.
column5: Failing to reach Me, Arjuna, those stupid persons are born life after life in demoniac wombs and then verily sink down to a still lower plane.

column3:
trividhaṃ(n) narakasyedaṃ(n), dvāraṃ(n) nāśanamātmanaḥ,
kāmaḥ(kh) krodhastathā lobhaḥ(s), tasmādetattrayaṃ(n) tyajet  16.21
column4: Desire, anger and greed – these triple gates of hell, bring about the downfall of the soul. Therefore, one must shun all these three.
column5: Desire, anger and greed – these triple gates of hell, bring about the downfall of the soul. Therefore, one must shun all these three.

column3:
etairvimuktaḥ(kh) kaunteya, tamodvāraistribhirnaraḥ,
ācaratyātmanaḥ(ś) śreyaḥ(s), tato yāti parāṃ(ṅ) gatim  16.22
column4: Freed from these three gates of hell, man works for his own salvation and thereby attains the Supreme goal ie. God.
column5: Freed from these three gates of hell, man works for his own salvation and thereby attains the Supreme goal ie. God.

column3:
yaḥ(ś) śāstravidhimutsṛjya, vartate kāmakārataḥ,
na sa siddhimavāpnoti, na sukhaṃ(n) na parāṃ(ṅ) gatim  16.23
column4: Discarding the injunctions of the scriptures, he who acts in an arbitrary way according to his own sweet will, such a person neither attains occult powers, nor the supreme goal, nor even happiness.
column5: Discarding the injunctions of the scriptures, he who acts in an arbitrary way according to his own sweet will, such a person neither attains occult powers, nor the supreme goal, nor even happiness.

column3:
tasmācchāstraṃ(m) pramāṇaṃ(n) te, kāryākāryavyavasthitau,
Jñātvā śāstravidhānoktaṃ(ṅ), karma kartumihārhasi  16.24
column4: Therefore, the scripture alone is your guide in determining what should be done and what should not be done. Knowing this, you ought to perform only such action as is ordained by the scriptures.
column5: Therefore, the scripture alone is your guide in determining what should be done and what should not be done. Knowing this, you ought to perform only such action as is ordained by the scriptures.

column3:
om tatsaditi śrīmadbhagavadgītāsu upaniṣatsu 
brahmavidyāyāṃ(y̐) yogaśāstre śrīkṛṣṇārjunasaṃvāde 
daivāsurasampadvibhāgayogo nāma ṣoḍaśo'dhyāyaḥ.

om śrīparamātmane namaḥ
śrīmadbhagavadgītā
atha saptadaśo'dhyāyaḥ

arjuna uvāca

ye   śāstravidhimutsṛjya,   yajante   śraddhayānvitāḥ। 
teṣāṃ(n)   niṣṭhā   tu   kā   kṛṣṇa,   sattvamāho   rajastamaḥ॥
17.1॥
column4: Arjuna said:Those, endowed with faith, who worship gods and others, disregarding the injunctions of scriptures, where do they stand, Kṛṣṇa,- in Sattva, Rajas or Tamas.
column5: Arjuna said: Those, endowed with faith, who worship gods and others, disregarding the injunctions of scriptures, where do they stand, Kṛṣṇa,- in Sattva, Rajas or Tamas.

column3:
śrībhagavānuvāca
trividhā   bhavati  śraddhā,  dehināṃ(m)  sā  svabhāvajā। 
sāttvikī   rājasī   caiva,   tāmasī   ceti   tāṃ(m)   śṛṇu॥
17.2॥
column4: Śrī Bhagavān said :That untutored innate faith of men is of three kinds– Sāttvika, Rājasika and Tāmasika. Hear of it from Me.
column5: Śrī Bhagavān said : That untutored innate faith of men is of three kinds– Sāttvika, Rājasika and Tāmasika. Hear of it from Me.

column3:
sattvānurūpā   sarvasya,   śraddhā   bhavati   bhāratav
śraddhāmayo'yaṃ(m) puruṣo, yo yacchraddhaḥ(s) sa eva saḥ॥
17.3॥
column4: The faith of all men conforms to their mental disposition, Arjuna. Faith constitutes a man; whatever the nature of his faith, he is verily that.
column5: The faith of all men conforms to their mental disposition, Arjuna. Faith constitutes a man; whatever the nature of his faith, he is verily that.

column3:
yajante   sāttvikā   devān,   yakṣarakṣāṃsi   rājasāḥ। 
pretānbhūtagaṇāṃścānye,   yajante   tāmasā   janāḥ॥
17.4॥
column4: Men of Sāttvika disposition worship gods; those of Rājasika temperament worship demigods and demons; while others, who are men of Tāmasika disposition, worship the spirits of dead and ghosts.
column5: Men of Sāttvika disposition worship gods; those of Rājasika temperament worship demigods and demons; while others, who are men of Tāmasika disposition, worship the spirits of dead and ghosts.

column3:
aśāstravihitaṃ(ṅ)   ghoraṃ(n),   tapyante   ye   tapo   janāḥ। 
dambhāhaṅkārasaṃyuktāḥ(kh),    kāmarāgabalānvitāḥ॥
17.5॥
column4: Men who practice severe penance of an arbitrary type, not sanctioned by scriptures, and who are full of hypocrisy and egotism are obsessed with desire, attachment and pride of power;
column5: Men who practice severe penance of an arbitrary type, not sanctioned by scriptures, and who are full of hypocrisy and egotism are obsessed with desire, attachment and pride of power;

column3:
karśayantaḥ(ś)  śarīrasthaṃ(m),  bhūtagrāmamacetasaḥ। 
māṃ(ñ) caivāntaḥ(ś) śarīrasthaṃ(n), tānviddhyāsuraniścayān॥
17.6॥
column4: And who emaciate the elements constituting their body as well as Me, the Supreme Spirit, dwelling in their heart- know those senseless people to have a demoniac disposition.
column5: And who emaciate the elements constituting their body as well as Me, the Supreme Spirit, dwelling in their heart- know those senseless people to have a demoniac disposition.

column3:
āhārastvapi   sarvasya,   trividho   bhavati   priyaḥ। 
yajñastapastathā dānaṃ(n), teṣāṃ(m) bhedamimaṃ(m) śṛṇu॥
17.7॥
column4: Food also, which is agreeable to different men according to their innate disposition is of three kinds. and likewise sacrifice, penance and charity too are of three kinds each; hear their distinction as follows.
column5: Food also, which is agreeable to different men according to their innate disposition is of three kinds. and likewise sacrifice, penance and charity too are of three kinds each; hear their distinction as follows.

column3:
āyuḥ(s)    sattvabalārogya,    sukhaprītivivardhanāḥ। 
rasyāḥ(s) snigdhāḥ(s) sthirā hṛdyā, āhārāḥ(s) sāttvikapriyāḥ॥
17.8॥
column4: Foods which promote longevity, intelligence, vigor, health, happiness and cheerfulness, and which are juicy, succulent, substantial and naturally agreeable, are liked by men of Sāttvika nature.
column5: Foods which promote longevity, intelligence, vigor, health, happiness and cheerfulness, and which are juicy, succulent, substantial and naturally agreeable, are liked by men of Sāttvika nature.

column3:
kaṭvamlalavaṇātyuṣṇa,        tīkṣṇarūkṣavidāhinaḥ। 
āhārā       rājasasyeṣṭā,       duḥkhaśokāmayapradāḥ॥
17.9॥
column4: Foods which are bitter, sour, salty, overhot, pungent, dry and burning, and which cause suffering, grief and sickness, are dear to the Rājasika type of men.
column5: Foods which are bitter, sour, salty, overhot, pungent, dry and burning, and which cause suffering, grief and sickness, are dear to the Rājasika type of men.

column3:
yātayāmaṃ(ṅ)  gatarasaṃ(m),  pūti  paryuṣitaṃ(ñ)  ca  yat। 
ucchiṣṭamapi  cāmedhyaṃ(m),  bhojanaṃ(n)  tāmasapriyam॥
17.10॥
column4: Food which is ill-cooked or not fully ripe, insipid, putrid, stale and polluted, and which is impure too, is dear to men of a Tāmasika disposition.
column5: Food which is ill-cooked or not fully ripe, insipid, putrid, stale and polluted, and which is impure too, is dear to men of a Tāmasika disposition.

column3:
aphalākāṅkṣibhiryajño,    vidhidṛṣṭo    ya    ijyate। 
yaṣṭavyameveti   manaḥ(s),   samādhāya   sa   sāttvikaḥ॥
17.11॥
column4: The sacrifice which is offered, as ordained by scriptural injunctions, by men who expect no return and who believe that such sacrifices must be performed, is Sāttvika in character.
column5: The sacrifice which is offered, as ordained by scriptural injunctions, by men who expect no return and who believe that such sacrifices must be performed, is Sāttvika in character.

column3:
abhisandhāya  tu  phalaṃ(n),  dambhārthamapi  caiva  yat। 
ijyate  bharataśreṣṭha,  taṃ(y̐)  yajñaṃ(v̐)  viddhi  rājasam॥
17.12॥
column4: That sacrifice however, which is offered for the sake of mere show or even with an eye to its fruit, know it to be Rājasika, Arjuna.
column5: That sacrifice however, which is offered for the sake of mere show or even with an eye to its fruit, know it to be Rājasika, Arjuna.

column3:
vidhihīnamasṛṣṭānnaṃ(m),     mantrahīnamadakṣiṇam। 
śraddhāvirahitaṃ(y̐)   yajñaṃ(n),   tāmasaṃ(m)   paricakṣate॥
17.13॥
column4: A sacrifice, which is not in conformity with scriptural injunctions, in which no food is offered, and no sacrificial fees are paid, which is without sacred chant of hymns and devoid of faith, is said to be Tāmasika.
column5: A sacrifice, which is not in conformity with scriptural injunctions, in which no food is offered, and no sacrificial fees are paid, which is without sacred chant of hymns and devoid of faith, is said to be Tāmasika.

column3:
devadvijaguruprājña,    pūjanaṃ(m)    śaucamārjavam। 
brahmacaryamahiṃsā    ca,    śārīraṃ(n)   tapa   ucyate॥
17.14॥
column4: Worship of gods, the Brāhamaṇs, one’s guru, elders and great soul, purity, straightforwardness, continence and non-violence –these are called penance of the body.
column5: Worship of gods, the Brāhamaṇs, one’s guru, elders and great soul, purity, straightforwardness, continence and non-violence –these are called penance of the body.

column3:
anudvegakaraṃ(v̐) vākyaṃ(m), satyaṃ(m) priyahitaṃ(ñ) ca yat। 
svādhyāyābhyasanaṃ(ñ)  caiva,  vāṅmayaṃ(n)  tapa  ucyate॥
17.15॥
column4: Words which cause no annoyance to others and are truthful, agreeable and beneficial, as well as the study of Vedas and other  Śāstras and the practice of the chanting of Divine Name— this is known as penance of speech.
column5: Words which cause no annoyance to others and are truthful, agreeable and beneficial, as well as the study of Vedas and other  Śāstras and the practice of the chanting of Divine Name— this is known as penance of speech.

column3:
manaḥ(ph) prasādaḥ(s) saumyatvaṃ(m), maunamātmavinigrahaḥ। 
bhāvasaṃśuddhirityetat,     tapo     mānasamucyate॥
17.16॥
column4: Cheerfulness of mind, placidity, habit of contemplation on God, control of mind and perfect purity of inner feelings–all this is called austerity of the mind..
column5: Cheerfulness of mind, placidity, habit of contemplation on God, control of mind and perfect purity of inner feelings–all this is called austerity of the mind..

column3:
śraddhayā  parayā  taptaṃ(n),  tapastattrividhaṃ(n)  naraiḥ। 
aphalākāṅkṣibhiryuktaiḥ(s),   sāttvikaṃ(m)   paricakṣate॥
17.17॥
column4: This threefold penance performed with supreme faith by Yogīs expecting no return is called Sāttvika.
column5: This threefold penance performed with supreme faith by Yogīs expecting no return is called Sāttvika.

column3:
satkāramānapūjārthaṃ(n),   tapo   dambhena   caiva   yat। 
kriyate  tadiha  proktaṃ(m),  rājasaṃ(ñ)  calamadhruvam॥
17.18॥
column4: The austerity which is performed for the sake of renown, honor or adoration, as well as for any other selfish gain, either in all sincerity or by way of ostentation, and yields an uncertain and momentary fruit, has been spoken of here as Rājasika.
column5: The austerity which is performed for the sake of renown, honor or adoration, as well as for any other selfish gain, either in all sincerity or by way of ostentation, and yields an uncertain and momentary fruit, has been spoken of here as Rājasika.

column3:
mūḍhagrāheṇātmano   yat,   pīḍayā   kriyate   tapaḥ। 
parasyotsādanārthaṃ(v̐)   vā,    tattāmasamudāhṛtam॥
17.19॥
column4: Penance which is resorted to out of foolish obstinacy and is accompanied with self-mortification or is intended to harm others, such penance has been declared as Tāmasika.
column5: Penance which is resorted to out of foolish obstinacy and is accompanied with self-mortification or is intended to harm others, such penance has been declared as Tāmasika.

column3:
dātavyamiti      yaddānaṃ(n),      dīyate'nupakāriṇe। 
deśe  kāle  ca  pātre  ca,  taddānaṃ(m)  sāttvikaṃ(m)  smṛtam॥
17.20॥
column4: A gift which is bestowed with a sense of duty on one from whom no return is expected, at appropriate time and place, and to a deserving person, that gift has been declared as Sāttvika.
column5: A gift which is bestowed with a sense of duty on one from whom no return is expected, at appropriate time and place, and to a deserving person, that gift has been declared as Sāttvika.

column3:
yattu  pratyupakārārthaṃ(m),  phalamuddiśya  vā  punaḥ। 
dīyate  ca  parikliṣṭaṃ(n),  taddānaṃ(m)  rājasaṃ(m)  smṛtam॥
17.21॥
column4: A gift which is bestowed in grudging spirit and with the object of getting a service in return or in the hope of obtaining a reward, is called Rājasika.
column5: A gift which is bestowed in grudging spirit and with the object of getting a service in return or in the hope of obtaining a reward, is called Rājasika.

column3:
adeśakāle     yaddānam,     apātrebhyaśca   dīyate। 
asatkṛtamavajñātaṃ(n),       tattāmasamudāhṛtam॥
17.22॥
column4: A gift which is made without good grace and in disdainful spirit out of time and place and to undeserving persons, is said to be Tāmasika.
column5: A gift which is made without good grace and in disdainful spirit out of time and place and to undeserving persons, is said to be Tāmasika.

column3:
oṃ  tatsaditi  nirdeśo,  brahmaṇastrividhaḥ(s)  smṛtaḥ। 
brāhmaṇāstena   vedāśca,   yajñāśca   vihitāḥ(ph)   purā॥
17.23॥
column4: OM, TAT, and SAT– this has been declared as the triple appellation of Brahma, who is Truth, Consciousness, and Bliss. By that were the Brāhmaṇas and the Vedas as well as sacrifices created at the cosmic dawn.
column5: OM, TAT, and SAT– this has been declared as the triple appellation of Brahma, who is Truth, Consciousness, and Bliss. By that were the Brāhmaṇas and the Vedas as well as sacrifices created at the cosmic dawn.

column3:
tasmādomityudāhṛtya,   yajñadānatapaḥ(kh)   kriyāḥ। 
pravartante   vidhānoktāḥ(s),   satataṃ(m)   brahmavādinām॥
17.24॥
column4: Therefore, acts of sacrifice, charity and austerity as enjoined by sacred precepts are always commenced by noble persons, used to the recitation of Vedic chants, with the invocation of the divine name 'OM'.
column5: Therefore, acts of sacrifice, charity and austerity as enjoined by sacred precepts are always commenced by noble persons, used to the recitation of Vedic chants, with the invocation of the divine name 'OM'.

column3:
tadityanabhisandhāya,  phalaṃ(y̐)  yajñatapaḥ(kh)  kriyāḥ। 
dānakriyāśca   vividhāḥ(kh),   kriyante   mokṣakāṅkṣibhiḥ॥
17.25॥
column4: With the idea that all this belongs to God, who is denoted by the appellation 'TAT', acts of sacrifice and austerity as well as acts of charity of various kinds are performed by seekers of liberation, expecting no return for them.
column5: With the idea that all this belongs to God, who is denoted by the appellation 'TAT', acts of sacrifice and austerity as well as acts of charity of various kinds are performed by seekers of liberation, expecting no return for them.

column3:
sadbhāve   sādhubhāve   ca,   sadityetatprayujyate। 
praśaste   karmaṇi   tathā,   sacchabdaḥ(ph)   pārtha   yujyate॥
17.26॥
column4: The name of God, 'SAT' is used in the sense of reality and goodness. And the word 'SAT' is also used in the sense of a praiseworthy and auspicious action, Arjuna.
column5: The name of God, 'SAT' is used in the sense of reality and goodness. And the word 'SAT' is also used in the sense of a praiseworthy and auspicious action, Arjuna.

column3:
yajñe   tapasi   dāne  ca,  sthitiḥ(s)  saditi  cocyate। 
karma   caiva   tadarthīyaṃ(m),   sadityevābhidhīyate॥
17.27॥
column4: And steadfastness in sacrifice, austerity and charity is likewise spoken of as ‘SAT’ and action for the sake of God is verily termed as ‘SAT’.
column5: And steadfastness in sacrifice, austerity and charity is likewise spoken of as ‘SAT’ and action for the sake of God is verily termed as ‘SAT’.

column3:
aśraddhayā hutaṃ(n) dattaṃ(n), tapastaptaṃ(ṅ) kṛtaṃ(ñ) ca yat। 
asadityucyate   pārtha,   na   ca   tatpretya   no   iha॥
17.28॥
column4: An oblation which is offered, a gift given, an austerity practiced, and whatever good deed is performed, if it is without faith, it is termed as naught i.e., 'asat'; therefore, it is of no avail here or hereafter.
column5: An oblation which is offered, a gift given, an austerity practiced, and whatever good deed is performed, if it is without faith, it is termed as naught i.e., 'asat'; therefore, it is of no avail here or hereafter.

column3:
om tatsaditi śrīmadbhagavadgītāsu upaniṣatsu brahmavidyāyāṃ(y̐) 
yogaśāstre śrīkṛṣṇārjunasaṃvāde śraddhātrayavibhāgayogo nāma
 saptadaśo'dhyāyaḥ ||

om śrīparamātmanenamaḥ
Śrīmadbhagavadgītā
atha aṣṭādaśo'dhyāyaḥ

arjuna uvāca
sannyāsasya      mahābāho,     tattvamicchāmi     veditum।
tyāgasya     ca       hṛṣīkeśa,      pṛthakkeśiniṣūdana॥18.1॥
column4:
Arjuna said: 
O mighty-armed Śrī Kṛṣņa, O inner controller of all, O Slayer of Keśi, I wish to know severally the truth of Samnyāsa as also of Tyāga.
column5: Arjuna said: O mighty-armed Śrī Kṛṣņa, O inner controller of all, O Slayer of Keśi, I wish to know severally the truth of Samnyāsa as also of Tyāga.

column3:
śrībhagavānuvāca
kāmyānāṃ(ṅ) karmaṇāṃ(n) nyāsaṃ(m), sannyāsaṃ(ṅ) kavayo viduḥ।
sarvakarmaphalatyāgaṃ(m), prāhustyāgaṃ(v̐) vicakṣaṇāḥ॥18.2॥
column4:
Śrī Bhagavān said : 
Some sages understand Samnyāsa as the giving up of all actions motivated by desire; and the wise declare that Tyāga consists in relinquishing the fruit of all actions.
column5: Śrī Bhagavān said : Some sages understand Samnyāsa as the giving up of all actions motivated by desire; and the wise declare that Tyāga consists in relinquishing the fruit of all actions.

column3:
tyājyaṃ(n)     doṣavadityeke,     karma     prāhurmanīṣiṇaḥ।
yajñadānatapaḥkarma,      na      tyājyamiti     cāpare॥18.3॥
column4: Some wise men declare that all actions contain a measure of evil, and are, therefore, worth giving up; while others say that acts of sacrifice, charity and penance are not to be shunned.
column5: Some wise men declare that all actions contain a measure of evil, and are, therefore, worth giving up; while others say that acts of sacrifice, charity and penance are not to be shunned.

column3:
niścayaṃ(m) śṛṇu me tatra, tyāge bharatasattama।
tyāgo hi puruṣavyāghra, trividhaḥ(s) samprakīrtitaḥ॥18.4॥
column4: Of Samnyāsa and Tyāga, first hear My conclusion on the subject of renunciation (Tyāga), Arjuna; for renunciation, O tiger among men, has been declared to be of three kinds-Sāttvika, Rājasika and Tāmasika.
column5: Of Samnyāsa and Tyāga, first hear My conclusion on the subject of renunciation (Tyāga), Arjuna; for renunciation, O tiger among men, has been declared to be of three kinds-Sāttvika, Rājasika and Tāmasika.

column3:
yajñadānatapaḥkarma,   na   tyājyaṃ(ṅ)   kāryameva   tat।
yajño dānaṃ(n) tapaścaiva, pāvanāni manīṣiṇām॥18.5॥
column4: Acts of sacrifice, charity and penance are not worth giving up; they must be performed. For sacrifice, charity and penance-all these are purifiers to the wise men.
column5: Acts of sacrifice, charity and penance are not worth giving up; they must be performed. For sacrifice, charity and penance-all these are purifiers to the wise men.

column3:
etānyapi   tu   karmāṇi,   saṅgaṃ(n)   tyaktvā   phalāni   ca।
kartavyānīti  me  pārtha,  niścitaṃ(m)  matamuttamam॥18.6॥
column4: Hence these acts of sacrifice, charity and penance, and all other acts of duty too, must be performed without attachment and expectation of reward : this is My well-considered and supreme verdict, Arjuna.
column5: Hence these acts of sacrifice, charity and penance, and all other acts of duty too, must be performed without attachment and expectation of reward : this is My well-considered and supreme verdict, Arjuna.

column3:
niyatasya   tu   sannyāsaḥ(kh),   karmaṇo   nopapadyate।
mohāttasya parityāgaḥ(s), tāmasaḥ(ph) parikīrtitaḥ॥18.7॥
column4: (Prohibited acts and those that are motivated by desire should, no doubt, be given up). But it is not advisable to abandon a prescribed duty. Such abandonment out of ignorance has been declared as Tāmasika.
column5: (Prohibited acts and those that are motivated by desire should, no doubt, be given up). But it is not advisable to abandon a prescribed duty. Such abandonment out of ignorance has been declared as Tāmasika.

column3:
duḥkhamityeva    yatkarma,    kāyakleśabhayāttyajet।
sa kṛtvā rājasaṃ(n) tyāgaṃ(n), naiva tyāgaphalaṃ(l̐) labhet॥18.8॥
column4: Should anyone give up his duties for fear of physical strain, thinking that all actions are verily painful-practising such Rājasika form of renunciation, he does not reap the fruit of renunciation.
column5: Should anyone give up his duties for fear of physical strain, thinking that all actions are verily painful-practising such Rājasika form of renunciation, he does not reap the fruit of renunciation.

column3:
kāryamityeva   yatkarma,   niyataṃ(ṅ)   kriyate’rjuna।
saṅgaṃ(n) tyaktvā phalaṃ(ñ) caiva, sa tyāgaḥ(s) sāttviko mataḥ॥18.9॥
column4: A prescribed duty which is performed simply because it has to be performed, giving up attachment and fruit, that alone has been recognized as the Sāttvika form of renunciation.
column5: A prescribed duty which is performed simply because it has to be performed, giving up attachment and fruit, that alone has been recognized as the Sāttvika form of renunciation.

column3:
na   dvestyakuśalaṃ(ṅ)   karma,   kuśale  nānuṣajjate।
tyāgī     sattvasamāviṣṭo,     medhāvī    chinnasaṃśayaḥ॥18.10॥
column4: He who has neither aversion for action which is leading to bondage (अकुशल) nor attachment to that which is conducive to blessedness (कुशल)- imbued with the quality of goodness, he has all his doubts resolved, is intelligent and a man of true renunciation.
column5: He who has neither aversion for action which is leading to bondage (अकुशल) nor attachment to that which is conducive to blessedness (कुशल)- imbued with the quality of goodness, he has all his doubts resolved, is intelligent and a man of true renunciation.

column3:
na hi dehabhṛtā śakyaṃ(n), tyaktuṃ(ṅ) karmāṇyaśeṣataḥ।
yastu     karmaphalatyāgī,     sa     tyāgītyabhidhīyate॥18.11॥
column4: Since all actions cannot be given up in their entirety by anyone possessing a body, he alone who renounces the fruit of actions is called a man of renunciation.
column5: Since all actions cannot be given up in their entirety by anyone possessing a body, he alone who renounces the fruit of actions is called a man of renunciation.

column3:
aniṣṭamiṣṭaṃ(m) miśraṃ(ñ) ca, trividhaṃ(ṅ) karmaṇaḥ(ph) phalam।
bhavatyatyāgināṃ(m) pretya, na tu sannyāsināṃ(ṅ) kvacit॥18.12॥
column4: Agreeable, disagreeable and mixed-threefold, indeed, is the fruit that accrues after death from the actions of the unrenouncing. But there is none whatsoever for those who have renounced.
column5: Agreeable, disagreeable and mixed-threefold, indeed, is the fruit that accrues after death from the actions of the unrenouncing. But there is none whatsoever for those who have renounced.

column3:
pañcaitāni   mahābāho,   kāraṇāni   nibodha  me।
sāṅkhye kṛtānte proktāni, siddhaye sarvakarmaṇām॥13॥
column4: In the branch of learning known as Sāṅkhya, which prescribes means for neutralizing all actions, the five factors have been mentioned as contributory to the accomplishment of all actions; know them all from Me, Arjuna.
column5: In the branch of learning known as Sāṅkhya, which prescribes means for neutralizing all actions, the five factors have been mentioned as contributory to the accomplishment of all actions; know them all from Me, Arjuna.

column3:
adhiṣṭhānaṃ (n) tathā  kartā,  karaṇaṃ(ñ)  ca pṛthagvidham।
vividhāśca pṛthakceṣṭā, daivaṃ(ñ) caivātra pañcamam॥18.14॥
column4: The following are the factors operating towards the accomplishment of actions, viz., the body and the doer, the organs of different kinds and the different functions of manifold kinds; and the fifth is Daiva, latencies of past actions.
column5: The following are the factors operating towards the accomplishment of actions, viz., the body and the doer, the organs of different kinds and the different functions of manifold kinds; and the fifth is Daiva, latencies of past actions.

column3:
śarīravāṅmanobhiryat,    karma    prārabhate    naraḥ।
nyāyyaṃ(v̐)  vā viparītaṃ(v̐) vā, pañcaite tasya hetavaḥ॥18.15॥
column4: These five are the contributory causes of whatever actions, prescribed or prohibited, man performs with the mind, speech and body.
column5: These five are the contributory causes of whatever actions, prescribed or prohibited, man performs with the mind, speech and body..

column3:
tatraivaṃ(m) sati kartāram, ātmānaṃ(ṅ) kevalaṃ(n) tu yaḥ।
paśyatyakṛtabuddhitvān,  na  sa  paśyati  durmatiḥ॥18.16॥
column4: Notwithstanding this, however, he who, having an impure mind, regards the absolute, taintless Self alone as the doer, that man of perverse understanding does not view aright.
column5: Notwithstanding this, however, he who, having an impure mind, regards the absolute, taintless Self alone as the doer, that man of perverse understanding does not view aright.

column3:
yasya  nāhaṅkṛto   bhāvo,   buddhiryasya   na  lipyate।
hatvāpi  sa  imāl̐lokān,  na  hanti  na  nibadhyate॥18.17॥
column4: He whose mind is free from the sense of doership, and whose reason is not affected by worldly objects and activities, does not really kill, even having killed all these people, nor does any sin accrue to him.
column5: He whose mind is free from the sense of doership, and whose reason is not affected by worldly objects and activities, does not really kill, even having killed all these people, nor does any sin accrue to him.

column3:
jñānaṃ(ñ)  jñeyaṃ(m)  parijñātā,  trividhā  karmacodanā।
karaṇaṃ(ṅ) karma karteti, trividhaḥ(kh) karmasaṅgrahaḥ॥18.18॥
column4: The Knower, knowledge and the object of knowledge-these three motivate action. Even so, the doer, the organs and activity-these are the three constituents of action.
column5: The Knower, knowledge and the object of knowledge-these three motivate action. Even so, the doer, the organs and activity-these are the three constituents of action.

column3:
jñānaṃ(ṅ) karma ca kartā ca, tridhaiva guṇabhedataḥ।
procyate       guṇasaṅkhyāne,     yathāvacchṛṇu tānyapi॥18.19॥
column4: In the branch of knowledge dealing with the Guņas or modes of Prakṛti, knowledge and action as well as the doer have been declared to be of three kinds according to the Guņa which predominates in each; hear them too duly from Me.
column5: In the branch of knowledge dealing with the Guņas or modes of Prakṛti, knowledge and action as well as the doer have been declared to be of three kinds according to the Guņa which predominates in each; hear them too duly from Me.

column3:
sarvabhūteṣu  yenaikaṃ(m),  bhāvamavyayamīkṣate।
avibhaktaṃ(v̐) vibhakteṣu, tajjñānaṃ(v̐) viddhi sāttvikam॥18.20॥
column4: That by which man perceives one imperishable divine existence as undivided and equally present in all individual beings, know that knowledge to be Sāttvika.
column5: That by which man perceives one imperishable divine existence as undivided and equally present in all individual beings, know that knowledge to be Sāttvika.

column3:
pṛthaktvena tu yajjñānaṃ(n),nānābhāvānpṛthagvidhān।
vetti  sarveṣu  bhūteṣu,  tajjñānaṃ(v̐)  viddhi rājasam॥18.21॥
column4: The knowledge by which man cognizes many existences of various kinds, as apart from one another, in all beings, know that knowledge to be Rājasika.
column5: The knowledge by which man cognizes many existences of various kinds, as apart from one another, in all beings, know that knowledge to be Rājasika.

column3:
yattu  kṛtsnavadekasmin,  kārye  saktamahaitukam।
atattvārthavadalpaṃ(ñ)     ca,    tattāmasamudāhṛtam॥18.22॥
column4: Again, that knowledge which clings to one body as if it were the whole, and which is irrational, has no real grasp of truth and is trivial, has been declared as Tāmasika.
column5: Again, that knowledge which clings to one body as if it were the whole, and which is irrational, has no real grasp of truth and is trivial, has been declared as Tāmasika.

column3:
niyataṃ(m)       saṅgarahitam,     arāgadveṣataḥ(kh)kṛtam।
aphalaprepsunā            karma,    yattatsāttvikamucyate॥18.23॥
column4: That action which is ordained by the scriptures and is not accompanied by the sense of doership, and has been done without any attachment or aversion by one who seeks no return, is called Sāttvika.
column5: That action which is ordained by the scriptures and is not accompanied by the sense of doership, and has been done without any attachment or aversion by one who seeks no return, is called Sāttvika

column3:
yattu     kāmepsunā   karma,   sāhaṅkāreṇa   vā   punaḥ।
kriyate        bahulāyāsaṃ(n),     tadrājasamudāhṛtam॥18.24॥
column4: That action, however, which involves much strain and is performed by one who seeks enjoyments or by a man full of egotism, has beenspoken of as Rājasika.
column5: That action, however, which involves much strain and is performed by one who seeks enjoyments or by a man full of egotism, has beenspoken of as Rājasika

column3:
anubandhaṃ(ṅ) kṣayaṃ(m) hiṃsām, anavekṣya ca pauruṣam।
mohādārabhyate    karma,    yattattāmasamucyate॥18.25॥
column4: That action which is undertaken through sheer ignorance, without regard to consequences or loss to oneself, injury to others and one's own resourcefulness, is declared as Tāmasika.
column5: That action which is undertaken through sheer ignorance, without regard to consequences or loss to oneself, injury to others and one's own resourcefulness, is declared as Tāmasika.

column3:
muktasaṅgo’nahaṃvādī,       dhṛtyutsāhasamanvitaḥ।
siddhyasiddhyornirvikāraḥ(kh), kartā sāttvika ucyate॥18.26॥
column4: Free from attachment, unegoistic, endowed with firmness and zeal and unswayed by success and failure-such a doer is said to be Sāttvika.
column5: Free from attachment, unegoistic, endowed with firmness and zeal and unswayed by success and failure-such a doer is said to be Sāttvika.

column3:
rāgī karmaphalaprepsuḥ(r), lubdho hiṃsātmako’śuciḥ।
harṣaśokānvitaḥ(kh) kartā, rājasaḥ(ph) parikīrtitaḥ॥18.27॥
column4: The doer who is full of attachment, seeks the fruit of actions and is greedy, and who is oppressive by nature and of impure conduct, and who feels joy and sorrow, has been called Rājasika.
column5: The doer who is full of attachment, seeks the fruit of actions and is greedy, and who is oppressive by nature and of impure conduct, and who feels joy and sorrow, has been called Rājasika.

column3:
ayuktaḥ(ph) prākṛtaḥ(s) stabdhaḥ(ś), śaṭho naiṣkṛtiko’lasaḥ।
viṣādī  dīrghasūtrī  ca,  kartā  tāmasa  ucyate॥18.28॥
column4: Lacking piety and self-control, uncultured, arrogant, deceitful, inclined to rob others of their livelihood, slothful, despondent and procrastinating-such a doer is called Tāmasika.
column5: Lacking piety and self-control, uncultured, arrogant, deceitful, inclined to rob others of their livelihood, slothful, despondent and procrastinating-such a doer is called Tāmasika.

column3:
buddherbhedaṃ(n) dhṛteścaiva, guṇatastrividhaṃ(m) śṛṇu।                                                                                                                                                   
procyamānamaśeṣeṇa,     pṛthaktvena    dhanañjaya॥18.29॥
column4: Now hear, Arjuna, the threefold divison, based on the predominance of each Guņa, of understanding (Buddhi) and firmness (Dhṛti), which I shall explain in detail, one by one.
column5: Now hear, Arjuna, the threefold divison, based on the predominance of each Guņa, of understanding (Buddhi) and firmness (Dhṛti), which I shall explain in detail, one by one

column3:
pravṛttiṃ(ñ) ca nivṛttiṃ(ñ) ca, kāryākārye bhayābhaye।
bandhaṃ(m) mokṣaṃ(ñ) ca yā vetti, buddhiḥ(s) sā pārtha sāttvikī॥18.30॥
column4: The intellect which correctly determines the paths of activity and renunciation, what ought to be done and what should not be done, what is fear and what is fearlessness, and what is bondage and what is liberation, that intellect is Sāttvika.
column5: The intellect which correctly determines the paths of activity and renunciation, what ought to be done and what should not be done, what is fear and what is fearlessness, and what is bondage and what is liberation, that intellect is Sāttvika.

column3:
yayā dharmamadharmaṃ(ñ) ca, kāryaṃ(ñ) cākāryameva ca।
ayathāvatprajānāti,   buddhiḥ(s)   sā   pārtha   rājasī॥18.31॥
column4: The intellect by which man does not truly perceive what is Dharma and what is Adharma, what ought to be done and what should not be done-that intellect is Rājasika.
column5: The intellect by which man does not truly perceive what is Dharma and what is Adharma, what ought to be done and what should not be done-that intellect is Rājasika.

column3:
adharmaṃ(n) dharmamiti yā, manyate tamasāvṛtā।
sarvārthānviparītāṃśca, buddhiḥ(s) sā pārtha tāmasī॥18.32॥
