column4: The intellect wrapped in ignorance, which imagines even Adharma to be Dharma, and sees all other things upside-down-that intellect is Tāmasika, Arjuna.
column5: The intellect wrapped in ignorance, which imagines even Adharma to be Dharma, and sees all other things upside-down-that intellect is Tāmasika, Arjuna.

column3:
dhṛtyā  yayā  dhārayate,  manaḥprāṇendriyakriyāḥ।
yogenāvyabhicāriṇyā,   dhṛtiḥ(s)   sā   pārtha   sāttvikī॥18.33॥
column4: The unwavering perseverance by which man controls through the Yoga of meditation the functions of the mind, the vital airs and the senses- that firmness, Arjuna, is Sāttvika.
column5: The unwavering perseverance by which man controls through the Yoga of meditation the functions of the mind, the vital airs and the senses- that firmness, Arjuna, is Sāttvika..

column3:
yayā tu dharmakāmārthān, dhṛtyā dhārayate’rjuna।
prasaṅgena phalākāṅkṣī, dhṛtiḥ(s) sā pārtha rājasī॥18.34॥
column4: The perseverance (Dhṛti), however, by which the man seeking reward for his actions clutches with extreme fondness virtues, earthly possessions and worldly enjoyments-that persevrance (Dhṛti) is said to be Rājasika, Arjuna.
column5: The perseverance (Dhṛti), however, by which the man seeking reward for his actions clutches with extreme fondness virtues, earthly possessions and worldly enjoyments-that persevrance (Dhṛti) is said to be Rājasika, Arjuna.

column3:
yayā svapnaṃ(m) bhayaṃ(m) śokaṃ(v̐), viṣādaṃ(m) madameva ca।
na vimuñcati durmedhā, dhṛtiḥ(s) sā pārtha tāmasī॥18.35॥
column4: The perseverance (Dhṛti) by which an evilminded person does not give up sleep, fear, anxiety, sorrow and vanity as well, that perseverance is Tāmasika.
column5: The perseverance (Dhṛti) by which an evilminded person does not give up sleep, fear, anxiety, sorrow and vanity as well, that perseverance is Tāmasika.

column3:
sukhaṃ(n) tvidānīṃ(n) trividhaṃ(m), śṛṇu me bharatarṣabha।
abhyāsādramate yatra, duḥkhāntaṃ(ñ) ca nigacchati॥18.36॥
column4: Now hear from Me the threefold joy too. That in which the striver finds enjoyment through practice of adoration, meditation and service to God etc., and whereby he reaches the end of sorrow-such a joy,
column5: Now hear from Me the threefold joy too. That in which the striver finds enjoyment through practice of adoration, meditation and service to God etc., and whereby he reaches the end of sorrow-such a joy,

column3:
yattadagre      viṣamiva,     pariṇāme’mṛtopamam।
tatsukhaṃ(m) sāttvikaṃ(m) proktam, ātmabuddhiprasādajam॥18.37॥
column4: though appearing as poison in the beginning, tastes like nectar in the end; hence that joy, born as it is of the placidity of mind brought about by meditation on God, has been declared as Sāttvika.
column5: though appearing as poison in the beginning, tastes like nectar in the end; hence that joy, born as it is of the placidity of mind brought about by meditation on God, has been declared as Sāttvika.

column3:
viṣayendriyasaṃyogād,       yattadagre’mṛtopamam।
pariṇāme viṣamiva, tatsukhaṃ(m) rājasaṃ(m) smṛtam॥18.38॥
column4: The delight which ensues from the contact of the senses with their objects is eventually poisonlike, though appearing at first as nectar; hence it has been spoken of as Rājasika.
column5: The delight which ensues from the contact of the senses with their objects is eventually poisonlike, though appearing at first as nectar; hence it has been spoken of as Rājasika.

column3:
yadagre cānubandhe ca, sukhaṃ(m) mohanamātmanaḥ।
nidrālasyapramādotthaṃ(n), tattāmasamudāhṛtam॥18.39॥
column4: That which stupefies the Self during its enjoyment as well as in the end-derived from sleep, indolence and obstinate error, such delight has been called Tāmasika.
column5: That which stupefies the Self during its enjoyment as well as in the end-derived from sleep, indolence and obstinate error, such delight has been called Tāmasika.

column3:
na tadasti pṛthivyāṃ(v̐) vā, divi deveṣu vā punaḥ।
sattvaṃ(m) prakṛtijairmuktaṃ(y̐), yadebhiḥ(s) syāttribhirguṇaiḥ॥18.40॥
column4: There is no being on earth, or in the middle region or even among the gods or anywhere else, who is free from these three Guņas, born of Prakṛti.
column5: There is no being on earth, or in the middle region or even among the gods or anywhere else, who is free from these three Guņas, born of Prakṛti.

column3:
brāhmaṇakṣatriyaviśāṃ(m), śūdrāṇāṃ(ñ) ca parantapa।
karmāṇi  pravibhaktāni,  svabhāvaprabhavairguṇaiḥ॥18.41॥
column4: The duties of the Brāhmaņas, the Kṣatriyas and the Vaiśyas, as well as of the Śūdras have been assigned according to their innate modes of Prakṛti (Guņas), Arjuna.
column5: The duties of the Brāhmaņas, the Kṣatriyas and the Vaiśyas, as well as of the Śūdras have been assigned according to their innate modes of Prakṛti (Guņas), Arjuna.

column3:
śamo damastapaḥ(ś) śaucaṃ(ṅ), kṣāntirārjavameva ca।
jñānaṃ(v̐) vijñānamāstikyaṃ(m), brahmakarma svabhāvajam॥18.42॥
column4: Subjugation of the mind and senses, enduring hardships for the discharge of one's sacred obligations, external and internal purity, forgiving the faults of others, straightness of mind, senses and behaviour, belief in the Vedas and other scriptures, God and life after death etc., study and teaching of the Vedas and other scriptures and realization of the truth relating to God-all these constitute the natural duties of a Brāhmaņa.
column5: Subjugation of the mind and senses, enduring hardships for the discharge of one's sacred obligations, external and internal purity, forgiving the faults of others, straightness of mind, senses and behaviour, belief in the Vedas and other scriptures, God and life after death etc., study and teaching of the Vedas and other scriptures and realization of the truth relating to God-all these constitute the natural duties of a Brāhmaņa.

column3:
śauryaṃ(n) tejo dhṛtirdākṣyaṃ(y̐), yuddhe cāpyapalāyanam।
dānamīśvarabhāvaśca, kṣātraṃ(ṅ) karma svabhāvajam॥18.43॥
column4: Heroism, majesty, firmness, diligence and dauntlessness in battle, bestowing gifts, and lordliness-all these constitute the natural duty of a Kṣatriya.
column5: Heroism, majesty, firmness, diligence and dauntlessness in battle, bestowing gifts, and lordliness-all these constitute the natural duty of a Kṣatriya.

column3:
kṛṣigaurakṣyavāṇijyaṃ(v̐),   vaiśyakarma  svabhāvajam।
paricaryātmakaṃ(ṅ)  karma,  śūdrasyāpi  svabhāvajam॥18.44॥
column4: Agriculture, rearing of cows and honest exchange of merchandise-these constitute the natural duty of a Vaiśya (a member of the trading class); and service of the other classes is the natural duty even of a Śūdra (a member of the labouring class).
column5: Agriculture, rearing of cows and honest exchange of merchandise-these constitute the natural duty of a Vaiśya (a member of the trading class); and service of the other classes is the natural duty even of a Śūdra (a member of the labouring class).

column3:
sve sve karmaṇyabhirataḥ(s), saṃsiddhiṃ(l̐) labhate naraḥ।
svakarmanirataḥ(s) siddhiṃ(y̐), yathā vindati tacchṛṇu॥18.45॥
column4: Keenly devoted to his own natural duty, man attains the highest perfection in the form of Godrealization. Hear the mode of performance whereby the man engaged in his inborn duty reaches that highest consummation.
column5: Keenly devoted to his own natural duty, man attains the highest perfection in the form of Godrealization. Hear the mode of performance whereby the man engaged in his inborn duty reaches that highest consummation.

column3:
yataḥ(ph) pravṛttirbhūtānāṃ(y̐), yena sarvamidaṃ(n) tatam।
svakarmaṇā tamabhyarcya, siddhiṃ(v̐) vindati mānavaḥ॥18.46॥
column4: From whom all beings come into being and by whom the whole universe is pervaded, by worshipping Him through the performance of his own natural duties, man attains the highest perfection.
column5: From whom all beings come into being and by whom the whole universe is pervaded, by worshipping Him through the performance of his own natural duties, man attains the highest perfection..

column3:
śreyānsvadharmo viguṇaḥ(ph), paradharmātsvanuṣṭhitāt।
svabhāvaniyataṃ(ṅ) karma, kurvannāpnoti kilbiṣam॥18.47॥
column4: Better is one's own duty, though devoid of merit, than the duty of another well-performed; for, performing the duty ordained by his own nature, man does not incur sin.
column5: Better is one's own duty, though devoid of merit, than the duty of another well-performed; for, performing the duty ordained by his own nature, man does not incur sin.

column3:
sahajaṃ(ṅ) karma kaunteya, sadoṣamapi na tyajet।
sarvārambhā    hi     doṣeṇa,     dhūmenāgnirivāvṛtāḥ ॥18.48॥
column4: Therefore, Arjuna, one should not relinquish one's innate duty, even though it has a measure of evil; for all undertakings are beset by some evil, as is the fire covered by smoke.
column5: Therefore, Arjuna, one should not relinquish one's innate duty, even though it has a measure of evil; for all undertakings are beset by some evil, as is the fire covered by smoke.

column3:
asaktabuddhiḥ(s)    sarvatra,     jitātmā    vigataspṛhaḥ।
naiṣkarmyasiddhiṃ(m) paramāṃ(m), sannyāsenādhigacchati॥18.49॥
column4: He whose intellect is unattached everywhere, whose thirst for enjoyment has altogether disappeared and who has subdued his mind, reaches through Sāṅkhyayoga (the path of Knowledge) the consummation of actionlessness.
column5: He whose intellect is unattached everywhere, whose thirst for enjoyment has altogether disappeared and who has subdued his mind, reaches through Sāṅkhyayoga (the path of Knowledge) the consummation of actionlessness.

column3:
siddhiṃ(m) prāpto yathā brahma, tathāpnoti nibodha me।
samāsenaiva   kaunteya,   niṣṭhā   jñānasya   yā   parā॥18.50॥
column4: Arjuna, know from Me only briefly the process through which man having attained actionlessness, which is the highest consummation of Jñānayoga (the path of Knowledge), reaches Brahma.
column5: Arjuna, know from Me only briefly the process through which man having attained actionlessness, which is the highest consummation of Jñānayoga (the path of Knowledge), reaches Brahma.

column3:
buddhyā viśuddhayā yukto, dhṛtyātmānaṃ(n) niyamya ca।
śabdādīnviṣayāṃstyaktvā,   rāgadveṣau   vyudasya   ca॥18.51॥
column4: Endowed with a pure intellect and partaking of a light, Sāttvika and regulated diet, living in a lonely and undefiled place, having rejected sound and other objects of sense,
column5: Endowed with a pure intellect and partaking of a light, Sāttvika and regulated diet, living in a lonely and undefiled place, having rejected sound and other objects of sense,

column3:
viviktasevī        laghvāśī,      yatavākkāyamānasaḥ।
dhyānayogaparo nityaṃ(v̐), vairāgyaṃ(m) samupāśritaḥ॥18.52॥
column4: having controlled the mind, speech and body by restraining the mind and senses through firmness of a Sāttvika type, taking a resolute stand on dispassion, after having completely got rid of attraction and aversion and remaining ever devoted to the Yoga of meditation,
column5: having controlled the mind, speech and body by restraining the mind and senses through firmness of a Sāttvika type, taking a resolute stand on dispassion, after having completely got rid of attraction and aversion and remaining ever devoted to the Yoga of meditation,

column3:
ahaṅkāraṃ(m) balaṃ(n) darpaṃ(ṅ), kāmaṃ(ṅ) krodhaṃ(m) parigraham।
vimucya nirmamaḥ(ś)  śānto,  brahmabhūyāya  kalpate॥18.53॥
column4: having given up egotism, violence, arrogance, lust, anger and luxuries, devoid of the feeling of meum and tranquil of heart-such a man becomes qualified for oneness with Brahma, who is Truth, Consciousness and Bliss.
column5: having given up egotism, violence, arrogance, lust, anger and luxuries, devoid of the feeling of meum and tranquil of heart-such a man becomes qualified for oneness with Brahma, who is Truth, Consciousness and Bliss.

column3:
brahmabhūtaḥ(ph) prasannātmā, na śocati na kāṅkṣati।
samaḥ(s) sarveṣu bhūteṣu,  madbhaktiṃ(l̐)   labhate  parām ॥18.54॥
column4: Established in identity with Brahma (who is Truth, Consciousness and Bliss solidified), and cheerful in mind, the Sāṅkhyayogī no longer grieves nor craves for anything. The same to all beings, such a Yogī attains supreme devotion to Me.
column5: Established in identity with Brahma (who is Truth, Consciousness and Bliss solidified), and cheerful in mind, the Sāṅkhyayogī no longer grieves nor craves for anything. The same to all beings, such a Yogī attains supreme devotion to Me.

column3:
bhaktyā  māmabhijānāti,  yāvānyaścāsmi  tattvataḥ।
tato  māṃ(n)  tattvato  jñātvā,  viśate tadanantaram॥18.55॥
column4: Through that supreme devotion he comes to know Me in reality, what and who I am; and thereby knowing Me truly, he forthwith merges into My being.
column5: Through that supreme devotion he comes to know Me in reality, what and who I am; and thereby knowing Me truly, he forthwith merges into My being.

column3:
sarvakarmāṇyapi  sadā, kurvāṇo  madvyapāśrayaḥ।
matprasādādavāpnoti,  śāśvataṃ(m)  padamavyayam॥18.56॥
column4: The Karmayogī, however, who depends on Me, attains by My grace the eternal, imperishable state, even though performing all actions.
column5: The Karmayogī, however, who depends on Me, attains by My grace the eternal, imperishable state, even though performing all actions.

column3:
cetasā   sarvakarmāṇi,   mayi   sannyasya   matparaḥ।
buddhiyogamupāśritya, maccittaḥ(s) satataṃ(m) bhava॥18.57॥
column4: Mentally dedicating all your actions to Me, and taking recourse to Yoga in the form of evenmindedness, be solely devoted to Me and constantly fix your mind on Me.
column5: Mentally dedicating all your actions to Me, and taking recourse to Yoga in the form of evenmindedness, be solely devoted to Me and constantly fix your mind on Me.

column3:
maccittaḥ(s)  sarvadurgāṇi,  matprasādāttariṣyasi।
atha  cettvamahaṅkārān,  na  śroṣyasi  vinaṅkṣyasi॥18.58॥
column4: With your mind thus devoted to Me, you shall, by My grace overcome all difficulties. But, if from self-conceit you do not care to listen to Me, you will be lost.
column5: With your mind thus devoted to Me, you shall, by My grace overcome all difficulties. But, if from self-conceit you do not care to listen to Me, you will be lost.

column3:
yadahaṅkāramāśritya,    na     yotsya    iti    manyase।
mithyaiṣa  vyavasāyaste,  prakṛtistvāṃ(n)  niyokṣyati॥18.59॥
column4: If, taking your stand on egotism, you think, "I will not fight," vain is this resolve of yours; nature will drive you to the act.
column5: If, taking your stand on egotism, you think, "I will not fight," vain is this resolve of yours; nature will drive you to the act.

column3:
svabhāvajena  kaunteya,  nibaddhaḥ(s)  svena  karmaṇā।
kartuṃ(n) necchasi yanmohāt, kariṣyasyavaśo'pi tat॥18.60॥
column4: That action, too, which you are not willing to undertake through ignorance you will perforce perform, bound by your own duty born of your nature.
column5: That action, too, which you are not willing to undertake through ignorance you will perforce perform, bound by your own duty born of your nature.

column3:
īśvaraḥ(s)   sarvabhūtānāṃ(m),   hṛddeśe’rjuna   tiṣṭhati।
bhrāmayansarvabhūtāni,     yantrārūḍhāni    māyayā॥18.61॥
column4: Arjuna, God abides in the heart of all creatures, causing them to revolve according to their Karma by His illusive power (Māyā) as though mounted on a machine.
column5: Arjuna, God abides in the heart of all creatures, causing them to revolve according to their Karma by His illusive power (Māyā) as though mounted on a machine.

column3:
tameva śaraṇaṃ(ṅ) gaccha, sarvabhāvena bhārata।
tatprasādātparāṃ(m) śāntiṃ(m), sthānaṃ(m) prāpsyasi śāśvatam॥18.62॥
column4: Take refuge in Him alone with all your being, Arjuna. By His mere grace you will attain supreme peace and the eternal abode.
column5: Take refuge in Him alone with all your being, Arjuna. By His mere grace you will attain supreme peace and the eternal abode

column3:
iti te jñānamākhyātaṃ(ṅ), guhyādguhyataraṃ(m) mayā।
vimṛśyaitadaśeṣeṇa,     yathecchasi    tathā   kuru॥18.63॥
column4: Thus, has this wisdom, the most profound secret of all secret knowledge, been imparted to you by Me; deeply pondering over it, now do as you like.
column5: Thus, has this wisdom, the most profound secret of all secret knowledge, been imparted to you by Me; deeply pondering over it, now do as you like

column3:
sarvaguhyatamaṃ(m) bhūyaḥ(ś), śṛṇu me paramaṃ(v̐) vacaḥ।
iṣṭo’si  me dṛḍhamiti,   tato  vakṣyāmi  te  hitam॥18.64॥
column4: Hear, again, My supremely profound words, the most esoteric of all truths; as you are extremely dear to Me, therefore, I shall give you this salutary advice for your own good.
column5: Hear, again, My supremely profound words, the most esoteric of all truths; as you are extremely dear to Me, therefore, I shall give you this salutary advice for your own good.

column3:
manmanā bhava madbhakto,  madyājī  māṃ(n)  namaskuru।
māmevaiṣyasi  satyaṃ(n)  te, pratijāne priyo’si me॥18.65॥
column4: Give your mind to Me, be devoted to Me, worship Me and bow to Me. Doing so, you will come to Me alone, I truly promise you; for, you are exceptionally dear to Me.
column5: Give your mind to Me, be devoted to Me, worship Me and bow to Me. Doing so, you will come to Me alone, I truly promise you; for, you are exceptionally dear to Me.

column3:
sarvadharmānparityajya, māmekaṃ(m) śaraṇaṃ(v̐) vraja।
ahaṃ(n) tvā  sarvapāpebhyo,  mokṣayiṣyāmi  mā  śucaḥ॥18.66॥
column4: Resigning all your duties to Me, the all-powerful and all supporting Lord, take refuge in Me alone; I shall absolve you of all sins, worry not.
column5: Resigning all your duties to Me, the all-powerful and all supporting Lord, take refuge in Me alone; I shall absolve you of all sins, worry not.

column3:
idaṃ(n)  te   nātapaskāya,   nābhaktāya   kadācana।
na cāśuśrūṣave vācyaṃ(n), na ca māṃ(y̐) yo’bhyasūyati॥18.67॥
column4: This secret gospel of the Gītā should never be imparted to a man who lacks in austerity, nor to him who is wanting in devotion, nor even to him who is not willing to hear; and in no case to him who finds fault with Me.
column5: This secret gospel of the Gītā should never be imparted to a man who lacks in austerity, nor to him who is wanting in devotion, nor even to him who is not willing to hear; and in no case to him who finds fault with Me.

column3:
ya imaṃ(m) paramaṃ(ṅ) guhyaṃ(m), madbhakteṣvabhidhāsyati।
bhaktiṃ(m) mayi parāṃ(ṅ) kṛtvā, māmevaiṣyatyasaṃśayaḥ॥18.68॥
column4: He who, offering the highest love to Me, preaches the most profound gospel of the Gītā among My devotees, shall come to Me alone; there is no doubt about it.
column5: He who, offering the highest love to Me, preaches the most profound gospel of the Gītā among My devotees, shall come to Me alone; there is no doubt about it.

column3:
na ca tasmānmanuṣyeṣu,  kaścinme  priyakṛttamaḥ।
bhavitā na ca me  tasmād,  anyaḥ(ph)  priyataro  bhuvi॥18.69॥
column4: Among men there is none who does Me a more loving service than he; nor shall anyone be dearer to Me on the entire globe than he.
column5: Among men there is none who does Me a more loving service than he; nor shall anyone be dearer to Me on the entire globe than he

column3:
adhyeṣyate ca ya imaṃ(n), dharmyaṃ(m) saṃvādamāvayoḥ।
jñānayajñena  tenāham,  iṣṭaḥ(s)  syāmiti me  matiḥ॥18.70॥
column4: Whosoever studies this sacred dialogue of ours in the form of the Gītā, by him too shall I be worshipped with Yajña of Knowledge; such is My conviction.
column5: Whosoever studies this sacred dialogue of ours in the form of the Gītā, by him too shall I be worshipped with Yajña of Knowledge; such is My conviction.

column3:
śraddhāvānanasūyaśca,         śṛṇuyādapi       yo       naraḥ।
so’pi muktaḥ(ś) śubhāl̐lokān, prāpnuyātpuṇyakarmaṇām॥18.71॥
column4: The man who listens to the holy Gītā with reverence, being free from malice, he too, liberated from sin, shall reach the propitious worlds of the virtuous.
column5: The man who listens to the holy Gītā with reverence, being free from malice, he too, liberated from sin, shall reach the propitious worlds of the virtuous.

column3:
kaccidetacchrutaṃ(m) pārtha,  tvayaikāgreṇa  cetasā।
kaccidajñānasammohaḥ(ph), pranaṣṭaste dhanañjaya॥18.72॥
column4: Have you, O Arjuna, heard this gospel of the Gītā attentively? And has your delusion born of ignorance been destroyed, O Dhanañjaya, conqueror of riches?
column5: Have you, O Arjuna, heard this gospel of the Gītā attentively? And has your delusion born of ignorance been destroyed, O Dhanañjaya, conqueror of riches?

column3:
arjuna uvāca
naṣṭo mohaḥ(s) smṛtirlabdhā, tvatprasādānmayācyuta।
sthito’smi gatasandehaḥ(kh), kariṣye vacanaṃ(n) tava॥18.73॥
column4:
Arjuna said: 
Kṛṣņa, by Your grace my delusion has been destroyed and I have gained wisdom. I am free of all doubt. I will do your bidding.
column5: Arjuna said: Kṛṣņa, by Your grace my delusion has been destroyed and I have gained wisdom. I am free of all doubt. I will do your bidding..

column3:
sañjaya uvāca
ityahaṃ(v̐) vāsudevasya, pārthasya ca mahātmanaḥ।
saṃvādamimamaśrauṣam, adbhutaṃ(m) romaharṣaṇam॥18.74॥
column4:
Sañjaya said: 
Thus I heard the mysterious and thrilling conversation between Śrī Kṛṣņa and the high-souled Arjuna, son of Kuntī.
column5: Sañjaya said: Thus I heard the mysterious and thrilling conversation between Śrī Kṛṣņa and the high-souled Arjuna, son of Kuntī

column3:
vyāsaprasādācchrutavān,  etadguhyamahaṃ(m) param।
yogaṃ(y̐) yogeśvarātkṛṣṇāt, sākṣātkathayataḥ(s) svayam॥18.75॥
column4: Having been blessed with the divine vision by the grace of Śrī Vyāsa, I heard in person this supremely esoteric gospel from the Lord of Yoga, Śrī Kṛṣņa Himself, imparting it to Arjuna.
column5: Having been blessed with the divine vision by the grace of Śrī Vyāsa, I heard in person this supremely esoteric gospel from the Lord of Yoga, Śrī Kṛṣņa Himself, imparting it to Arjuna.

column3:
rājansaṃsmṛtya saṃsmṛtya, saṃvādamimamadbhutam।
keśavārjunayoḥ(ph)  puṇyaṃ(m),  hṛṣyāmi  ca  muhurmuhuḥ॥18.76॥
column4: Remembering, over and over, that sacred and mystic conversation between Bhagavān Śrī Kṛṣņa and Arjuna, O King! I am thrilled again and yet again.
column5: Remembering, over and over, that sacred and mystic conversation between Bhagavān Śrī Kṛṣņa and Arjuna, O King! I am thrilled again and yet again.

column3:
tacca saṃsmṛtya-saṃsmṛtya, rūpamatyadbhutaṃ(m) hareḥ।
vismayo me mahān rājan, hṛṣyāmi ca punaḥ(ph) punaḥ॥18.77॥
column4: Remembering also, again and again, that most wonderful form of Śrī Kṛṣņa, great is my wonder and I am thrilled over and over again.
column5:
Sañjaya said: 
Arjuna, Remembering also, again and again, that most wonderful form of Śrī Kṛṣņa, great is my wonder and I am thrilled over and over again.

column3:
yatra yogeśvaraḥ(kh) kṛṣṇo,  yatra  pārtho  dhanurdharaḥ।
tatra  śrīrvijayo  bhūtiḥ(r),  dhruvā  nītirmatirmama॥18.78॥
column4: Wherever there is Bhagavān Śrī Kṛṣņa, the Lord of Yoga, and wherever there is Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāņḍīva bow, goodness, victory, glory and unfailing righteousness will surely be there : such is My conviction.
column5: Wherever there is Bhagavān Śrī Kṛṣņa, the Lord of Yoga, and wherever there is Arjuna, the wielder of the Gāņḍīva bow, goodness, victory, glory and unfailing righteousness will surely be there : such is My conviction.

column3:
Om tatsaditi śrīmadbhagavadgītāsu upaniṣatsu brahmavidyāyāṃ(y̐)
 yogaśāstre śrīkṛṣṇārjunasaṃvāde mokṣasannyāsayogo nāma
 aṣṭādaśo'dhyāyaḥ ||
|| OM ŚRĪKṚṢṆĀRPAṆAMASTU ||
column4: Thus, in the Upaniṣad sung by the Lord, the Science of Brahma, the scripture of Yoga, the dialogue between Śrī Kṛṣņa and Arjuna, ends the eighteenth chapter entitled "The Yoga of Liberation through the Path of Knowledge and Self-Surrender."
column5: Thus, in the Upaniṣad sung by the Lord, the Science of Brahma, the scripture of Yoga, the dialogue between Śrī Kṛṣņa and Arjuna, ends the eighteenth chapter entitled "The Yoga of Liberation through the Path of Knowledge and Self-Surrender."
